02-23.text: Five-time Argentine Formula One champion Juan Manuel Fangio is kidnapped by rebels involved in the Cuban Revolution, on the eve of the Cuban Grand Prix. He was released the following day after the race.

02-23.title: Syria
02-23.subtitle: In Syria, Ba'ath Party member Salah Jadid leads an intra-party military coup that replaces the previous government of Ge...
02-23.text: In Syria, Ba'ath Party member Salah Jadid leads an intra-party military coup that replaces the previous government of General Amin al-Hafiz, also a Baathist.

02-23.title: Symbionese Liberation Army
02-23.subtitle: The Symbionese Liberation Army demands $4 million more to release kidnap victim Patty Hearst.
02-23.text: The Symbionese Liberation Army demands $4 million more to release kidnap victim Patty Hearst.

02-28.title: Discoverer 1
02-28.subtitle: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.
02-28.text: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.

02-28.title: Prime minister
02-28.subtitle: Prime Minister Modi of India inaugurates the 2nd Space Port of India - Kulasekarapattinam Spaceport.
02-28.text: Prime Minister Modi of India inaugurates the 2nd Space Port of India - Kulasekarapattinam Spaceport.

02-28.title: Northrop T-38 Talon
02-28.subtitle: A NASA T-38 Talon crashes into the McDonnell Aircraft factory while attempting a poor-visibility landing at Lambert Field, St.
02-28.text: A NASA T-38 Talon crashes into the McDonnell Aircraft factory while attempting a poor-visibility landing at Lambert Field, St. Louis, killing astronauts Elliot See and Charles Bassett.

02-28.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
02-28.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-36.
02-28.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

02-28.title: 2001 Nisqually earthquake
02-28.subtitle: The 2001 Nisqually earthquake, having a moment magnitude of 6.
02-28.text: The 2001 Nisqually earthquake, having a moment magnitude of 6.8, with epicenter in southern Puget Sound, damages the Seattle metropolitan area.

02-26.title: Apollo program
02-26.subtitle: Apollo program: Launch of AS-201, the first flight of the Saturn IB rocket.
02-26.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

02-26.title: First Nagorno-Karabakh War
02-26.subtitle: First Nagorno-Karabakh War: Khojaly Massacre: Armenian armed forces open fire on Azeri civilians at a military post outs...
02-26.text: The First Nagorno-Karabakh War was an ethnic and territorial conflict that took place from February 1988 to May 1994 in the enclave of Nagorno-Karabakh in southwestern Azerbaijan between the majority ethnic Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh backed by Armenia, and the Republic of Azerbaijan with support from Turkey. As the war progressed, Armenia and Azerbaijan, both former Soviet republics, entangled themselves in protracted, undeclared mountain warfare in the mountainous heights of Karabakh as Azerbaijan attempted to curb the Karabakh Movement.

02-26.title: Trayvon Martin
02-26.subtitle: Seventeen-year-old African-American student Trayvon Martin is shot to death by neighborhood watch coordinator George Zim...
02-26.text: Seventeen-year-old African-American student Trayvon Martin is shot to death by neighborhood watch coordinator George Zimmerman in an altercation in Sanford, Florida.

02-26.title: Barings Bank
02-26.subtitle: The UK's oldest investment banking institute, Barings Bank, collapses after a rogue securities broker Nick Leeson loses $1.
02-26.text: The UK's oldest investment banking institute, Barings Bank, collapses after a rogue securities broker Nick Leeson loses $1.4 billion by speculating on the Singapore International Monetary Exchange using futures contracts.

02-26.title: Secretary-General of the United Nations
02-26.subtitle: U.
02-26.text: U.N. Secretary-General U Thant signs United Nations proclamation of the vernal equinox as Earth Day.

02-29.title: Flour Massacre
02-29.subtitle: The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the G...
02-29.text: The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the Gaza war, killing over 100 and wounding 750.

02-29.title: United States
02-29.subtitle: The United States and the Taliban sign the Doha Agreement for bringing peace to Afghanistan.
02-29.text: The United States and the Taliban sign the Doha Agreement for bringing peace to Afghanistan.

02-29.title: Berkeley, California
02-29.subtitle: In a ceremony held in Berkeley, California, physicist Ernest Lawrence receives the 1939 Nobel Prize in Physics from Swed...
02-29.text: In a ceremony held in Berkeley, California, physicist Ernest Lawrence receives the 1939 Nobel Prize in Physics from Sweden's consul general in San Francisco.

02-29.title: Jean-Bertrand Aristide
02-29.subtitle: Jean-Bertrand Aristide is removed as president of Haiti following a coup.
02-29.text: Jean-Bertrand Aristide is a Haitian former Salesian priest and politician who served as president of Haiti in 1991, from 1993 to 1994, from 1994 to 1996, and from 2001 to 2004. He was in exile after the 1991 military coup until 1994 and again after his overthrow in 2004 until 2011. Aristide was a member of the Lavalas Political Organization before he founded the party Fanmi Lavalas in 1996.

02-29.title: Angelina Jolie
02-29.subtitle: Angelina Jolie wears a White Marc Bouwer dress to the 76th Academy Awards, which has since been placed on numerous lists for best Oscars fashion.
02-29.text: Angelina Jolie wears a White Marc Bouwer dress to the 76th Academy Awards, which has since been placed on numerous lists for best Oscars fashion.

02-22.title: Official Irish Republican Army
02-22.subtitle: The Official Irish Republican Army detonates a car bomb at Aldershot barracks, killing seven and injuring nineteen others.
02-22.text: The Official Irish Republican Army detonates a car bomb at Aldershot barracks, killing seven and injuring nineteen others.

02-22.title: Ngo Dinh Diem
02-22.subtitle: Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam survives a communist shooting assassination attempt in Buôn Ma Thuột.
02-22.text: Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam survives a communist shooting assassination attempt in Buôn Ma Thuột.

02-22.title: Securitas depot robbery
02-22.subtitle: The Securitas depot robbery was the UK's largest heist.
02-22.text: The Securitas depot robbery was the UK's largest heist. Almost £53m (about $92.5 million or €78 million) was stolen from a Securitas depot in Tonbridge, Kent.

02-22.title: Cold War
02-22.subtitle: Cold War: Following President Richard Nixon's visit to the People's Republic of China, the two countries agree to establish liaison offices.
02-22.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
02-22.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

02-22.title: UTC+03:00
02-22.subtitle: At approximately 6:44 a.
02-22.text:
UTC+03:00 is an identifier for a time offset from UTC of +03:00. In areas using this time offset, the time is three hours ahead of the Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).
Following the ISO 8601 standard, a time with this offset would be written as, for example, 2019-02-08T23:36:06+03:00.
02-22.location: Identifier for a time offset from UTC of +3

02-27.title: 2010 Chile earthquake
02-27.subtitle: An earthquake measuring 8.
02-27.text: An earthquake measuring 8.8 on the moment magnitude scale strikes central parts of Chile leaving over 500 victims, and thousands injured. The quake triggers a tsunami which strikes Hawaii shortly after.

02-27.title: Dominican Republic
02-27.subtitle: The Dominican Republic receives its first democratically elected president, Juan Bosch, since the end of the dictatorship led by Rafael Trujillo.
02-27.text: The Dominican Republic receives its first democratically elected president, Juan Bosch, since the end of the dictatorship led by Rafael Trujillo.

02-27.title: Western Sahara
02-27.subtitle: The former Spanish territory of Western Sahara, under the auspices of the Polisario Front declares independence as the S...
02-27.text: The former Spanish territory of Western Sahara, under the auspices of the Polisario Front declares independence as the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic.

02-27.title: Twenty-second Amendment to the United States
02-27.subtitle: The Twenty-second Amendment to the United States Constitution, limiting Presidents to two terms, is ratified.
02-27.text: The Twenty-second Amendment to the United States Constitution limits the number of times a person can be elected to the office of President of the United States to two, and sets additional eligibility conditions for presidents who succeed to the unexpired terms of their predecessors. Congress approved the Twenty-second Amendment on March 21, 1947, and submitted it to the state legislatures for ratification.
02-27.location: 1951 amendment limiting presidents to two terms

02-27.title: Vietnam War
02-27.subtitle: Vietnam War: Two dissident Republic of Vietnam Air Force pilots bomb the Independence Palace in Saigon in a failed attem...
02-27.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
02-27.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-01.title: Venera 3
03-01.subtitle: Venera 3 Soviet space probe crashes on Venus becoming the first spacecraft to land on another planet's surface.
03-01.text: Venera 3 Soviet space probe crashes on Venus becoming the first spacecraft to land on another planet's surface.

03-01.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
03-01.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-109 to service the Hubble Space Telescope.
03-01.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

03-01.title: Cold War
03-01.subtitle: Cold War: Klaus Fuchs is convicted of spying for the Soviet Union by disclosing top secret atomic bomb data.
03-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-01.title: Nuclear weapons testing
03-01.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: The Castle Bravo, a 15-megaton hydrogen bomb, is detonated on Bikini Atoll in the Pacific Ocean...
03-01.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
03-01.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

03-01.title: Envisat
03-01.subtitle: The Envisat environmental satellite successfully launches aboard an Ariane 5 rocket to reach an orbit of 800 km (500 mi)...
03-01.text: The Envisat environmental satellite successfully launches aboard an Ariane 5 rocket to reach an orbit of 800 km (500 mi) above the Earth, which was the then-largest payload at 10.5 m long and with a diameter of 4.57 m.

03-02.title: Armenia
03-02.subtitle: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, San Marino, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan, all of which ...
03-02.text: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, San Marino, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan, all of which (except San Marino) were former Soviet republics, join the United Nations.

03-02.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
03-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches from the Kennedy Space Center on STS-67, carrying the ASTRO-2 spacelab observatory.
03-02.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

03-02.title: Galileo
03-02.subtitle: Data sent from the Galileo spacecraft indicates that Jupiter's moon Europa has a liquid ocean under a thick crust of ice.
03-02.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
03-02.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

03-02.title: Pioneer 10
03-02.subtitle: The Pioneer 10 space probe is launched from Cape Canaveral, Florida with a mission to explore the outer planets.
03-02.text: The Pioneer 10 space probe is launched from Cape Canaveral, Florida with a mission to explore the outer planets.

03-02.title: Czechoslovakia
03-02.subtitle: Czech Vladimír Remek becomes the first non-Soviet or non-American to go into space, when he is launched aboard Soyuz 28.
03-02.text: Czech Vladimír Remek becomes the first non-Soviet or non-American to go into space, when he is launched aboard Soyuz 28.

03-07.title: Massereene Barracks shooting
03-07.subtitle: Massereene Barracks shooting: The Real Irish Republican Army kills two British soldiers and injures two other soldiers a...
03-07.text: Massereene Barracks shooting: The Real Irish Republican Army kills two British soldiers and injures two other soldiers and two civilians at Massereene Barracks, the first British military deaths in Northern Ireland since the end of The Troubles.

03-07.title: Ottoman Empire
03-07.subtitle: A peace treaty is signed between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice, ending the Ottoman–Venetian War and leaving Cyprus in Ottoman hands.
03-07.text: A peace treaty is signed between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice, ending the Ottoman–Venetian War and leaving Cyprus in Ottoman hands.

03-07.title: Korean War
03-07.subtitle: Korean War: Operation Ripper: United Nations troops led by General Matthew Ridgway begin an assault against Chinese forces.
03-07.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

03-07.title: Sweden
03-07.subtitle: Sweden officially joins NATO, becoming its 32nd member.
03-07.text: Sweden officially joins NATO, becoming its 32nd member.

03-07.title: Bloody Sunday
03-07.subtitle: Bloody Sunday: A group of 600 civil rights marchers are brutally attacked by state and local police in Selma, Alabama.
03-07.text: Bloody Sunday: A group of 600 civil rights marchers are brutally attacked by state and local police in Selma, Alabama.

03-04.title: Space Shuttle program
03-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: The Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-62.
03-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

03-04.title: International Criminal Court
03-04.subtitle: The International Criminal Court (ICC) issues an arrest warrant for Sudanese President Omar Hassan al-Bashir for war cri...
03-04.text: The International Criminal Court (ICC) issues an arrest warrant for Sudanese President Omar Hassan al-Bashir for war crimes and crimes against humanity in Darfur. Al-Bashir is the first sitting head of state to be indicted by the ICC since its establishment in 2002.

03-04.title: World War II
03-04.subtitle: World War II: The Battle of Fardykambos, one of the first major battles between the Greek Resistance and the occupying Royal Italian Army, begins.
03-04.text: World War II: The Battle of Fardykambos, one of the first major battles between the Greek Resistance and the occupying Royal Italian Army, begins. It ends on 6 March with the surrender of an entire Italian battalion and the liberation of the town of Grevena.

03-04.title: Lennox Sebe
03-04.subtitle: Lennox Sebe, President for life of the South African Bantustan of Ciskei, is ousted from power in a bloodless military c...
03-04.text: Lennox Sebe, President for life of the South African Bantustan of Ciskei, is ousted from power in a bloodless military coup led by Brigadier Oupa Gqozo.

03-04.title: Robert Mugabe
03-04.subtitle: Nationalist leader Robert Mugabe wins a sweeping election victory to become Zimbabwe's first black prime minister.
03-04.text: Robert Gabriel Mugabe was a Zimbabwean revolutionary and politician who led Zimbabwe from 1980 until he was deposed in a coup in 2017. He served as the first Prime Minister of Zimbabwe from internationally recognised independence in 1980 to 1987, then as the second president of Zimbabwe from 1987 to 2017.

03-08.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
03-08.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-102, carrying the Expedition 2 crew to the International Space Station.
03-08.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-102, carrying the Expedition 2 crew to the International Space Station.

03-08.title: Cold War
03-08.subtitle: Cold War: While addressing a convention of Evangelicals, U.
03-08.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-08.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-08.title: Aurat March
03-08.subtitle: The first Aurat March (social/political demonstration) is held on International Women's Day in Karachi, Pakistan, since ...
03-08.text: The first Aurat March (social/political demonstration) is held on International Women's Day in Karachi, Pakistan, since then held annually across Pakistan, and the feminist slogan "Mera Jism Meri Marzi" (My body, my choice), in demand for women's right to bodily autonomy and against gender-based violence, came into vogue in Pakistan.

03-08.title: Voyager 1
03-08.subtitle: Images taken by Voyager 1 prove the existence of volcanoes on Io, a moon of Jupiter.
03-08.text: Images taken by Voyager 1 prove the existence of volcanoes on Io, a moon of Jupiter.

03-08.title: President of France
03-08.subtitle: President of France Vincent Auriol and ex-Vietnamese emperor Bảo Đại sign the Élysée Accords, giving Vietnam greater ind...
03-08.text: President of France Vincent Auriol and ex-Vietnamese emperor Bảo Đại sign the Élysée Accords, giving Vietnam greater independence from France and creating the State of Vietnam to oppose Viet Minh-led Democratic Republic of Vietnam.

03-10.title: Silicon Valley Bank
03-10.subtitle: Silicon Valley Bank collapses due to a run on its deposits, in the second largest bank failure in US history.
03-10.text: Silicon Valley Bank collapses due to a run on its deposits, in the second largest bank failure in US history. Its operations are taken over by the FDIC.

03-10.title: Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909
03-10.subtitle: By signing the Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909, Thailand relinquishes its sovereignty over the Malay states of Kedah, Kelan...
03-10.text: By signing the Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909, Thailand relinquishes its sovereignty over the Malay states of Kedah, Kelantan, Perlis and Terengganu, which become British protectorates.

03-10.title: 2022 Hungarian presidential election
03-10.subtitle: 2022 Hungarian presidential election: The National Assembly of Hungary elects former minister for Family Affairs, Katali...
03-10.text: 2022 Hungarian presidential election: The National Assembly of Hungary elects former minister for Family Affairs, Katalin Novák, as president of Hungary in a 137–51 vote, becoming the first female president in the country's history.

03-10.title: Impeachment of Park Geun-hye
03-10.subtitle: The impeachment of President Park Geun-hye of South Korea in response to a major political scandal is unanimously upheld...
03-10.text: The impeachment of President Park Geun-hye of South Korea in response to a major political scandal is unanimously upheld by the country's Constitutional Court, ending her presidency.

03-10.title: 2024 Portuguese legislative election
03-10.subtitle: 2024 Portuguese legislative election: Elections are held in Portugal for all 230 seats in the Assembly of the Republic.
03-10.text: 2024 Portuguese legislative election: Elections are held in Portugal for all 230 seats in the Assembly of the Republic. The Partido Socialista loses its absolute majority to the Partido Social Democrata, winning 77 and 79 seats respectively.

03-09.title: McCarthyism
03-09.subtitle: McCarthyism: CBS television broadcasts the See It Now episode, "A Report on Senator Joseph McCarthy", produced by Fred Friendly.
03-09.text: McCarthyism: CBS television broadcasts the See It Now episode, "A Report on Senator Joseph McCarthy", produced by Fred Friendly.

03-09.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
03-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery makes its final landing after 39 flights.
03-09.text: Space Shuttle Discovery makes its final landing after 39 flights.

03-09.title: Korabl-Sputnik 4
03-09.subtitle: Sputnik 9 successfully launches, carrying a dog and a human dummy, and demonstrating that the Soviet Union was ready to begin human spaceflight.
03-09.text: Sputnik 9 successfully launches, carrying a dog and a human dummy, and demonstrating that the Soviet Union was ready to begin human spaceflight.

03-09.title: Suharto
03-09.subtitle: President Soeharto inaugurated Jagorawi Toll Road, the first toll highway in Indonesia, connecting Jakarta, Bogor and Ciawi, West Java.
03-09.text: Suharto was an Indonesian military officer and politician who served as the second and longest-serving president of Indonesia from 1967 to 1998. Widely regarded as a military dictator by international observers, Suharto led Indonesia as an authoritarian regime from 1967 until his resignation in 1998 following nationwide unrest.

03-09.title: Belding Hibbard Scribner
03-09.subtitle: Dr.
03-09.text: Dr. Belding Hibbard Scribner implants for the first time a shunt he invented into a patient, which allows the patient to receive hemodialysis on a regular basis.
03-09.location: 20th-century American physician and pioneer of kidney dialysis

03-11.title: 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami
03-11.subtitle: An earthquake measuring 9.
03-11.text: An earthquake measuring 9.0 in magnitude strikes 130 km (81 mi) east of Sendai, Japan, triggering a tsunami killing thousands of people. This event also triggered the second largest nuclear accident in history, and one of only two events to be classified as a Level 7 on the International Nuclear Event Scale.

03-11.title: World Health Organization
03-11.subtitle: The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the COVID-19 virus epidemic a pandemic.
03-11.text: The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the COVID-19 virus epidemic a pandemic.

03-11.title: Tatmadaw
03-11.subtitle: The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.
03-11.text: The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.

03-11.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
03-11.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-123, carrying the first component of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.
03-11.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

03-11.title: World War II
03-11.subtitle: World War II: The Empire of Vietnam, a short-lived Japanese puppet state, is established.
03-11.text: World War II: The Empire of Vietnam, a short-lived Japanese puppet state, is established.

03-13.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
03-13.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-29 carrying the TDRS-4 satellite.
03-13.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-29 carrying the TDRS-4 satellite.

03-13.title: New JEWEL Movement
03-13.subtitle: The New Jewel Movement, headed by Maurice Bishop, ousts the Prime Minister of Grenada, Eric Gairy, in a coup d'état.
03-13.text: The New Jewel Movement, headed by Maurice Bishop, ousts the Prime Minister of Grenada, Eric Gairy, in a coup d'état.

03-13.title: Battle of Dien Bien Phu
03-13.subtitle: The Battle of Điện Biên Phủ begins with an artillery barrage by Viet Minh forces under Võ Nguyên Giáp; Viet Minh victory...
03-13.text: The Battle of Điện Biên Phủ begins with an artillery barrage by Viet Minh forces under Võ Nguyên Giáp; Viet Minh victory led to the end of the First Indochina War and French withdrawal from Vietnam.

03-13.title: Cuba
03-13.subtitle: Cuban student revolutionaries storm the presidential palace in Havana in a failed attempt on the life of President Fulgencio Batista.
03-13.text: Cuba, officially the Republic of Cuba, is an island country in the Caribbean. It comprises the eponymous main island as well as 4,195 islands, islets, and cays. Situated at the convergence of the Caribbean Sea, Gulf of Mexico, and Atlantic Ocean, Cuba is located east of the Yucatán Peninsula, south of both Florida and the Bahamas, west of Hispaniola, and north of Jamaica and the Cayman Islands. Havana is the largest city and capital.

03-13.title: Donald Trump
03-13.subtitle: President Donald Trump declares the COVID-19 pandemic to be a national emergency in the United States.
03-13.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
03-13.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

03-14.title: Norman Thagard
03-14.subtitle: Norman Thagard becomes the first American astronaut to ride to space on board a Russian launch vehicle.
03-14.text: Norman Thagard becomes the first American astronaut to ride to space on board a Russian launch vehicle.

03-14.title: United States Air Force
03-14.subtitle: A USAF B-52 bomber carrying nuclear weapons crashes near Yuba City, California.
03-14.text: A USAF B-52 bomber carrying nuclear weapons crashes near Yuba City, California.

03-14.title: Korean War
03-14.subtitle: Korean War: United Nations troops recapture Seoul for the second time.
03-14.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

03-14.title: 2006 Chadian coup attempt
03-14.subtitle: The 2006 Chadian coup d'état attempt ends in failure.
03-14.text: The 2006 Chadian coup d'état attempt ends in failure.

03-14.title: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007
03-14.subtitle: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007 crashes during final approach near Warsaw, Poland, killing 87 people, including a 14-man American boxing team.
03-14.text: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007 crashes during final approach near Warsaw, Poland, killing 87 people, including a 14-man American boxing team.

03-12.title: Warsaw Pact
03-12.subtitle: Former Warsaw Pact members the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland join NATO.
03-12.text: Former Warsaw Pact members the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland join NATO.

03-12.title: North Korea
03-12.subtitle: North Korea announces that it will withdraw from the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and refuses to a...
03-12.text: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia. It constitutes the northern half of the Korean Peninsula and borders China and Russia to the north at the Yalu (Amnok) and Tumen rivers, and South Korea to the south at the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ). The country's western border is formed by the Yellow Sea, while its eastern border is defined by the Sea of Japan.

03-12.title: Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant
03-12.subtitle: A reactor at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant explodes and releases radioactivity into the atmosphere a day aft...
03-12.text: A reactor at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant explodes and releases radioactivity into the atmosphere a day after the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami.

03-12.title: President of South Korea
03-12.subtitle: President of South Korea, Roh Moo-hyun, is impeached by its National Assembly, the first such impeachment in the nation's history.
03-12.text: President of South Korea, Roh Moo-hyun, is impeached by its National Assembly, the first such impeachment in the nation's history.

03-12.title: Battle of Java
03-12.subtitle: The Battle of Java ends with the surrender of the American-British-Dutch-Australian Command to the Empire of Japan in Ba...
03-12.text: The Battle of Java was a battle of the Pacific theatre of World War II. It occurred on the island of Java from 28 February – 12 March 1942. It involved forces from the Empire of Japan, which invaded on 28 February 1942, and Allied personnel. Allied commanders signed a formal surrender at Japanese headquarters at Bandung on 12 March.
03-12.location: Battle in the Pacific theatre of World War II

03-15.title: Ukrainian War of Independence
03-15.subtitle: Ukrainian War of Independence: The Kontrrazvedka is established as the counterintelligence division of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.
03-15.text: Ukrainian War of Independence: The Kontrrazvedka is established as the counterintelligence division of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.

03-15.title: Cold War
03-15.subtitle: Cold War: The Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany comes into effect, granting full sovereignty to the Federal Republic of Germany.
03-15.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-15.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-15.title: Sterling Airways Flight 901
03-15.subtitle: Fifteen people are killed when Sterling Airways Flight 901, a Sud Aviation Caravelle, catches fire following a landing g...
03-15.text: Fifteen people are killed when Sterling Airways Flight 901, a Sud Aviation Caravelle, catches fire following a landing gear collapse at Mehrabad International Airport in Tehran, Iran.

03-15.title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
03-15.subtitle: The assassination of Julius Caesar, the dictator of the Roman Republic, by a group of senators takes place on the Ides of March.
03-15.text: The assassination of Julius Caesar, the dictator of the Roman Republic, by a group of senators takes place on the Ides of March.

03-15.title: Treaty of Lublowa
03-15.subtitle: Treaty of Lublowa: After the Peace of Thorn, Grand Master Heinrich von Plauen asks Sigismund of Hungary for economocial aid.
03-15.text: Treaty of Lublowa: After the Peace of Thorn, Grand Master Heinrich von Plauen asks Sigismund of Hungary for economocial aid. Sigismund agrees to mediate reduction to the third installment, demarcation of the Samogitian border, and other matters - with a grand tournament. Hunts and lavish feasts were also organized. Sigismund invited, among others, polish king Wladyslaw Jagiello, Heinrich von Plauen and bosnian king Tvrtko II. There were people from 17 countries and languages - 40.

03-16.title: Gemini 8
03-16.subtitle: Launch of Gemini 8 with astronauts Neil Armstrong and David Scott.
03-16.text: Launch of Gemini 8 with astronauts Neil Armstrong and David Scott. It would perform the first docking of two spacecraft in orbit.

03-16.title: Amoco Cadiz
03-16.subtitle: Supertanker Amoco Cadiz splits in two after running aground on the Portsall Rocks, three miles off the coast of Brittany...
03-16.text: Supertanker Amoco Cadiz splits in two after running aground on the Portsall Rocks, three miles off the coast of Brittany, resulting in the largest oil spill in history at that time.

03-16.title: Vietnam War
03-16.subtitle: Vietnam War: My Lai massacre occurs; between 347 and 500 Vietnamese villagers are killed by American troops.
03-16.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
03-16.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-16.title: 2020 stock market crash
03-16.subtitle: The Dow Jones Industrial Average falls by 2,997.
03-16.text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average falls by 2,997.10, the single largest point drop in history and the second-largest percentage drop ever at 12.93%, an even greater crash than Black Monday (1929). This follows the U.S. Federal Reserve announcing that it will cut its target interest rate to 0–0.25%.

03-16.title: Mississippi
03-16.subtitle: Mississippi formally ratifies the Thirteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution, becoming the last state to approve the abolition of slavery.
03-16.text: Mississippi formally ratifies the Thirteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution, becoming the last state to approve the abolition of slavery. The Thirteenth Amendment was officially ratified in 1865.

03-18.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
03-18.subtitle: Bosnia's Bosniaks and Croats sign the Washington Agreement, ending war between the Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia an...
03-18.text: Bosnia's Bosniaks and Croats sign the Washington Agreement, ending war between the Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia and the Republic of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and establishing the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

03-18.title: Astronaut
03-18.subtitle: Cosmonaut Alexei Leonov, leaving his spacecraft Voskhod 2 for 12 minutes, becomes the first person to walk in space.
03-18.text: An astronaut is a person trained, equipped, and deployed by a human spaceflight program to serve as a commander or crew member of a spacecraft. Although generally reserved for professional space travelers, the term is sometimes applied to anyone who travels into space, including scientists, politicians, journalists, and space tourists. In the United States, it is a designated term used by three agencies: NASA, the FAA, and the military.

03-18.title: East Germany
03-18.subtitle: Germans in the German Democratic Republic vote in the first democratic elections in the former communist dictatorship.
03-18.text: Germans in the German Democratic Republic vote in the first democratic elections in the former communist dictatorship.

03-18.title: Art theft
03-18.subtitle: In the largest art theft in US history, 12 paintings, collectively worth around $500 million, are stolen from the Isabel...
03-18.text: In the largest art theft in US history, 12 paintings, collectively worth around $500 million, are stolen from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston.

03-18.title: Vostok-2M
03-18.subtitle: A Vostok-2M rocket at Plesetsk Cosmodrome Site 43 explodes during a fueling operation, killing 48 people.
03-18.text: A Vostok-2M rocket at Plesetsk Cosmodrome Site 43 explodes during a fueling operation, killing 48 people.

03-17.title: Vanguard 1
03-17.subtitle: The United States launches the first solar-powered satellite, which is also the first satellite to achieve a long-term orbit.
03-17.text: Vanguard 1 is an American satellite that was the fourth artificial Earth-orbiting satellite to be successfully launched, following Sputnik 1, Sputnik 2, and Explorer 1. It was launched 17 March 1958. Vanguard 1 was the first satellite to have solar electric power. Although communications with the satellite were lost in 1964, it remains the oldest artificial object still in orbit, together with the upper stage of its launch vehicle.
03-17.location: American satellite launched in 1958; oldest manmade object currently in Earth orbit

03-17.title: Eritrean War of Independence
03-17.subtitle: Eritrean War of Independence: The Nadew Command, an Ethiopian army corps in Eritrea, is attacked on three sides by milit...
03-17.text: Eritrean War of Independence: The Nadew Command, an Ethiopian army corps in Eritrea, is attacked on three sides by military units of the Eritrean People's Liberation Front in the opening action of the Battle of Afabet.

03-17.title: Pulitzer Prize
03-17.subtitle: The Pulitzer Prize–winning photograph Burst of Joy is taken, depicting a former prisoner of war being reunited with his ...
03-17.text: The Pulitzer Prize–winning photograph Burst of Joy is taken, depicting a former prisoner of war being reunited with his family, which came to symbolize the end of United States involvement in the Vietnam War.

03-17.title: Belgium
03-17.subtitle: Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and the United Kingdom sign the Treaty of Brussels, a precursor to the Nort...
03-17.text: Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and the United Kingdom sign the Treaty of Brussels, a precursor to the North Atlantic Treaty establishing NATO.

03-17.title: 1992 South African apartheid referendum
03-17.subtitle: A referendum to end apartheid in South Africa is passed 68.
03-17.text: A referendum to end apartheid in South Africa is passed 68.7% to 31.2%.

03-20.title: Eritrean War of Independence
03-20.subtitle: Eritrean War of Independence: Having defeated the Nadew Command, the Eritrean People's Liberation Front enters the town ...
03-20.text: Eritrean War of Independence: Having defeated the Nadew Command, the Eritrean People's Liberation Front enters the town of Afabet, victoriously concluding the Battle of Afabet.

03-20.title: European Space Agency
03-20.subtitle: The precursor of the European Space Agency, ESRO (European Space Research Organisation) is established per an agreement signed on June 14, 1962.
03-20.text: The European Space Agency (ESA), pronounced 'ee-sah', is a 23-member international organisation devoted to space exploration. It has its headquarters in Paris and a staff of around 2,547 people globally as of 2023. ESA was founded in 1975 in the context of European integration. Its 2026 annual budget was around US$10 billion.
03-20.location: European organisation dedicated to space exploration

03-20.title: The Troubles
03-20.subtitle: The Troubles: The first car bombing by the Provisional IRA in Belfast kills seven people and injures 148 others in Northern Ireland.
03-20.text: The Troubles: The first car bombing by the Provisional IRA in Belfast kills seven people and injures 148 others in Northern Ireland.

03-20.title: Albert Einstein
03-20.subtitle: Albert Einstein submits his paper, "The Foundation of the General Theory of Relativity", which establishes his general t...
03-20.text: Albert Einstein submits his paper, "The Foundation of the General Theory of Relativity", which establishes his general theory of relativity, to the journal Annalen der Physik.

03-20.title: Solar eclipse of March 20, 2015
03-20.subtitle: A Solar eclipse, equinox, and a supermoon all occur on the same day.
03-20.text: A total solar eclipse occurred at the Moon's descending node of orbit on Friday, March 20, 2015, with a magnitude of 1.0445. A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon passes between Earth and the Sun, thereby totally or partly obscuring the image of the Sun for a viewer on Earth. A total solar eclipse occurs when the Moon's apparent diameter is larger than the Sun's, blocking all direct sunlight, turning day into darkness.

03-21.title: Hungarian Soviet Republic
03-21.subtitle: The Hungarian Soviet Republic is established becoming the first Communist government to be formed in Europe after the October Revolution in Russia.
03-21.text: The Hungarian Soviet Republic is established becoming the first Communist government to be formed in Europe after the October Revolution in Russia.

03-21.title: Social media
03-21.subtitle: The social media site Twitter (now officially named X) is founded.
03-21.text: The social media site Twitter (now officially named X) is founded.

03-21.title: Alan Freed
03-21.subtitle: Alan Freed presents the Moondog Coronation Ball, the first rock and roll concert, in Cleveland, Ohio.
03-21.text: Alan Freed presents the Moondog Coronation Ball, the first rock and roll concert, in Cleveland, Ohio.

03-21.title: Ranger program
03-21.subtitle: Ranger program: NASA launches Ranger 9, the last in a series of uncrewed lunar space probes.
03-21.text: Ranger program: NASA launches Ranger 9, the last in a series of uncrewed lunar space probes.

03-21.title: Cold War
03-21.subtitle: Cold War: American President Jimmy Carter announces a United States boycott of the 1980 Summer Olympics in Moscow to protest the Soviet–Afghan War.
03-21.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-21.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-19.title: President of Kazakhstan
03-19.subtitle: The first President of Kazakhstan, Nursultan Nazarbayev, resigns from office after nearly three decades, leaving Senate ...
03-19.text: The first President of Kazakhstan, Nursultan Nazarbayev, resigns from office after nearly three decades, leaving Senate Chairman Kassym-Jomart Tokayev as the acting President and successor.

03-19.title: Egypt
03-19.subtitle: The Egyptian flag is raised at Taba, marking the end of Israeli occupation since the Six Days War in 1967 and the Egypt–Israel peace treaty in 1979.
03-19.text: The Egyptian flag is raised at Taba, marking the end of Israeli occupation since the Six Days War in 1967 and the Egypt–Israel peace treaty in 1979.

03-19.title: SS Georgiana
03-19.subtitle: The wreck of the SS Georgiana, valued at over $50,000,000 and said to have been the most powerful Confederate cruiser, i...
03-19.text: The wreck of the SS Georgiana, valued at over $50,000,000 and said to have been the most powerful Confederate cruiser, is discovered by teenage diver and pioneer underwater archaeologist E. Lee Spence, exactly 102 years after its destruction.

03-19.title: Ethnic clashes of Târgu Mureș
03-19.subtitle: The ethnic clashes of Târgu Mureș begin four days after the anniversary of the Revolutions of 1848 in the Austrian Empire.
03-19.text: The ethnic clashes of Târgu Mureș begin four days after the anniversary of the Revolutions of 1848 in the Austrian Empire.

03-19.title: Irish War of Independence
03-19.subtitle: Irish War of Independence: One of the biggest engagements of the war takes place at Crossbarry, County Cork.
03-19.text: Irish War of Independence: One of the biggest engagements of the war takes place at Crossbarry, County Cork. About 100 Irish Republican Army (IRA) volunteers escape an attempt by over 1,300 British forces to encircle them.

03-25.title: Space Shuttle
03-25.subtitle: The first fully functional Space Shuttle orbiter, Columbia, is delivered to the John F.
03-25.text: The first fully functional Space Shuttle orbiter, Columbia, is delivered to the John F. Kennedy Space Center to be prepared for its first launch.

03-25.title: European Economic Community
03-25.subtitle: The European Economic Community is established with West Germany, France, Italy, Belgium, Netherlands and Luxembourg as the first members.
03-25.text: The European Economic Community is established with West Germany, France, Italy, Belgium, Netherlands and Luxembourg as the first members.

03-25.title: WikiWikiWeb
03-25.subtitle: WikiWikiWeb, the world's first wiki, and part of the Portland Pattern Repository, is made public by Ward Cunningham.
03-25.text: WikiWikiWeb, the world's first wiki, and part of the Portland Pattern Repository, is made public by Ward Cunningham.

03-25.title: Army of the Republic of Vietnam
03-25.subtitle: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam abandon an attempt to cut off the Ho Chi Minh trail in Laos.
03-25.text: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam abandon an attempt to cut off the Ho Chi Minh trail in Laos.

03-25.title: Civil rights movement
03-25.subtitle: Civil rights activists led by Martin Luther King Jr.
03-25.text: Civil rights activists led by Martin Luther King Jr. successfully complete their 4-day 50-mile (80 km) march from Selma to the capitol in Montgomery, Alabama.

03-23.title: Pakistan
03-23.subtitle: Pakistan becomes the first Islamic republic in the world.
03-23.text: Pakistan becomes the first Islamic republic in the world. This date is now celebrated as Republic Day in Pakistan.

03-23.title: United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon
03-23.subtitle: The first UNIFIL troops arrived in Lebanon for peacekeeping mission along the Blue Line.
03-23.text: The first UNIFIL troops arrived in Lebanon for peacekeeping mission along the Blue Line.

03-23.title: David Frost's interviews with Richard Nixon
03-23.subtitle: The first of The Nixon Interviews (12 will be recorded over four weeks) is videotaped with British journalist David Fros...
03-23.text: The first of The Nixon Interviews (12 will be recorded over four weeks) is videotaped with British journalist David Frost interviewing former United States President Richard Nixon about the Watergate scandal and the Nixon tapes.

03-23.title: Revolutionary United Front
03-23.subtitle: The Revolutionary United Front, with support from the special forces of Charles Taylor's National Patriotic Front of Lib...
03-23.text: The Revolutionary United Front, with support from the special forces of Charles Taylor's National Patriotic Front of Liberia, invades Sierra Leone in an attempt to overthrow Joseph Saidu Momoh, sparking the 11-year Sierra Leone Civil War.

03-23.title: NASA
03-23.subtitle: NASA launches Gemini 3, the United States' first two-man space flight (crew: Gus Grissom and John Young).
03-23.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-26.title: Anwar Sadat
03-26.subtitle: Anwar al-Sadat, Menachem Begin and Jimmy Carter sign the Egypt–Israel peace treaty in Washington, D.
03-26.text: Anwar al-Sadat, Menachem Begin and Jimmy Carter sign the Egypt–Israel peace treaty in Washington, D.C.

03-26.title: Argentina
03-26.subtitle: Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay and Paraguay sign the Treaty of Asunción, establishing Mercosur, the South Common Market.
03-26.text: Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay and Paraguay sign the Treaty of Asunción, establishing Mercosur, the South Common Market.

03-26.title: East Pakistan
03-26.subtitle: East Pakistan, then province of Pakistan, declares its independence from Pakistan to form Bangladesh; the Bangladesh's War of Independence begins.
03-26.text: East Pakistan, then province of Pakistan, declares its independence from Pakistan to form Bangladesh; the Bangladesh's War of Independence begins.

03-26.title: Nuclear weapons testing
03-26.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: The Romeo shot of Operation Castle is detonated at Bikini Atoll.
03-26.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
03-26.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

03-26.title: Francis Scott Key Bridge
03-26.subtitle: The Francis Scott Key Bridge collapses following a collision between the MV Dali container ship and one of the bridge's ...
03-26.text: The Francis Scott Key Bridge collapses following a collision between the MV Dali container ship and one of the bridge's support pillars, killing 6 people.

03-28.title: Nobel Prize in Literature
03-28.subtitle: Greek poet and Nobel Prize laureate Giorgos Seferis makes a famous statement on the BBC World Service opposing the junta in Greece.
03-28.text: The Nobel Prize in Literature, here meaning for Literature, is a Swedish literature prize that is awarded annually to an author from any country who has, in the words of Alfred Nobel, "in the field of literature, produced the most outstanding work in an idealistic direction". Though individual works are sometimes cited as being particularly noteworthy, the award is based on an author's body of work as a whole. The Swedish Academy decides who, if anyone, will receive the prize.
03-28.location: Prize established in 1895 by Alfred Nobel

03-28.title: General
03-28.subtitle: General John J.
03-28.text: General John J. Pershing, during World War I, cancels 42nd 'Rainbow' Division's orders to Rolampont for further training and diverted it to the occupy the Baccarat sector. Rainbow Division becomes "the first American division to take over an entire sector on its own, which it held longer than any other American division-occupied sector alone for a period of three months".

03-28.title: Three Mile Island Nuclear Generating Station
03-28.subtitle: A coolant leak at the Three Mile Island's Unit 2 nuclear reactor outside Harrisburg, Pennsylvania leads to the core over...
03-28.text: A coolant leak at the Three Mile Island's Unit 2 nuclear reactor outside Harrisburg, Pennsylvania leads to the core overheating and a partial meltdown.

03-28.title: 2025 Myanmar earthquake
03-28.subtitle: An earthquake strikes close to Mandalay, Myanmar with a magnitude of 7.
03-28.text: An earthquake strikes close to Mandalay, Myanmar with a magnitude of 7.7, killing over 100 people.

03-28.title: African National Congress
03-28.subtitle: In South Africa, African National Congress security guards kill dozens of Inkatha Freedom Party protesters.
03-28.text: The African National Congress (ANC) is a political party in South Africa. It originated as a liberation movement known for its opposition to apartheid and has governed the country since 1994, when the first post-apartheid election resulted in Nelson Mandela being elected as President of South Africa. Cyril Ramaphosa, the incumbent national president, has served as president of the ANC since 18 December 2017.

03-29.title: Passover massacre
03-29.subtitle: In reaction to the Passover massacre two days prior, Israel launches Operation Defensive Shield against Palestinian mili...
03-29.text: In reaction to the Passover massacre two days prior, Israel launches Operation Defensive Shield against Palestinian militants, its largest military operation in the West Bank since the 1967 Six-Day War.

03-29.title: NASA
03-29.subtitle: NASA's Mariner 10 becomes the first space probe to fly by Mercury.
03-29.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-29.title: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
03-29.subtitle: Prime Minister Theresa May invokes Article 50 of the Treaty on European Union, formally beginning the United Kingdom's w...
03-29.text: The prime minister of the United Kingdom is the head of government of the United Kingdom. The prime minister advises the sovereign on the exercise of much of the royal prerogative, chairs the Cabinet, and selects its ministers. Modern prime ministers hold office by virtue of their ability to command the confidence of the House of Commons, so they are invariably members of Parliament.
03-29.location: Head of government of the United Kingdom

03-29.title: Bulgaria
03-29.subtitle: Bulgaria, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, and Slovenia join NATO as full members.
03-29.text: Bulgaria, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, and Slovenia join NATO as full members.

03-29.title: Arturo Frondizi
03-29.subtitle: Arturo Frondizi, the president of Argentina, is overthrown in a military coup by Argentina's armed forces, ending an 11+1⁄2 day constitutional crisis.
03-29.text: Arturo Frondizi, the president of Argentina, is overthrown in a military coup by Argentina's armed forces, ending an 11+1⁄2 day constitutional crisis.

03-22.title: NASA
03-22.subtitle: NASA's Space Shuttle Columbia is launched from the Kennedy Space Center on its third mission, STS-3.
03-22.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-22.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
03-22.subtitle: NASA's Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on its 16th mission, STS-76.
03-22.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

03-22.title: Astronaut
03-22.subtitle: Cosmonaut Valeri Polyakov returns to earth after setting a record of 438 days in space.
03-22.text: An astronaut is a person trained, equipped, and deployed by a human spaceflight program to serve as a commander or crew member of a spacecraft. Although generally reserved for professional space travelers, the term is sometimes applied to anyone who travels into space, including scientists, politicians, journalists, and space tourists. In the United States, it is a designated term used by three agencies: NASA, the FAA, and the military.

03-22.title: Intel
03-22.subtitle: The Intel Corporation ships the first Pentium chips (80586), featuring a 60 MHz clock speed, 100+ MIPS, and a 64 bit data path.
03-22.text: The Intel Corporation ships the first Pentium chips (80586), featuring a 60 MHz clock speed, 100+ MIPS, and a 64 bit data path.

03-22.title: The Beatles
03-22.subtitle: The Beatles release their debut album Please Please Me.
03-22.text: The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960. The core lineup of the band comprised John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr. They are widely regarded as the most influential band in popular music and were integral to the development of 1960s counterculture and the recognition of popular music as an art form.

03-27.title: Passover massacre
03-27.subtitle: Passover massacre: A Palestinian suicide bomber kills 29 people at a Passover seder in Netanya, Israel.
03-27.text: Passover massacre: A Palestinian suicide bomber kills 29 people at a Passover seder in Netanya, Israel.

03-27.title: Kosovo War
03-27.subtitle: Kosovo War: An American Lockheed F-117A Nighthawk is shot down by a Yugoslav Army SAM, the first and only Nighthawk to be lost in combat.
03-27.text: Kosovo War: An American Lockheed F-117A Nighthawk is shot down by a Yugoslav Army SAM, the first and only Nighthawk to be lost in combat.

03-27.title: Christian Democracy
03-27.subtitle: Italian former minister and Christian Democracy leader Giulio Andreotti is accused of mafia allegiance by the tribunal of Palermo.
03-27.text: Italian former minister and Christian Democracy leader Giulio Andreotti is accused of mafia allegiance by the tribunal of Palermo.

03-27.title: Government of the Philippines
03-27.subtitle: Philippines signs a peace accord with the largest Muslim rebel group, the Moro Islamic Liberation Front, ending decades of conflict.
03-27.text: Philippines signs a peace accord with the largest Muslim rebel group, the Moro Islamic Liberation Front, ending decades of conflict.

03-27.title: 1964 Alaska earthquake
03-27.subtitle: The Good Friday earthquake, the most powerful earthquake recorded in North American history at a magnitude of 9.
03-27.text: The Good Friday earthquake, the most powerful earthquake recorded in North American history at a magnitude of 9.2 strikes Southcentral Alaska, killing 125 people and inflicting massive damage to the city of Anchorage.

03-30.title: SpaceX
03-30.subtitle: SpaceX conducts the world's first reflight of an orbital class rocket.
03-30.text: SpaceX conducts the world's first reflight of an orbital class rocket.

03-30.title: Space Shuttle program
03-30.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: STS-3 mission is completed with the landing of Columbia at White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico.
03-30.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

03-30.title: Donald Trump
03-30.subtitle: Donald Trump becomes the first former United States president to be indicted by a grand jury.
03-30.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
03-30.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

03-30.title: Cold War
03-30.subtitle: Cold War: A riot breaks out in Austurvöllur square in Reykjavík, when Iceland joins NATO.
03-30.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-30.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-30.title: Airey Neave
03-30.subtitle: Airey Neave, a British Member of Parliament (MP), is killed by a car bomb as he exits the Palace of Westminster.
03-30.text: Airey Neave, a British Member of Parliament (MP), is killed by a car bomb as he exits the Palace of Westminster. The Irish National Liberation Army claims responsibility.
03-30.location: British politician, military officer and lawyer (1916–1979)

03-24.title: Kosovo War
03-24.subtitle: Kosovo War: NATO began attacks on Yugoslavia without United Nations Security Council (UNSC) approval, marking the first ...
03-24.text: Kosovo War: NATO began attacks on Yugoslavia without United Nations Security Council (UNSC) approval, marking the first time NATO has attacked a sovereign country.

03-24.title: Abdus Sattar
03-24.subtitle: Bangladeshi President Abdus Sattar is deposed in a bloodless coup led by Army Chief Lieutenant general Hussain Muhammad ...
03-24.text: Bangladeshi President Abdus Sattar is deposed in a bloodless coup led by Army Chief Lieutenant general Hussain Muhammad Ershad, who suspends the Constitution and imposes martial law.

03-24.title: Argentina
03-24.subtitle: In Argentina, the armed forces overthrow the constitutional government of President Isabel Perón and start a seven-year ...
03-24.text: In Argentina, the armed forces overthrow the constitutional government of President Isabel Perón and start a seven-year dictatorial period self-styled the National Reorganization Process.

03-24.title: Bhutan
03-24.subtitle: Bhutan officially becomes a democracy, with its first ever general election.
03-24.text: Bhutan officially becomes a democracy, with its first ever general election.

03-24.title: Rüdiger Marmulla
03-24.subtitle: Dr.
03-24.text: Dr. Rüdiger Marmulla performed the first computer-assisted Bone Segment Navigation at the University of Regensburg, Germany.

04-03.title: Osborne 1
04-03.subtitle: The Osborne 1, the first successful portable computer, is unveiled at the West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.
04-03.text: The Osborne 1, the first successful portable computer, is unveiled at the West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.

04-03.title: Apple Inc.
04-03.subtitle: Apple Inc.
04-03.text: Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, in Silicon Valley, best known for its consumer electronics, software and online services. Founded in 1976 as Apple Computer Company by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne, the company was incorporated by Jobs and Wozniak as Apple Computer, Inc. the following year. It was renamed to its current name in 2007 as the company had expanded its focus from computers to consumer electronics.

04-03.title: Bobby Fischer
04-03.subtitle: Bobby Fischer refuses to play in a chess match against Anatoly Karpov, giving Karpov the title of World Champion by default.
04-03.text: Bobby Fischer refuses to play in a chess match against Anatoly Karpov, giving Karpov the title of World Champion by default.

04-03.title: Martin Cooper
04-03.subtitle: Martin Cooper of Motorola makes the first handheld mobile phone call to Joel S.
04-03.text: Martin Cooper of Motorola makes the first handheld mobile phone call to Joel S. Engel of Bell Labs.

04-03.title: 1974 Super Outbreak
04-03.subtitle: The 1974 Super Outbreak occurs, the second largest tornado outbreak in recorded history (after the 2011 Super Outbreak).
04-03.text: The 1974 Super Outbreak occurs, the second largest tornado outbreak in recorded history (after the 2011 Super Outbreak). The death toll is 315, with nearly 5,500 injured.

04-02.title: As the World Turns
04-02.subtitle: As the World Turns and The Edge of Night premiere on CBS.
04-02.text: As the World Turns and The Edge of Night premiere on CBS. The two soaps become the first daytime dramas to debut in the 30-minute format.

04-02.title: Rita Johnston
04-02.subtitle: Rita Johnston becomes the first female Premier of a Canadian province when she succeeds William Vander Zalm (who had res...
04-02.text: Rita Johnston becomes the first female Premier of a Canadian province when she succeeds William Vander Zalm (who had resigned) as Premier of British Columbia.

04-02.title: Hatton Garden safe deposit burglary
04-02.subtitle: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area i...
04-02.text: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area in what has been called the "largest burglary in English legal history."

04-02.title: Bijeljina massacre
04-02.subtitle: Forty-two civilians are massacred in the town of Bijeljina in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
04-02.text: Forty-two civilians are massacred in the town of Bijeljina in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

04-02.title: India national cricket team
04-02.subtitle: India wins the Cricket World Cup for the second time in history under the captaincy of MS Dhoni.
04-02.text: India wins the Cricket World Cup for the second time in history under the captaincy of MS Dhoni.

04-01.title: Bangladesh Liberation War
04-01.subtitle: Bangladesh Liberation War: The Pakistan Army massacre more than a thousand people in Keraniganj Upazila, Bangladesh.
04-01.text: The Bangladesh Liberation War, also known as the Bangladesh War of Independence, was an armed conflict sparked by the rise of the Bengali nationalist and self-determination movement in East Pakistan, which resulted in the independence of Bangladesh with the help of India. The war began when the Pakistani military junta based in West Pakistan—under the orders of Yahya Khan—launched Operation Searchlight against East Pakistanis on the night of 25 March 1971, initiating the Bangladesh genocide.

04-01.title: Steve Jobs
04-01.subtitle: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.
04-01.text: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.

04-01.title: President of Yugoslavia
04-01.subtitle: Former President of Federal Republic of Yugoslavia Slobodan Milošević surrenders to police special forces, to be tried on war crimes charges.
04-01.text: Former President of Federal Republic of Yugoslavia Slobodan Milošević surrenders to police special forces, to be tried on war crimes charges.

04-01.title: Lockheed EP-3
04-01.subtitle: An EP-3E United States Navy surveillance aircraft collides with a Chinese People's Liberation Army Shenyang J-8 fighter jet.
04-01.text: An EP-3E United States Navy surveillance aircraft collides with a Chinese People's Liberation Army Shenyang J-8 fighter jet. The Chinese pilot ejected but is subsequently lost. The Navy crew makes an emergency landing in Hainan, China and is detained.

04-01.title: Television Infrared Observation Satellite
04-01.subtitle: The TIROS-1 satellite transmits the first television picture from space.
04-01.text: The TIROS-1 satellite transmits the first television picture from space.

04-04.title: Space Shuttle program
04-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Challenger makes its maiden voyage into space on STS-6.
04-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

04-04.title: Space Shuttle program
04-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-83.
04-04.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

04-04.title: John Heinz
04-04.subtitle: Senator John Heinz of Pennsylvania and six others are killed when a helicopter collides with their airplane over an elem...
04-04.text: Senator John Heinz of Pennsylvania and six others are killed when a helicopter collides with their airplane over an elementary school in Merion, Pennsylvania.

04-04.title: Microsoft
04-04.subtitle: Microsoft is founded as a partnership between Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
04-04.text: Microsoft is founded as a partnership between Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

04-04.title: MPLA
04-04.subtitle: The MPLA government of Angola and UNITA rebels sign a peace treaty ending the Angolan Civil War.
04-04.text: The MPLA government of Angola and UNITA rebels sign a peace treaty ending the Angolan Civil War.

04-05.title: Ripple Rock
04-05.subtitle: Ripple Rock, an underwater threat to navigation in the Seymour Narrows in Canada is destroyed in one of the largest non-...
04-05.text: Ripple Rock, an underwater threat to navigation in the Seymour Narrows in Canada is destroyed in one of the largest non-nuclear controlled explosions of the time.

04-05.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-05.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-131 to resupply the International Space Station.
04-05.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-131 to resupply the International Space Station.

04-05.title: North Korea
04-05.subtitle: North Korea launches its controversial Kwangmyŏngsŏng-2 satellite.
04-05.text: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia. It constitutes the northern half of the Korean Peninsula and borders China and Russia to the north at the Yalu (Amnok) and Tumen rivers, and South Korea to the south at the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ). The country's western border is formed by the Yellow Sea, while its eastern border is defined by the Sea of Japan.

04-05.title: Suada and Olga bridge
04-05.subtitle: Peace protesters Suada Dilberovic and Olga Sučić are killed on the Vrbanja Bridge in Sarajevo, becoming the first casualties of the Bosnian War.
04-05.text: Peace protesters Suada Dilberovic and Olga Sučić are killed on the Vrbanja Bridge in Sarajevo, becoming the first casualties of the Bosnian War.

04-05.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
04-05.subtitle: The Space shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-37 to deploy the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory.
04-05.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

03-31.title: Remington Rand
03-31.subtitle: Remington Rand delivers the first UNIVAC I computer to the United States Census Bureau.
03-31.text: Remington Rand delivers the first UNIVAC I computer to the United States Census Bureau.

03-31.title: NASA
03-31.subtitle: NASA astronaut Scott Kelly and Roscosmos cosmonaut Mikhail Kornienko return to Earth after a yearlong mission at the International Space Station.
03-31.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-31.title: Soviet Union
03-31.subtitle: The Soviet Union launches Luna 10 which later becomes the first space probe to enter orbit around the Moon.
03-31.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

03-31.title: Dwarf planet
03-31.subtitle: The dwarf planet Makemake is discovered by a team led by astronomer Michael E.
03-31.text: A dwarf planet is a small planetary-mass object that is in direct orbit around the Sun, massive enough to be gravitationally rounded, but insufficient to achieve orbital dominance like the eight classical planets of the Solar System. The prototypical dwarf planet is Pluto, which for decades was regarded as a planet before the "dwarf" concept was adopted in 2006.

03-31.title: Olímpio Mourão Filho
03-31.subtitle: Brazilian General Olímpio Mourão Filho orders his troops to move towards Rio de Janeiro, beginning the coup d'état and 2...
03-31.text: Olímpio Mourão Filho was a Brazilian military officer known as the author of the Cohen Plan, a forged document used to justify the Estado Novo coup in 1937, and, as head of the 4th Military Region/Infantry Division, as the precipitator of the 1964 coup d'état that installed the military dictatorship in Brazil. He reached the rank of army general and ended his career presiding over the Superior Military Court (STM) from 1967 to 1969.
03-31.location: 20th-century Brazilian general and Integralist; leader in the 1964 coup

04-08.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on mission STS-56.
04-08.text: The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on mission STS-56.
