04-08.subtitle: The conservative New Democracy party of Constantine Mitsotakis is elected in the Greek parliamentary election.
04-08.text: The conservative New Democracy party of Constantine Mitsotakis is elected in the Greek parliamentary election.

04-08.title: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024
04-08.subtitle: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.
04-08.text: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.

04-08.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
04-08.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on mission STS-110, carrying the S0 truss to the International Space Station.
04-08.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

04-08.title: Barack Obama
04-08.subtitle: U.
04-08.text: Barack Hussein Obama II is an American politician who served as the 44th president of the United States from 2009 to 2017. A member of the Democratic Party, he was the first African American president. Obama previously served as a U.S. senator representing Illinois from 2005 to 2008 and as an Illinois state senator from 1997 to 2004.
04-08.location: President of the United States from 2009 to 2017

04-07.title: Israel Defense Forces
04-07.subtitle: The Israel Defense Forces use their Iron Dome missile system to successfully intercept a BM-21 Grad launched from Gaza, ...
04-07.text: The Israel Defense Forces use their Iron Dome missile system to successfully intercept a BM-21 Grad launched from Gaza, marking the first short-range missile intercept ever.

04-07.title: STS-6
04-07.subtitle: During STS-6, astronauts Story Musgrave and Don Peterson perform the first Space Shuttle spacewalk.
04-07.text: STS-6 was the sixth NASA Space Shuttle mission and the maiden flight of the Space Shuttle Challenger. Launched from Kennedy Space Center on April 4, 1983, the mission deployed the first Tracking and Data Relay Satellite, TDRS-1, into orbit, before landing at Edwards Air Force Base on April 9, 1983. STS-6 was the first Space Shuttle mission during which a Extravehicular activity was conducted, and hence was the first in which the Extravehicular Mobility Unit (EMU) was used.
04-07.location: 1983 American crewed spaceflight and maiden flight of Space Shuttle Challenger

04-07.title: First Chechen War
04-07.subtitle: First Chechen War: Russian paramilitary troops begin a massacre of civilians in Samashki, Chechnya.
04-07.text: The First Chechen War, also referred to as the First Russo-Chechen War, was a conflict between the separatist Chechen Republic of Ichkeria and the Russian Federation from 1994 to 1996. The conflict ended in a peace treaty that saw Russian forces withdraw from the territory only for them to invade again three years later sparking the Second Chechen War of 1999–2009.
04-07.location: 1994–1996 war between Russia and Chechen separatists

04-07.title: AT&T Corporation
04-07.subtitle: AT&T engineer Herbert Ives transmits the first long-distance public television broadcast (from Washington, D.
04-07.text: AT&T Corporation, an abbreviation of its former name, the American Telephone and Telegraph Company, was an American telecommunications company that provided voice, video, data, and Internet telecommunications and professional services to businesses, consumers, and government agencies.

04-07.title: NASA
04-07.subtitle: NASA launches the 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter.
04-07.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

04-06.title: Rolandas Paksas
04-06.subtitle: Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed from office by impeachment.
04-06.text: Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed from office by impeachment.

04-06.title: Gaafar Nimeiry
04-06.subtitle: Sudanese President Gaafar Nimeiry is ousted from power in a coup d'état led by Field Marshal Abdel Rahman Swar al-Dahab.
04-06.text: Sudanese President Gaafar Nimeiry is ousted from power in a coup d'état led by Field Marshal Abdel Rahman Swar al-Dahab.

04-06.title: California Jam
04-06.subtitle: The first California Jam festival takes place at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California.
04-06.text: The first California Jam festival takes place at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California. Co-headlined by Deep Purple and Emerson, Lake & Palmer. The festival set what were then records for the loudest amplification system ever installed, the highest paid attendance, and highest gross in history.

04-06.title: Brighton
04-06.subtitle: In Brighton, United Kingdom, ABBA wins the 1974 edition of the Eurovision Song Contest with "Waterloo", the first of a j...
04-06.text: In Brighton, United Kingdom, ABBA wins the 1974 edition of the Eurovision Song Contest with "Waterloo", the first of a joint-record seven Swedish wins.

04-06.title: Intelsat I
04-06.subtitle: Launch of Early Bird, the first commercial communications satellite to be placed in geosynchronous orbit.
04-06.text: Launch of Early Bird, the first commercial communications satellite to be placed in geosynchronous orbit.

04-09.title: Space Shuttle program
04-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-59.
04-09.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

04-09.title: Project Mercury
04-09.subtitle: Project Mercury: NASA announces the selection of the United States' first seven astronauts, whom the news media quickly dub the "Mercury Seven".
04-09.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

04-09.title: Tbilisi massacre
04-09.subtitle: Tbilisi massacre: An anti-Soviet peaceful demonstration and hunger strike in Tbilisi, demanding restoration of Georgian ...
04-09.text: Tbilisi massacre: An anti-Soviet peaceful demonstration and hunger strike in Tbilisi, demanding restoration of Georgian independence, is dispersed by the Soviet Army, resulting in 20 deaths and hundreds of injuries.

04-09.title: Hugo Ballivián
04-09.subtitle: Hugo Ballivián's government is overthrown by the Bolivian National Revolution, starting a period of agrarian reform, uni...
04-09.text: Hugo Ballivián's government is overthrown by the Bolivian National Revolution, starting a period of agrarian reform, universal suffrage and the nationalization of tin mines

04-09.title: Hendrik Verwoerd
04-09.subtitle: Dr.
04-09.text: Dr. Hendrik Verwoerd, Prime Minister of South Africa and architect of apartheid, narrowly survives an assassination attempt by a white farmer, David Pratt in Johannesburg.

04-10.title: Yinqueshan Han Tombs Bamboo Slips Museum
04-10.subtitle: Tombs containing bamboo slips, among them Sun Tzu's Art of War and Sun Bin's lost military treatise, are discovered by c...
04-10.text: Tombs containing bamboo slips, among them Sun Tzu's Art of War and Sun Bin's lost military treatise, are discovered by construction workers in Shandong.

04-10.title: Vietnam War
04-10.subtitle: Vietnam War: For the first time since November 1967, American B-52 bombers reportedly begin bombing North Vietnam.
04-10.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
04-10.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

04-10.title: Paul McCartney
04-10.subtitle: Paul McCartney announces that he is leaving The Beatles for personal and professional reasons.
04-10.text: Paul McCartney announces that he is leaving The Beatles for personal and professional reasons.

04-10.title: South Atlantic tropical cyclone
04-10.subtitle: A rare tropical storm develops in the South Atlantic Ocean near Angola; the first to be documented by satellites.
04-10.text: A rare tropical storm develops in the South Atlantic Ocean near Angola; the first to be documented by satellites.

04-10.title: President of Fiji
04-10.subtitle: President of Fiji Ratu Josefa Iloilo announces the abrogation of the constitution and assumes all governance in the coun...
04-10.text: President of Fiji Ratu Josefa Iloilo announces the abrogation of the constitution and assumes all governance in the country, creating a constitutional crisis.

04-11.title: Australia men's national soccer team
04-11.subtitle: The Australia national men's soccer team sets a world record for the largest victory in an international association foo...
04-11.text: The Australia national men's soccer team sets a world record for the largest victory in an international association football match, winning the game 31–0 against American Samoa at the 2002 FIFA World Cup qualifiers for OFC. Australia's Archie Thompson also breaks the record for most goals scored by a player in an international match by scoring 13 goals.

04-11.title: 2012 Indian Ocean earthquakes
04-11.subtitle: A pair of great earthquakes occur in the Wharton Basin west of Sumatra in Indonesia.
04-11.text: A pair of great earthquakes occur in the Wharton Basin west of Sumatra in Indonesia. The maximum Mercalli intensity of this strike-slip doublet earthquake is VII (Very strong). Ten are killed, twelve are injured, and a non-destructive tsunami is observed on the island of Nias.

04-11.title: Borussia Dortmund
04-11.subtitle: The tour bus of the German football team Borussia Dortmund was attacked with roadside bombs in Dortmund, Germany.
04-11.text: The tour bus of the German football team Borussia Dortmund was attacked with roadside bombs in Dortmund, Germany. Three bombs exploded as the bus ferried the team to the Westfalenstadion for the first leg of their quarter-final against Monaco.

04-11.title: Pope John XXIII
04-11.subtitle: Pope John XXIII issues Pacem in terris, the first encyclical addressed to all Christians instead of only Catholics, and ...
04-11.text: Pope John XXIII issues Pacem in terris, the first encyclical addressed to all Christians instead of only Catholics, and which described the conditions for world peace in human terms.

04-11.title: Air India
04-11.subtitle: The Air India Kashmir Princess is bombed and crashes in a failed assassination attempt on Zhou Enlai by the Kuomintang.
04-11.text: Air India is the flag carrier of India with its main hub at Indira Gandhi International Airport in Delhi, and secondary hubs at Kempegowda International Airport in Bengaluru and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport in Mumbai, alongside several focus cities across India. Headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, the airline is owned by Air India Limited, which is owned by the Tata Group (74.9%) and Singapore Airlines (25.1%).

04-13.title: World War II
04-13.subtitle: World War II: The discovery of mass graves of Polish prisoners of war killed by Soviet forces in the Katyń Forest Massac...
04-13.text: World War II: The discovery of mass graves of Polish prisoners of war killed by Soviet forces in the Katyń Forest Massacre is announced, causing a diplomatic rift between the Polish government-in-exile in London and the Soviet Union, which denies responsibility.

04-13.title: Oxygen
04-13.subtitle: At 10:08 PM EST an oxygen tank aboard the Apollo 13 Service Module explodes, putting the crew in great danger and causin...
04-13.text: At 10:08 PM EST an oxygen tank aboard the Apollo 13 Service Module explodes, putting the crew in great danger and causing major damage to the Apollo command and service module (codenamed "Odyssey") while en route to the Moon.

04-13.title: 36th Academy Awards
04-13.subtitle: At the Academy Awards, Sidney Poitier becomes the first African-American man to win the Best Actor award for the 1963 film Lilies of the Field.
04-13.text: At the Academy Awards, Sidney Poitier becomes the first African-American man to win the Best Actor award for the 1963 film Lilies of the Field.

04-13.title: 1975 Beirut bus massacre
04-13.subtitle: An attack by the Phalangist resistance kills 26 militia members of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine, ma...
04-13.text: The 1975 Beirut bus massacre, also known as the Ain el-Rammaneh incident and Black Sunday, was the collective name given to a short series of armed clashes involving Phalangist and Palestinian elements in the streets of central Beirut, which is commonly presented as the spark that set off the Lebanese Civil War in the mid-1970s.
04-13.location: 1975 armed clashes between Phalangist and Palestinian organizations in Beirut, Lebanon

04-13.title: Transit
04-13.subtitle: The United States launches Transit 1-B, the world's first satellite navigation system.
04-13.text: The United States launches Transit 1-B, the world's first satellite navigation system.

04-12.title: Space Race
04-12.subtitle: Space Race: The Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human to travel into outer space and perform the first c...
04-12.text: The Space Race was a 20th-century competition between the Cold War rivals, the United States and the Soviet Union, to achieve superior spaceflight capability. It had its origins in the ballistic missile-based nuclear arms race between the two nations following World War II and the onset of the Cold War.

04-12.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-12.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-51D to deploy two communications satellites.
04-12.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-51D to deploy two communications satellites.

04-12.text: Shanghai massacre of 1927: Chiang Kai-shek orders the Chinese Communist Party members executed in Shanghai, ending the First United Front.[citation needed]

04-12.text: During the NATO bombing of Yugoslavia, an American McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle shoots a passenger train, killing between 20 and 60 people.

04-15.text: U.S. Senator John B. Kendrick of Wyoming introduces a resolution calling for an investigation of a secret land deal, which leads to the discovery of the Teapot Dome scandal.

04-15.subtitle: At Shaw University in Raleigh, North Carolina, Ella Baker leads a conference that results in the creation of the Student...
04-15.text: At Shaw University in Raleigh, North Carolina, Ella Baker leads a conference that results in the creation of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, one of the principal organizations of the civil rights movement in the 1960s.

04-15.subtitle: During the Cambodian Civil War, massacre of the Vietnamese minority results in 800 bodies flowing down the Mekong river into South Vietnam.
04-15.text: During the Cambodian Civil War, massacre of the Vietnamese minority results in 800 bodies flowing down the Mekong river into South Vietnam.

04-15.text: Hillsborough disaster: A human crush occurs at Hillsborough Stadium, home of Sheffield Wednesday, in the FA Cup Semi-final, resulting in the deaths of 97 Liverpool fans.

04-15.text: The EC-121 shootdown incident: North Korea shoots down a United States Navy aircraft over the Sea of Japan, killing all 31 on board.

04-16.text: The Pulitzer Prize winners were announced, it was the first time since 1977 that no book won the Fiction Prize.

04-16.text: The Treaty of Accession is signed in Athens admitting ten new member states to the European Union.

04-16.title: The New York Times
04-16.subtitle: The New York Times and the New Yorker win the Pulitzer Prize for Public Service for breaking news of the Harvey Weinstein sexual abuse scandal.
04-16.text: The New York Times and the New Yorker win the Pulitzer Prize for Public Service for breaking news of the Harvey Weinstein sexual abuse scandal.

04-16.title: Martin Luther King Jr.
04-16.subtitle: U.
04-16.text: U.S. civil rights campaigner Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. writes his open letter from Birmingham Jail, sometimes known as "The Negro Is Your Brother", while incarcerated in Birmingham, Alabama, for protesting against segregation.

04-16.title: Apollo program
04-16.subtitle: Apollo program: The launch of Apollo 16 from Cape Canaveral, Florida.
04-16.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

04-14.title: Georgia
04-14.subtitle: The Republic of Georgia introduces the post of President following its declaration of independence from the Soviet Union.
04-14.text: The Republic of Georgia introduces the post of President following its declaration of independence from the Soviet Union.

04-14.title: United Nations
04-14.subtitle: In a United Nations ceremony in Geneva, Switzerland, the Soviet Union signs an agreement pledging to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan.
04-14.text: In a United Nations ceremony in Geneva, Switzerland, the Soviet Union signs an agreement pledging to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan.

04-14.title: Soviet Union
04-14.subtitle: The Soviet satellite Sputnik 2 falls from orbit after a mission duration of 162 days.
04-14.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

04-14.title: Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer
04-14.subtitle: The Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (JUICE) is launched by the European Space Agency.
04-14.text: The Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer is an interplanetary spacecraft developed by the European Space Agency (ESA) and on its way to orbit and study three icy moons of Jupiter: Ganymede, Callisto, and Europa. These planetary-mass moons are planned to be studied because they are thought to have significant bodies of liquid water beneath their frozen surfaces, which would make them potentially habitable for extraterrestrial life.
04-14.location: European mission to study Jupiter and its moons since 2023

04-14.title: STS-1
04-14.subtitle: STS-1: The first operational Space Shuttle, Columbia completes its first test flight.
04-14.text: STS-1 was the first orbital spaceflight of NASA's Space Shuttle program. The first orbiter, Columbia, launched on April 12, 1981, and returned on April 14, 1981, 54.5 hours later, having orbited the Earth 37 times. Columbia carried a crew of two—commander John W. Young and pilot Robert L. Crippen. It was the first American crewed space flight since the Apollo–Soyuz Test Project (ASTP) in 1975.
04-14.location: First Space Shuttle mission, first orbital flight of the Space Shuttle Columbia

04-17.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
04-17.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-90, the final Spacelab mission.
04-17.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

04-17.title: Constitution Act, 1982
04-17.subtitle: Constitution Act, 1982 Patriation of the Canadian constitution in Ottawa by Proclamation of Elizabeth II, Queen of Canada.
04-17.text: Constitution Act, 1982 Patriation of the Canadian constitution in Ottawa by Proclamation of Elizabeth II, Queen of Canada.

04-17.title: Apollo program
04-17.subtitle: Apollo program: The damaged Apollo 13 spacecraft returns to Earth safely.
04-17.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

04-17.title: Cambodian Civil War
04-17.subtitle: The Cambodian Civil War ends and the Cambodian Genocide begins.
04-17.text: The Cambodian Civil War ends and the Cambodian Genocide begins. The Khmer Rouge captures the capital Phnom Penh and Cambodian government forces surrender.

04-17.title: NASA
04-17.subtitle: NASA's Kepler space telescope confirms the discovery of the first Earth-size planet in the habitable zone of another star.
04-17.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

04-18.title: Zimbabwe
04-18.subtitle: The Republic of Zimbabwe (formerly Rhodesia) comes into being, with Canaan Banana as the country's first President.
04-18.text: The Republic of Zimbabwe (formerly Rhodesia) comes into being, with Canaan Banana as the country's first President. The Zimbabwean dollar replaces the Rhodesian dollar as the official currency.

04-18.title: War of the First Coalition
04-18.subtitle: War of the First Coalition: The Peace of Leoben is signed by Napoleon Bonaparte and Maximilian, Count of Merveldt, creat...
04-18.text: The War of the First Coalition was a set of wars between a coalition of several European powers and France fought between 1792 and 1797. The coalition was only loosely allied and fought without much coordination; each power wanted to annex a different part of France should they defeat the French, something that never occurred.
04-18.location: 1792–1797 battles between French revolutionaries and neighbouring monarchies

04-18.title: Qana massacre
04-18.subtitle: The Israeli military commits the Qana massacre in a deliberate shelling of a United Nations compound near the village of...
04-18.text: The Israeli military commits the Qana massacre in a deliberate shelling of a United Nations compound near the village of Qana in southern Lebanon, killing 106 Lebanese civilians who were taking shelter there and wounding over 100 more.

04-18.title: Republic of Venice
04-18.subtitle: Peace of Ferrara between Republic of Venice, Duchy of Milan, Republic of Florence and House of Gonzaga: ending of the se...
04-18.text: Peace of Ferrara between Republic of Venice, Duchy of Milan, Republic of Florence and House of Gonzaga: ending of the second campaign of the Wars in Lombardy fought until the Treaty of Lodi in 1454, which will then guarantee the conditions for the development of the Italian Renaissance.

04-18.title: Operation Praying Mantis
04-18.subtitle: The United States launches Operation Praying Mantis against Iranian naval forces in the largest naval battle since World War II.
04-18.text: The United States launches Operation Praying Mantis against Iranian naval forces in the largest naval battle since World War II.

04-19.title: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and
04-19.subtitle: Two hundred ATF and FBI agents lay siege to the compound of the white supremacist survivalist group The Covenant, the Sw...
04-19.text: Two hundred ATF and FBI agents lay siege to the compound of the white supremacist survivalist group The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord in Arkansas; the CSA surrenders two days later.

04-19.title: The Simpsons
04-19.subtitle: The Simpsons first appear as a series of shorts on The Tracey Ullman Show, first starting with "Good Night".
04-19.text: The Simpsons first appear as a series of shorts on The Tracey Ullman Show, first starting with "Good Night".

04-19.title: Sierra Leone
04-19.subtitle: Sierra Leone becomes a republic, and Siaka Stevens the president.
04-19.text: Sierra Leone becomes a republic, and Siaka Stevens the president.

04-19.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
04-19.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-100 carrying the Canadarm2 to the International Space Station.
04-19.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

04-19.title: India
04-19.subtitle: India's first satellite Aryabhata launched in orbit from Kapustin Yar, Russia.
04-19.text: India's first satellite Aryabhata launched in orbit from Kapustin Yar, Russia.

04-20.title: SpaceX Starship
04-20.subtitle: SpaceX's Starship rocket, the largest and most powerful rocket ever built, launches for the first time.
04-20.text: SpaceX's Starship rocket, the largest and most powerful rocket ever built, launches for the first time. It explodes four minutes into flight.

04-20.title: Johnson Space Center shooting
04-20.subtitle: Johnson Space Center shooting: William Phillips barricades himself with a handgun in NASA's Johnson Space Center in Hous...
04-20.text: Johnson Space Center shooting: William Phillips barricades himself with a handgun in NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas before killing a male hostage and himself.

04-20.title: Apollo program
04-20.subtitle: Apollo program: Apollo 16 Lunar Module, commanded by John Young and piloted by Charles Duke, lands on the Moon.
04-20.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

04-20.title: University of California, Riverside 1985 laboratory
04-20.subtitle: University of California, Riverside 1985 laboratory raid: Animal Liberation Front rescues 467 animals being tested in a ...
04-20.text: University of California, Riverside 1985 laboratory raid: Animal Liberation Front rescues 467 animals being tested in a lab at University of California, Riverside in Riverside, California, causing $700,000 in damages to the laboratory in advocation for Animal rights.

04-20.title: Garowe attack
04-20.subtitle: Ten people are killed in a bomb attack on a convoy carrying food supplies to a United Nations compound in Garowe in the Somali region of Puntland.
04-20.text: Ten people are killed in a bomb attack on a convoy carrying food supplies to a United Nations compound in Garowe in the Somali region of Puntland.

04-22.title: Optical fiber
04-22.subtitle: Optical fiber is first used to carry live telephone traffic.
04-22.text: Optical fiber is first used to carry live telephone traffic.

04-22.title: Red Scare
04-22.subtitle: Red Scare: Witnesses begin testifying and live television coverage of the Army–McCarthy hearings begins.
04-22.text: Red Scare: Witnesses begin testifying and live television coverage of the Army–McCarthy hearings begins.

04-22.title: Junichiro Koizumi
04-22.subtitle: Japan's Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi apologizes for Japan's war record.
04-22.text: Japan's Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi apologizes for Japan's war record.

04-22.title: Robin Knox-Johnston
04-22.subtitle: British yachtsman Sir Robin Knox-Johnston wins the Sunday Times Golden Globe Race and completes the first solo non-stop circumnavigation of the world.
04-22.text: British yachtsman Sir Robin Knox-Johnston wins the Sunday Times Golden Globe Race and completes the first solo non-stop circumnavigation of the world.

04-22.title: 2020 Eastern Freeway truck crash
04-22.subtitle: Four police officers are killed after being struck by a truck on the Eastern Freeway in Melbourne while speaking to a sp...
04-22.text: Four police officers are killed after being struck by a truck on the Eastern Freeway in Melbourne while speaking to a speeding driver, marking the largest loss of police lives in Victoria Police history.

04-21.title: John Young
04-21.subtitle: Astronauts John Young and Charles Duke fly Apollo 16's Apollo Lunar Module to the Moon's surface, the fifth NASA Apollo Program crewed lunar landing.
04-21.text: Astronauts John Young and Charles Duke fly Apollo 16's Apollo Lunar Module to the Moon's surface, the fifth NASA Apollo Program crewed lunar landing.

04-21.title: Transit
04-21.subtitle: A Transit-5bn satellite fails to reach orbit after launch; as it re-enters the atmosphere, 2.
04-21.text: A Transit-5bn satellite fails to reach orbit after launch; as it re-enters the atmosphere, 2.1 pounds (0.95 kg) of radioactive plutonium in its SNAP RTG power source is widely dispersed.

04-21.title: Kharkiv Pact
04-21.subtitle: The controversial Kharkiv Pact (Russian Ukrainian Naval Base for Gas Treaty) is signed in Kharkiv, Ukraine, by Ukrainian...
04-21.text: The controversial Kharkiv Pact (Russian Ukrainian Naval Base for Gas Treaty) is signed in Kharkiv, Ukraine, by Ukrainian president Viktor Yanukovych and Russian president Dmitry Medvedev; it was unilaterally terminated by Russia on March 31, 2014.

04-21.title: Baseball
04-21.subtitle: Baseball: Rollie Fingers of the Milwaukee Brewers becomes the first pitcher to record 300 saves.
04-21.text: Baseball: Rollie Fingers of the Milwaukee Brewers becomes the first pitcher to record 300 saves.

04-21.title: Century 21 Exposition
04-21.subtitle: The Seattle World's Fair (Century 21 Exposition) opens.
04-21.text: The Century 21 Exposition was a world's fair held April 21, 1962, to October 21, 1962, in Seattle, Washington, United States. Nearly 10 million people attended the fair during its six-month run.
04-21.location: World's fair held in Seattle, Washington

04-23.title: NATO
04-23.subtitle: NATO bombs the headquarters of Radio Television of Serbia, as part of their aerial campaign against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.
04-23.text: NATO bombs the headquarters of Radio Television of Serbia, as part of their aerial campaign against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

04-23.title: Soviet space program
04-23.subtitle: Soviet space program: Soyuz 1 (Russian: Союз 1, Union 1) a crewed spaceflight carrying cosmonaut Colonel Vladimir Komarov is launched into orbit.
04-23.text: Soviet space program: Soyuz 1 (Russian: Союз 1, Union 1) a crewed spaceflight carrying cosmonaut Colonel Vladimir Komarov is launched into orbit.

04-23.title: Namibia
04-23.subtitle: Namibia becomes the 160th member of the United Nations and the 50th member of the Commonwealth of Nations.
04-23.text: Namibia becomes the 160th member of the United Nations and the 50th member of the Commonwealth of Nations.

04-23.title: Eritrea
04-23.subtitle: Eritreans vote overwhelmingly for independence from Ethiopia in a United Nations-monitored referendum.
04-23.text: Eritreans vote overwhelmingly for independence from Ethiopia in a United Nations-monitored referendum.

04-23.title: Bangladesh Liberation War
04-23.subtitle: Bangladesh Liberation War: The Pakistan Army and Razakars massacre approximately 3,000 Hindu emigrants in the Jathibhang...
04-23.text: The Bangladesh Liberation War, also known as the Bangladesh War of Independence, was an armed conflict sparked by the rise of the Bengali nationalist and self-determination movement in East Pakistan, which resulted in the independence of Bangladesh with the help of India. The war began when the Pakistani military junta based in West Pakistan—under the orders of Yahya Khan—launched Operation Searchlight against East Pakistanis on the night of 25 March 1971, initiating the Bangladesh genocide.

04-28.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-28.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-39, the first unclassified shuttle mission for the United States Department of Defense.
04-28.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-39, the first unclassified shuttle mission for the United States Department of Defense.

04-28.title: Treaty of Taipei
04-28.subtitle: The Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty (Treaty of Taipei) is signed in Taipei, Taiwan between Japan and the Republic of China to...
04-28.text: The Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty (Treaty of Taipei) is signed in Taipei, Taiwan between Japan and the Republic of China to officially end the Second Sino-Japanese War.

04-28.title: Treaty of San Francisco
04-28.subtitle: The Treaty of San Francisco comes into effect, restoring Japanese sovereignty and ending its state of war with most of the Allies of World War II.
04-28.text: The Treaty of San Francisco comes into effect, restoring Japanese sovereignty and ending its state of war with most of the Allies of World War II.

04-28.title: Chernobyl disaster
04-28.subtitle: High levels of radiation resulting from the Chernobyl disaster are detected at Forsmark Nuclear Power Plant in Sweden, l...
04-28.text: High levels of radiation resulting from the Chernobyl disaster are detected at Forsmark Nuclear Power Plant in Sweden, leading Soviet authorities to publicly announce the accident.

04-28.title: The Dark Side of the Moon
04-28.subtitle: The Dark Side of the Moon by Pink Floyd, recorded in Abbey Road Studios goes to number one on the US Billboard chart, be...
04-28.text: The Dark Side of the Moon by Pink Floyd, recorded in Abbey Road Studios goes to number one on the US Billboard chart, beginning a record-breaking 741-week chart run.

04-24.title: STS-31
04-24.subtitle: STS-31: The Hubble Space Telescope is launched from the Space Shuttle Discovery.
04-24.text: STS-31: The Hubble Space Telescope is launched from the Space Shuttle Discovery.

04-24.title: Dominican Civil War
04-24.subtitle: Civil war breaks out in the Dominican Republic when Colonel Francisco Caamaño overthrows the triumvirate that had been i...
04-24.text: Civil war breaks out in the Dominican Republic when Colonel Francisco Caamaño overthrows the triumvirate that had been in power since the coup d'état against Juan Bosch.

04-24.title: Suez Crisis
04-24.subtitle: Suez Crisis: The Suez Canal is reopened following the introduction of UNEF peacekeepers to the region.
04-24.text: Suez Crisis: The Suez Canal is reopened following the introduction of UNEF peacekeepers to the region.

04-24.title: Astronaut
04-24.subtitle: Cosmonaut Vladimir Komarov dies in Soyuz 1 when its parachute fails to open.
04-24.text: An astronaut is a person trained, equipped, and deployed by a human spaceflight program to serve as a commander or crew member of a spacecraft. Although generally reserved for professional space travelers, the term is sometimes applied to anyone who travels into space, including scientists, politicians, journalists, and space tourists. In the United States, it is a designated term used by three agencies: NASA, the FAA, and the military.

04-24.title: The Gambia
04-24.subtitle: The Gambia becomes a republic within the Commonwealth of Nations, with Dawda Jawara as its first President.
04-24.text: The Gambia becomes a republic within the Commonwealth of Nations, with Dawda Jawara as its first President.

04-25.title: Cold War
04-25.subtitle: Cold War: American schoolgirl Samantha Smith is invited to visit the Soviet Union by its leader Yuri Andropov after he r...
04-25.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
04-25.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

04-25.title: Carnation Revolution
04-25.subtitle: Carnation Revolution: A leftist military coup in Portugal overthrows the authoritarian-conservative Estado Novo regime.
04-25.text: Carnation Revolution: A leftist military coup in Portugal overthrows the authoritarian-conservative Estado Novo regime.

04-25.title: World War II
04-25.subtitle: World War II: Liberation Day (Italy): The National Liberation Committee for Northern Italy calls for a general uprising ...
04-25.text: World War II: Liberation Day (Italy): The National Liberation Committee for Northern Italy calls for a general uprising against the German occupation and the Italian Social Republic.

04-25.title: Francis Crick
04-25.subtitle: Francis Crick and James Watson publish "Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid" ...
04-25.text: Francis Crick and James Watson publish "Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid" describing the double helix structure of DNA.

04-25.title: Solar cell
04-25.subtitle: The first practical solar cell is publicly demonstrated by Bell Telephone Laboratories.
04-25.text: The first practical solar cell is publicly demonstrated by Bell Telephone Laboratories.

04-29.title: Space Shuttle Challenger
04-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Challenger is launched on STS-51-B.
04-29.text: Space Shuttle Challenger is launched on STS-51-B.

04-29.title: Sikhs
04-29.subtitle: An assembly of Sikhs, known as a Sarbat Khalsa, officially declared independence for a state of Khalistan.
04-29.text: An assembly of Sikhs, known as a Sarbat Khalsa, officially declared independence for a state of Khalistan.

04-29.title: 3D television
04-29.subtitle: The first U.
04-29.text: The first U.S. experimental 3D television broadcast shows an episode of Space Patrol on Los Angeles ABC affiliate KECA-TV.

04-29.title: Aircraft carrier
04-29.subtitle: The United States Navy aircraft carrier USS Enterprise becomes the first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to transit the...
04-29.text: The United States Navy aircraft carrier USS Enterprise becomes the first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to transit the Suez Canal, navigating from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea to relieve the USS Coral Sea.

04-29.title: World War I
04-29.subtitle: World War I: The UK's 6th Indian Division surrenders to Ottoman Forces at the Siege of Kut in one of the largest surrend...
04-29.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

04-30.title: Soviet submarine K-19
04-30.subtitle: K-19, the first Soviet nuclear submarine equipped with nuclear missiles, is commissioned.
04-30.text: K-19, the first Soviet nuclear submarine equipped with nuclear missiles, is commissioned.

04-30.title: 2009 attack on the Dutch royal
04-30.subtitle: Seven civilians and the perpetrator are killed and another ten injured at a Queen's Day parade in Apeldoorn, Netherlands...
04-30.text: Seven civilians and the perpetrator are killed and another ten injured at a Queen's Day parade in Apeldoorn, Netherlands in an attempted assassination on Queen Beatrix.

04-30.title: Eruption of Mount Marapi
04-30.subtitle: Eruption of Mount Marapi: Mount Marapi, a complex volcano on the Indonesian island of Sumatra, erupted.
04-30.text: Eruption of Mount Marapi: Mount Marapi, a complex volcano on the Indonesian island of Sumatra, erupted. Between 80 and 100 people were killed.

04-30.title: Fall of Saigon
04-30.subtitle: Fall of Saigon: Communist forces gain control of Saigon.
04-30.text: Saigon, the capital of South Vietnam, was captured by North Vietnam on 30 April 1975. This caused the collapse of South Vietnam to communism and the evacuation of thousands of civilians and U.S. personnel, and ended the Vietnam War. The aftermath ushered in a transition period under the Provisional Revolutionary Government of the Republic of South Vietnam until the formal reunification in 1976.
04-30.location: End of the Vietnam War, 30 April 1975

04-30.title: J. J. Thomson
04-30.subtitle: J.
04-30.text: J. J. Thomson of the Cavendish Laboratory announces his discovery of the electron as a subatomic particle, over 1,800 times smaller than a proton (in the atomic nucleus), at a lecture at the Royal Institution in London.

04-26.title: 1966 Tashkent earthquake
04-26.subtitle: The magnitude 5.
04-26.text: The magnitude 5.1 Tashkent earthquake affects the largest city in Soviet Central Asia with a maximum MSK intensity of VII (Very strong). Tashkent is mostly destroyed and 15–200 are killed.

04-26.title: NASA
04-26.subtitle: NASA's Ranger 4 spacecraft crashes into the Moon.
04-26.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.
