04-26.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on mission STS-55 to conduct experiments aboard the Spacelab module.
04-26.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

04-26.title: British space programme
04-26.subtitle: The British space programme launches its first satellite, the Ariel 1.
04-26.text: The British space programme launches its first satellite, the Ariel 1.

04-26.title: Jonas Salk
04-26.subtitle: The first clinical trials of Jonas Salk's polio vaccine begin in Fairfax County, Virginia.
04-26.text: The first clinical trials of Jonas Salk's polio vaccine begin in Fairfax County, Virginia.

04-27.title: Saur Revolution
04-27.subtitle: The Saur Revolution begins in Afghanistan, ending the following morning with the murder of Afghan President Mohammed Dao...
04-27.text: The Saur Revolution begins in Afghanistan, ending the following morning with the murder of Afghan President Mohammed Daoud Khan and the establishment of the Democratic Republic of Afghanistan.

04-27.title: South African general election
04-27.subtitle: South African general election: The first democratic general election in South Africa, in which black citizens could vote.
04-27.text: South African general election: The first democratic general election in South Africa, in which black citizens could vote. The Interim Constitution comes into force.

04-27.title: Zambia national football team
04-27.subtitle: Most of the Zambia national football team lose their lives in a plane crash off Libreville, Gabon en route to Dakar, Sen...
04-27.text: The Zambia national football team represents Zambia in men's association football and is governed by the Football Association of Zambia (FAZ). During the 1980s, they were known as the KK 11, after founding president Dr. Kenneth Kaunda ("KK") who ruled Zambia from 1964 to 1991. After the country adopted multiparty politics, the side was nicknamed Chipolopolo which means the "Copper Bullets". The team won an Africa Cup of Nations title in 2012.

04-27.title: Russia
04-27.subtitle: The Russian Federation and 12 other former Soviet republics become members of the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank.
04-27.text: The Russian Federation and 12 other former Soviet republics become members of the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank.

04-27.title: Betty Boothroyd
04-27.subtitle: Betty Boothroyd becomes the first woman to be elected Speaker of the British House of Commons in its 700-year history.
04-27.text: Betty Boothroyd becomes the first woman to be elected Speaker of the British House of Commons in its 700-year history.

05-02.title: First ascent
05-02.subtitle: First ascent of Shishapangma, the fourteenth highest mountain in the world and the lowest of the Eight-thousanders.
05-02.text: First ascent of Shishapangma, the fourteenth highest mountain in the world and the lowest of the Eight-thousanders.

05-02.title: Falklands War
05-02.subtitle: Falklands War: The British nuclear submarine HMS Conqueror sinks the Argentine cruiser ARA General Belgrano.
05-02.text: The Falklands War was a ten-week undeclared war between Argentina and the United Kingdom in 1982 over two British Overseas Territories in the South Atlantic: the Falkland Islands and its territorial dependency, South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands. The conflict began on 2 April 1982, when Argentina invaded and occupied the Falkland Islands, followed by the invasion of South Georgia the next day.

05-02.title: Pastel
05-02.subtitle: A pastel version of The Scream, by Norwegian painter Edvard Munch, sells for $120 million in a New York City auction, se...
05-02.text: A pastel version of The Scream, by Norwegian painter Edvard Munch, sells for $120 million in a New York City auction, setting a new world record for a work of art at auction.

05-02.title: World War II
05-02.subtitle: World War II: Following the coup d'état against Iraq Crown Prince 'Abd al-Ilah earlier that year, the United Kingdom lau...
05-02.text: World War II: Following the coup d'état against Iraq Crown Prince 'Abd al-Ilah earlier that year, the United Kingdom launches the Anglo-Iraqi War to restore him to power.

05-02.title: Croatian War of Independence
05-02.subtitle: During the Croatian War of Independence, the Army of the Republic of Serb Krajina fires cluster bombs at Zagreb, killing...
05-02.text: During the Croatian War of Independence, the Army of the Republic of Serb Krajina fires cluster bombs at Zagreb, killing seven and wounding over 175 civilians.

05-03.title: Semi-trailer
05-03.subtitle: Two men are rescued from a semitrailer that crashed over the side of the Pit River Bridge before it fell into the Sacramento River.
05-03.text: Two men are rescued from a semitrailer that crashed over the side of the Pit River Bridge before it fell into the Sacramento River. Amateur photographer Virginia Schau photographs "Rescue on Pit River Bridge", the first and only winning submission for the Pulitzer Prize for Photography to have been taken by a woman.

05-03.title: Email
05-03.subtitle: The first unsolicited bulk commercial email (which would later become known as "spam") is sent by a Digital Equipment Co...
05-03.text: The first unsolicited bulk commercial email (which would later become known as "spam") is sent by a Digital Equipment Corporation marketing representative to every ARPANET address on the west coast of the United States.

05-03.title: Geocaching
05-03.subtitle: The sport of geocaching begins, with the first cache placed and the coordinates from a GPS posted on Usenet.
05-03.text: The sport of geocaching begins, with the first cache placed and the coordinates from a GPS posted on Usenet.

05-03.title: Oklahoma City
05-03.subtitle: The southwestern portion of Oklahoma City is devastated by an F5 tornado, killing forty-five people, injuring 665, and causing $1 billion in damage.
05-03.text: The southwestern portion of Oklahoma City is devastated by an F5 tornado, killing forty-five people, injuring 665, and causing $1 billion in damage. The tornado is one of 66 from the 1999 Oklahoma tornado outbreak. This tornado also produces the highest wind speed ever recorded, measured at 484 ± 32 kilometres per hour (301 ± 20 mph). In meteorology, the term "May 3" is synonymous with the F5 tornado.

05-03.title: World War II
05-03.subtitle: World War II: Japanese naval troops invade Tulagi Island in the Solomon Islands during the first part of Operation Mo th...
05-03.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

05-04.title: PEPCON disaster
05-04.subtitle: The PEPCON disaster rocks Henderson, Nevada, as tons of Space Shuttle fuel detonate during a fire.
05-04.text: The PEPCON disaster rocks Henderson, Nevada, as tons of Space Shuttle fuel detonate during a fire.

05-04.title: Prime minister
05-04.subtitle: Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin and PLO leader Yasser Arafat sign a peace accord, granting self-rule in the Gaza Strip and Jericho.
05-04.text: Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin and PLO leader Yasser Arafat sign a peace accord, granting self-rule in the Gaza Strip and Jericho.

05-04.title: Don't Make a Wave Committee
05-04.subtitle: The Don't Make A Wave Committee, a fledgling environmental organization founded in Canada in 1971, officially changes it...
05-04.text: The Don't Make A Wave Committee, a fledgling environmental organization founded in Canada in 1971, officially changes its name to "Greenpeace Foundation".

05-04.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
05-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on mission STS-30 to deploy the Venus-bound Magellan space probe.
05-04.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

05-04.title: American civil rights movement
05-04.subtitle: American civil rights movement: The "Freedom Riders" begin a bus trip through the South.
05-04.text: American civil rights movement: The "Freedom Riders" begin a bus trip through the South.

05-06.title: Devolution
05-06.subtitle: The first elections to the devolved Scottish Parliament and Welsh Assembly are held.
05-06.text: The first elections to the devolved Scottish Parliament and Welsh Assembly are held.

05-06.title: Coronation of Charles III and Camilla
05-06.subtitle: The coronation of Charles III and Camilla as King and Queen of the United Kingdom and the other Commonwealth realms is h...
05-06.text: The coronation of Charles III and Camilla as King and Queen of the United Kingdom and the other Commonwealth realms is held in Westminster Abbey, London.

05-06.title: Syria
05-06.subtitle: During a trip to Syria, Pope John Paul II becomes the first pope to enter a mosque.
05-06.text: During a trip to Syria, Pope John Paul II becomes the first pope to enter a mosque.

05-06.title: Pim Fortuyn
05-06.subtitle: Dutch politician Pim Fortuyn is assassinated following a radio-interview at the Mediapark in Hilversum.
05-06.text: Dutch politician Pim Fortuyn is assassinated following a radio-interview at the Mediapark in Hilversum.

05-06.title: Wedding of Princess Margaret and Antony
05-06.subtitle: More than 20 million viewers watch the first televised royal wedding when Princess Margaret marries Antony Armstrong-Jones at Westminster Abbey.
05-06.text: The wedding of Princess Margaret and Antony Armstrong-Jones took place on Friday, 6 May 1960 at Westminster Abbey in London. Princess Margaret was the younger sister of Queen Elizabeth II, while Antony Armstrong-Jones was a noted society photographer.

05-05.title: Bonn–Paris conventions
05-05.subtitle: The General Treaty, by which France, Britain and the United States recognize the sovereignty of West Germany, comes into effect.
05-05.text: The General Treaty, by which France, Britain and the United States recognize the sovereignty of West Germany, comes into effect.

05-05.title: Project Mercury
05-05.subtitle: Project Mercury: Alan Shepard becomes the first American to travel into outer space, on a sub-orbital flight.
05-05.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

05-05.title: World Health Organization
05-05.subtitle: The World Health Organization declares the end of the COVID-19 pandemic as a global health emergency.
05-05.text: The World Health Organization declares the end of the COVID-19 pandemic as a global health emergency.

05-05.title: Secretariat
05-05.subtitle: Secretariat wins the 1973 Kentucky Derby in 1:59.
05-05.text: Secretariat wins the 1973 Kentucky Derby in 1:59.4, an as-yet-unbeaten record.

05-05.title: Sudan
05-05.subtitle: The government of Sudan signs an accord with the Sudan Liberation Army.
05-05.text: The government of Sudan signs an accord with the Sudan Liberation Army.

05-09.title: Florida
05-09.subtitle: In Florida, United States, Liberian freighter MV Summit Venture collides with the Sunshine Skyway Bridge over Tampa Bay,...
05-09.text: In Florida, United States, Liberian freighter MV Summit Venture collides with the Sunshine Skyway Bridge over Tampa Bay, making a 430-meter (1,400 ft) section of the southbound span collapse. Thirty-five people in six cars and a Greyhound bus fall 46 metres (150 ft) into the water and die.

05-09.title: Food and Drug Administration
05-09.subtitle: The Food and Drug Administration announces it will approve birth control as an additional indication for Searle's Enovid...
05-09.text: The Food and Drug Administration announces it will approve birth control as an additional indication for Searle's Enovid, making Enovid the world's first approved oral contraceptive pill.

05-09.title: Russo-Ukrainian War
05-09.subtitle: Russo-Ukrainian War: United States President Joe Biden signs the 2022 Lend-Lease Act into law, a rebooted World War II-e...
05-09.text: Russo-Ukrainian War: United States President Joe Biden signs the 2022 Lend-Lease Act into law, a rebooted World War II-era policy expediting American equipment to Ukraine and other Eastern European countries.

05-09.title: Armenia
05-09.subtitle: Armenian forces capture Shusha, marking a major turning point in the First Nagorno-Karabakh War.
05-09.text: Armenian forces capture Shusha, marking a major turning point in the First Nagorno-Karabakh War.

05-09.title: Carlos Lamarca
05-09.subtitle: Carlos Lamarca leads the first urban guerrilla action against the military dictatorship of Brazil in São Paulo, by robbing two banks.
05-09.text: Carlos Lamarca leads the first urban guerrilla action against the military dictatorship of Brazil in São Paulo, by robbing two banks.

05-08.title: The New Revolution
05-08.subtitle: The rollercoaster The New Revolution, the first steel coaster with a vertical loop, opens at Six Flags Magic Mountain.
05-08.text: The rollercoaster The New Revolution, the first steel coaster with a vertical loop, opens at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

05-08.title: Thames Barrier
05-08.subtitle: The Thames Barrier is officially opened, preventing the floodplain of most of Greater London from being flooded except under extreme circumstances.
05-08.text: The Thames Barrier is a retractable barrier system built to protect the floodplain of most of Greater London from exceptionally high tides and storm surges moving up from the North Sea. It has been operational since 1982. When needed, it is closed (raised) during high tide; at low tide, it can be opened to restore the river's flow towards the sea.
05-08.location: Flood defence system for London, England

05-08.title: Special Air Service
05-08.subtitle: The SAS kills eight Provisional Irish Republican Army volunteers and a civilian during an ambush in Loughgall, Northern Ireland.
05-08.text: The SAS kills eight Provisional Irish Republican Army volunteers and a civilian during an ambush in Loughgall, Northern Ireland.

05-08.title: Mount Everest
05-08.subtitle: The first ascent of Mount Everest without supplemental oxygen, by Reinhold Messner and Peter Habeler.
05-08.text: The first ascent of Mount Everest without supplemental oxygen, by Reinhold Messner and Peter Habeler.

05-08.title: Soviet Union
05-08.subtitle: The Soviet Union announces a boycott upon the Summer Olympics at Los Angeles, later joined by 14 other countries.
05-08.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

05-10.title: Elizabeth II
05-10.subtitle: Queen Elizabeth II misses the State Opening of Parliament for the first time in 59 years.
05-10.text: Elizabeth II was Queen of the United Kingdom and other Commonwealth realms from 6 February 1952 until her death in 2022. She was queen regnant of 32 sovereign states during her lifetime and was the monarch of 15 realms at her death. Her reign of 70 years and 214 days is the longest of any British monarch, the second-longest of any sovereign state, and the longest of any queen regnant in history.
05-10.location: Queen of the United Kingdom from 1952 to 2022

05-10.title: Sony
05-10.subtitle: Sony introduces the Betamax videocassette recorder.
05-10.text: Sony introduces the Betamax videocassette recorder.

05-10.title: 10 May 2012 Damascus bombings
05-10.subtitle: The Damascus bombings are carried out using a pair of car bombs detonated by suicide bombers outside a military intellig...
05-10.text: The 10 May 2012 Damascus bombings were carried out using a pair of car bombs allegedly detonated by suicide bombers outside a military intelligence complex in Damascus, Syria. Combined, the perpetrators detonated more than 1,000 kilograms (2,200 lb) of explosives, tearing the facade off of a 10-story building. With 55 people confirmed dead and almost 400 others injured, the attack was the deadliest bombing until then in the Syrian Civil War, though later outpaced by other events.
05-10.location: Car bombing outside a military intelligence complex

05-10.title: Moment magnitude scale
05-10.subtitle: The 7.
05-10.text: The 7.3 Mw Qayen earthquake strikes Iran's Khorasan Province killing 1,567 people.

05-10.title: Nelson Mandela
05-10.subtitle: Nelson Mandela is inaugurated as South Africa's first black president.
05-10.text: Nelson Mandela is inaugurated as South Africa's first black president.

05-11.title: David Cameron
05-11.subtitle: David Cameron takes office as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom as the Conservatives and Liberal Democrats form the c...
05-11.text: David Cameron takes office as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom as the Conservatives and Liberal Democrats form the country's first coalition government since the Second World War.

05-11.title: Eurovision Song Contest 2024
05-11.subtitle: The 68th edition of the Eurovision Song Contest is held in Malmö, Sweden.
05-11.text: The 68th edition of the Eurovision Song Contest is held in Malmö, Sweden. Nemo from Switzerland wins with their song "The Code", making them the contest's first non-binary winner.

05-11.title: Deep Blue
05-11.subtitle: Deep Blue, a chess-playing supercomputer, defeats Garry Kasparov in the last game of the rematch, becoming the first com...
05-11.text: Deep Blue, a chess-playing supercomputer, defeats Garry Kasparov in the last game of the rematch, becoming the first computer to beat a world-champion chess player in a classic match format.

05-11.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
05-11.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on the final mission to service the Hubble Space Telescope.
05-11.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

05-11.title: Tatmadaw
05-11.subtitle: The Burmese military executes at least 37 villagers during the Mon Taing Pin massacre in Sagaing, Myanmar.
05-11.text: The Burmese military executes at least 37 villagers during the Mon Taing Pin massacre in Sagaing, Myanmar.

05-13.title: Li Hongzhi
05-13.subtitle: Li Hongzhi gives the first public lecture on Falun Gong in Changchun, People's Republic of China.
05-13.text: Li Hongzhi gives the first public lecture on Falun Gong in Changchun, People's Republic of China.

05-13.title: Kosovo War
05-13.subtitle: Kosovo War: NATO bombs the village of Koriša, killing at least 87 people.
05-13.text: Kosovo War: NATO bombs the village of Koriša, killing at least 87 people.

05-13.title: Rajya Sabha
05-13.subtitle: The Rajya Sabha, the upper house of the Parliament of India, holds its first sitting.
05-13.text: The Rajya Sabha, the upper house of the Parliament of India, holds its first sitting.

05-13.title: Ben Carlin
05-13.subtitle: Ben Carlin becomes the first (and only) person to circumnavigate the world by amphibious vehicle, having travelled over ...
05-13.text: Ben Carlin becomes the first (and only) person to circumnavigate the world by amphibious vehicle, having travelled over 17,000 kilometres (11,000 mi) by sea and 62,000 kilometres (39,000 mi) by land during a ten-year journey.

05-13.title: 1950 British Grand Prix
05-13.subtitle: The inaugural Formula One World Championship race takes place at Silverstone Circuit.
05-13.text: The 1950 British Grand Prix, formally known as The Royal Automobile Club Grand Prix d'Europe Incorporating The British Grand Prix, was a Formula One motor race held on 13 May 1950 at the Silverstone Circuit in Silverstone, England. It was the first World Championship Formula One race, as well as the fifth British Grand Prix, and the third to be held at Silverstone after motor racing resumed after World War II.
05-13.location: Formula One motor race held in 1950

05-14.title: Skylab
05-14.subtitle: Skylab, the United States' first space station, is launched.
05-14.text: Skylab, the United States' first space station, is launched.

05-14.title: Civil rights movement
05-14.subtitle: Civil rights movement: A white mob twice attacks a Freedom Riders bus near Anniston, Alabama, before fire-bombing the bu...
05-14.text: Civil rights movement: A white mob twice attacks a Freedom Riders bus near Anniston, Alabama, before fire-bombing the bus and attacking the civil rights protesters who flee the burning vehicle.

05-14.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
05-14.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on the STS-132 mission to deliver the first shuttle-launched Russian ISS component — Rassvet.
05-14.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

05-14.title: Cold War
05-14.subtitle: Cold War: Eight Communist bloc countries, including the Soviet Union, sign a mutual defense treaty called the Warsaw Pact.
05-14.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
05-14.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

05-14.title: Zhurong
05-14.subtitle: China successfully lands Zhurong, the country's first Mars rover.
05-14.text: China successfully lands Zhurong, the country's first Mars rover.

05-15.title: Project Mercury
05-15.subtitle: Project Mercury: The launch of the final Mercury mission, Mercury-Atlas 9 with astronaut Gordon Cooper on board.
05-15.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

05-15.title: Ma'alot massacre
05-15.subtitle: Ma'alot massacre: Members of the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine attack and take hostages at an Israeli...
05-15.text: Ma'alot massacre: Members of the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine attack and take hostages at an Israeli school; a total of 31 people are killed, including 22 schoolchildren.

05-15.title: Malden Island
05-15.subtitle: At Malden Island in the Pacific Ocean, Britain tests its first hydrogen bomb in Operation Grapple.
05-15.text: At Malden Island in the Pacific Ocean, Britain tests its first hydrogen bomb in Operation Grapple.

05-15.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
05-15.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-84 to dock with the Russian space station Mir.
05-15.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

05-15.title: Same-sex marriage in California
05-15.subtitle: California becomes the second U.
05-15.text: California becomes the second U.S. state after Massachusetts in 2004 to legalize same-sex marriage after the state's own Supreme Court rules a previous ban unconstitutional.

05-17.title: United States Department of Energy
05-17.subtitle: The U.
05-17.text: The U.S. Department of Energy declassifies documents showing world's largest mercury pollution event in Oak Ridge, Tennessee (ultimately found to be 4.2 million pounds [1.9 kt]), in response to the Appalachian Observer's Freedom of Information Act request.

05-17.title: Six-Day War
05-17.subtitle: Six-Day War: President Gamal Abdel Nasser of Egypt demands dismantling of the peace-keeping UN Emergency Force in Egypt.
05-17.text: Six-Day War: President Gamal Abdel Nasser of Egypt demands dismantling of the peace-keeping UN Emergency Force in Egypt.

05-17.title: Nolan Bushnell
05-17.subtitle: Nolan Bushnell opened the first Chuck E.
05-17.text: Nolan Kay Bushnell is an American businessman and electrical engineer. He established Atari, Inc., and the Chuck E. Cheese chain. He has been inducted into the Video Game Hall of Fame and the Consumer Electronics Association Hall of Fame, received the BAFTA Fellowship and the Nations Restaurant News "Innovator of the Year" award and was named one of Newsweek's "50 Men Who Changed America".

05-17.title: Laurent-Désiré Kabila
05-17.subtitle: Troops of Laurent-Désiré Kabila march into Kinshasa.
05-17.text: Troops of Laurent-Désiré Kabila march into Kinshasa. Zaire is officially renamed Democratic Republic of the Congo.

05-17.title: North Korea
05-17.subtitle: Trains from North and South Korea cross the 38th Parallel in a test-run agreed by both governments.
05-17.text: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia. It constitutes the northern half of the Korean Peninsula and borders China and Russia to the north at the Yalu (Amnok) and Tumen rivers, and South Korea to the south at the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ). The country's western border is formed by the Yellow Sea, while its eastern border is defined by the Sea of Japan.

05-18.title: Hubble Space Telescope
05-18.subtitle: A second photo from the Hubble Space Telescope confirms that Pluto has two additional moons, Nix and Hydra.
05-18.text: A second photo from the Hubble Space Telescope confirms that Pluto has two additional moons, Nix and Hydra.

05-18.title: Nørrebro
05-18.subtitle: Riots in Nørrebro, Copenhagen, caused by the approval of the four Danish exceptions in the Maastricht Treaty referendum.
05-18.text: Riots in Nørrebro, Copenhagen, caused by the approval of the four Danish exceptions in the Maastricht Treaty referendum. Police open fire against civilians for the first time since World War II and injure 11 demonstrators.

05-18.title: Operation Passage to Freedom
05-18.subtitle: Operation Passage to Freedom, the evacuation of 310,000 Vietnamese civilians, soldiers and non-Vietnamese members of the...
05-18.text: Operation Passage to Freedom, the evacuation of 310,000 Vietnamese civilians, soldiers and non-Vietnamese members of the French Army from communist North Vietnam to South Vietnam following the end of the First Indochina War, ends.

05-18.title: Nuclear weapons testing
05-18.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: Under project Smiling Buddha, India successfully detonates its first nuclear weapon becoming the sixth nation to do so.
05-18.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
05-18.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

05-18.title: Somalia
05-18.subtitle: Northern Somalia declares independence from the rest of Somalia as the Republic of Somaliland.
05-18.text: Northern Somalia declares independence from the rest of Somalia as the Republic of Somaliland.

05-16.title: STS-134
05-16.subtitle: STS-134 (ISS assembly flight ULF6), launched from the Kennedy Space Center on the 25th and final flight for Space Shuttle Endeavour.
05-16.text: STS-134 (ISS assembly flight ULF6), launched from the Kennedy Space Center on the 25th and final flight for Space Shuttle Endeavour.

05-16.title: Venera program
05-16.subtitle: Venera program: Venera 5, a Soviet space probe, lands on Venus.
05-16.text: Venera program: Venera 5, a Soviet space probe, lands on Venus.

05-16.title: Park Chung Hee
05-16.subtitle: Park Chung Hee leads a coup d'état to overthrow the Second Republic of South Korea.
05-16.text: Park Chung Hee leads a coup d'état to overthrow the Second Republic of South Korea.

05-16.title: Theodore Maiman
05-16.subtitle: Theodore Maiman operates the first optical laser (a ruby laser), at Hughes Research Laboratories in Malibu, California.
05-16.text: Theodore Maiman operates the first optical laser (a ruby laser), at Hughes Research Laboratories in Malibu, California.

05-16.title: Kuwait
05-16.subtitle: Kuwait permits women's suffrage in a 35–23 National Assembly vote.
05-16.text: Kuwait permits women's suffrage in a 35–23 National Assembly vote.

05-19.title: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
05-19.subtitle: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk lands at Samsun on the Anatolian Black Sea coast, initiating what is later termed the Turkish War of Independence.
05-19.text: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk lands at Samsun on the Anatolian Black Sea coast, initiating what is later termed the Turkish War of Independence.

05-19.title: Space Shuttle program
05-19.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on mission STS-101 to resupply the International Space Station.
05-19.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

05-19.title: Royal Thai Armed Forces
05-19.subtitle: The Royal Thai Armed Forces concludes its crackdown on protests by forcing the surrender of United Front for Democracy Against Dictatorship leaders.
05-19.text: The Royal Thai Armed Forces concludes its crackdown on protests by forcing the surrender of United Front for Democracy Against Dictatorship leaders.

05-19.title: Mars probe program
05-19.subtitle: Mars probe program: Mars 2 is launched by the Soviet Union.
05-19.text: Mars probe program: Mars 2 is launched by the Soviet Union.

05-19.title: Space Shuttle program
05-19.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on mission STS-77.
05-19.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

05-20.title: HIV
05-20.subtitle: First publications of the discovery of the HIV virus that causes AIDS in the journal Science by a team of French scienti...
05-20.text: First publications of the discovery of the HIV virus that causes AIDS in the journal Science by a team of French scientists including Françoise Barré-Sinoussi, Jean-Claude Chermann, and Luc Montagnier.

05-20.title: Popular Movement of the Revolution
05-20.subtitle: The Popular Movement of the Revolution political party is established in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
05-20.text: The Popular Movement of the Revolution political party is established in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

05-20.title: Singapore
05-20.subtitle: The government of Singapore authorised the controversial execution of convicted murderer Kho Jabing for the murder of a ...
05-20.text: The government of Singapore authorised the controversial execution of convicted murderer Kho Jabing for the murder of a Chinese construction worker despite the international pleas for clemency, notably from Amnesty International and the United Nations.

05-20.title: Democracy
05-20.subtitle: The Chinese authorities declare martial law in the face of pro-democracy demonstrations, setting the scene for the Tiananmen Square massacre.
05-20.text: The Chinese authorities declare martial law in the face of pro-democracy demonstrations, setting the scene for the Tiananmen Square massacre.

05-20.title: Civil rights
05-20.subtitle: Civil rights: The Supreme Court of the United States rules in Romer v.
05-20.text: Civil rights: The Supreme Court of the United States rules in Romer v. Evans against a law that would have prevented any city, town or county in the state of Colorado from taking any legislative, executive, or judicial action to protect the rights of gays and lesbians.

05-22.title: 1960 Valdivia earthquake
05-22.subtitle: The Great Chilean earthquake, measuring 9.
05-22.text: The Great Chilean earthquake, measuring 9.5 on the moment magnitude scale, hits southern Chile, becoming the most powerful earthquake ever recorded.

05-22.title: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2
05-22.subtitle: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2 launches a Dragon capsule on a Falcon 9 rocket in the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.
05-22.text: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2 launches a Dragon capsule on a Falcon 9 rocket in the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.

05-22.title: Sri Lankan Constitution of 1972
05-22.subtitle: Ceylon adopts a new constitution, becoming a republic and changing its name to Sri Lanka.
05-22.text: The Sri Lankan Constitution of 1972 was a constitution of Sri Lanka, replaced by the 1978 constitution currently in force. It was Sri Lanka's first republican constitution, and its second since independence in 1948. The constitution changed the country's name from Ceylon to Sri Lanka, and established it as an independent republic. The country was officially designated as the "Republic of Sri Lanka," leading to the constitution being known as the 1972 Republican Constitution.
05-22.location: Fundamental law of Sri Lanka from 1972 to 1978

05-22.title: Apollo 10
05-22.subtitle: Apollo 10's Lunar Module flies within 8.
05-22.text: Apollo 10's Lunar Module flies within 8.4 nautical miles (16 km) of the Moon's surface.

05-22.title: 1987 Rugby World Cup
05-22.subtitle: First ever Rugby World Cup kicks off with New Zealand playing Italy at Eden Park in Auckland, New Zealand.
05-22.text: First ever Rugby World Cup kicks off with New Zealand playing Italy at Eden Park in Auckland, New Zealand.

05-21.title: JAXA
05-21.subtitle: JAXA, the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency, launches the solar-sail spacecraft IKAROS aboard an H-IIA rocket.
05-21.text: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)  is the Japanese national air and space agency. Through the merger of three previously independent organizations, JAXA was formed on 1 October 2003. JAXA is responsible for research, technology development and launch of satellites into orbit, and is involved in many more advanced missions such as asteroid exploration and possible human exploration of the Moon. Its motto is One JAXA and its corporate slogan is Explore to Realize.
05-21.location: Japan's national air and space agency

05-21.title: 2003 Boumerdès earthquake
05-21.subtitle: The 6.
05-21.text: The 6.8 Mw  Boumerdès earthquake shakes northern Algeria with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme). More than 2,200 people were killed and a moderate tsunami sank boats at the Balearic Islands.

05-21.title: White Night riots
05-21.subtitle: White Night riots in San Francisco following the manslaughter conviction of Dan White for the assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey Milk.
05-21.text: White Night riots in San Francisco following the manslaughter conviction of Dan White for the assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey Milk.

05-21.title: American civil rights movement
05-21.subtitle: American civil rights movement: Alabama Governor John Malcolm Patterson declares martial law in an attempt to restore or...
05-21.text: American civil rights movement: Alabama Governor John Malcolm Patterson declares martial law in an attempt to restore order after race riots break out.

05-21.title: Republic of Montenegro
05-21.subtitle: The Republic of Montenegro holds a referendum proposing independence from the State Union of Serbia and Montenegro; 55% ...
05-21.text: The Republic of Montenegro was a constituent federated state of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and then Serbia and Montenegro between 1992 and 2006. The declaration of independence of Montenegro in 2006 ended the ex-Yugoslav state. After the collapse of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (SFRY), the remaining republics of Montenegro and Serbia agreed to the formation of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (FRY) which officially abandoned communism and endorsed liberal institutions.
05-21.location: Federal unit of Yugoslavia/Serbia & Montenegro between 1992 and 2006

05-23.title: Tsunami
05-23.subtitle: A tsunami caused by an earthquake in Chile the previous day kills 61 people in Hilo, Hawaii.
05-23.text: A tsunami caused by an earthquake in Chile the previous day kills 61 people in Hilo, Hawaii.

05-23.title: Interstate 5 in Washington
05-23.subtitle: A freeway bridge carrying Interstate 5 over the Skagit River collapses in Mount Vernon, Washington.
05-23.text: A freeway bridge carrying Interstate 5 over the Skagit River collapses in Mount Vernon, Washington.

05-23.title: International Court of Justice
05-23.subtitle: The International Court of Justice (ICJ) awards Middle Rocks to Malaysia and Pedra Branca (Pulau Batu Puteh) to Singapor...
05-23.text: The International Court of Justice (ICJ) awards Middle Rocks to Malaysia and Pedra Branca (Pulau Batu Puteh) to Singapore, ending a 29-year territorial dispute between the two countries.

05-23.title: Aviogenex Flight 130
05-23.subtitle: Seventy-eight people are killed when Aviogenex Flight 130 crashes on approach to Rijeka Airport in present-day Rijeka, C...
05-23.text: Seventy-eight people are killed when Aviogenex Flight 130 crashes on approach to Rijeka Airport in present-day Rijeka, Croatia (then the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia).

05-23.title: Abdul Hamid II
05-23.subtitle: Abdul Hamid II, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, publicly announces the creation of the Ullah millet for the Aromanians of ...
05-23.text: Abdul Hamid II, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, publicly announces the creation of the Ullah millet for the Aromanians of the empire, which had been established one day earlier. For this reason, the Aromanian National Day is usually celebrated on May 23, although some do so on May 22 instead.

05-26.title: Zviad Gamsakhurdia
05-26.subtitle: Zviad Gamsakhurdia becomes the first elected President of the Republic of Georgia in the post-Soviet era.
05-26.text: Zviad Gamsakhurdia becomes the first elected President of the Republic of Georgia in the post-Soviet era.

05-26.title: Apollo program
05-26.subtitle: Apollo program: Apollo 10 returns to Earth after a successful eight-day test of all the components needed for the forthc...
05-26.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

05-26.title: Românul de la Pind
05-26.subtitle: Românul de la Pind, the longest-running newspaper by and about Aromanians until World War II, is founded.
05-26.text: Românul de la Pind, the longest-running newspaper by and about Aromanians until World War II, is founded.

05-26.title: 1983 Sea of Japan earthquake
05-26.subtitle: The 7.
05-26.text: The 7.8 Mw  Sea of Japan earthquake shakes northern Honshu with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). A destructive tsunami is generated that leaves about 100 people dead.

05-26.title: Cold War
05-26.subtitle: Cold War: The United States and the Soviet Union sign the Anti-Ballistic Missile Treaty.
05-26.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
05-26.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

05-07.title: Integrated circuit
05-07.subtitle: The concept of the integrated circuit, the basis for all modern computers, is first published by Geoffrey Dummer.
05-07.text: The concept of the integrated circuit, the basis for all modern computers, is first published by Geoffrey Dummer.

05-07.title: Treaty of Moscow
05-07.subtitle: Treaty of Moscow: Soviet Russia recognizes the independence of the Democratic Republic of Georgia only to invade the country six months later.
05-07.text: Treaty of Moscow: Soviet Russia recognizes the independence of the Democratic Republic of Georgia only to invade the country six months later.

05-07.title: Cold War
05-07.subtitle: Cold War: U-2 Crisis of 1960: Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev announces that his nation is holding American U-2 pilot Gary Powers.
05-07.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
05-07.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

05-07.title: World War I
05-07.subtitle: World War I: German submarine U-20 sinks RMS Lusitania, killing 1,199 people, including 128 Americans.
05-07.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

05-07.title: Space Shuttle program
05-07.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: The Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on its first mission, STS-49.
05-07.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

05-25.title: SpaceX Dragon 1
05-25.subtitle: The SpaceX Dragon 1 becomes the first commercial spacecraft to successfully rendezvous and berth with the International Space Station.
05-25.text: The SpaceX Dragon 1 becomes the first commercial spacecraft to successfully rendezvous and berth with the International Space Station.

05-25.title: NASA
05-25.subtitle: NASA's Phoenix lander touches down in the Green Valley region of Mars to search for environments suitable for water and microbial life.
05-25.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

05-25.title: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga
05-25.subtitle: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga: On the British Kangchenjunga expedition led by Charles Evans, Joe Brown and George ...
05-25.text: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga: On the British Kangchenjunga expedition led by Charles Evans, Joe Brown and George Band reach the summit of the third-highest mountain in the world (8,586 meters); Norman Hardie and Tony Streather join them the following day.

05-25.title: Nuclear weapons testing
05-25.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: At the Nevada Test Site, the United States conducts its first and only nuclear artillery test.
05-25.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
05-25.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

05-25.title: Apollo program
05-25.subtitle: Apollo program: U.
05-25.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

05-24.title: 1960 Valdivia earthquake
05-24.subtitle: Following the 1960 Valdivia earthquake, the largest ever recorded earthquake, Cordón Caulle begins to erupt.
05-24.text: Following the 1960 Valdivia earthquake, the largest ever recorded earthquake, Cordón Caulle begins to erupt.

05-24.title: Liberation of Khorramshahr
05-24.subtitle: Liberation of Khorramshahr: Iranians recapture of the port city of Khorramshahr from the Iraqis during the Iran–Iraq War.
05-24.text: Liberation of Khorramshahr: Iranians recapture of the port city of Khorramshahr from the Iraqis during the Iran–Iraq War.

05-24.title: Soviet Union
05-24.subtitle: Acting on the orders of Soviet leader Joseph Stalin, NKVD agent Iosif Grigulevich orchestrates an unsuccessful assassina...
05-24.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

05-24.title: Project Mercury
05-24.subtitle: Project Mercury: American astronaut Scott Carpenter orbits the Earth three times in the Aurora 7 space capsule.
05-24.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

05-24.title: American civil rights movement
05-24.subtitle: American civil rights movement: Freedom Riders are arrested in Jackson, Mississippi, for "disturbing the peace" after disembarking from their bus.
05-24.text: American civil rights movement: Freedom Riders are arrested in Jackson, Mississippi, for "disturbing the peace" after disembarking from their bus.

05-27.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-27.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-96, the first shuttle mission to dock with the International Space Station.
05-27.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-96, the first shuttle mission to dock with the International Space Station.

05-27.title: Vietnam War
05-27.subtitle: Vietnam War: American warships begin the first bombardment of National Liberation Front targets within South Vietnam.
05-27.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
05-27.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

05-27.title: Somaliland War of Independence
05-27.subtitle: Somaliland War of Independence: The Somali National Movement launches a major offensive against Somali government forces...
05-27.text: The Somaliland War of Independence was a rebellion waged by the Somali National Movement (SNM) against the ruling military junta in Somalia led by General Siad Barre lasting from its founding on 6 April 1981 and ended on 18 May 1991 when the SNM declared what was then northern Somalia independent as the Republic of Somaliland. The conflict served as the main theater of the larger Somali Rebellion that started in 1978.
05-27.location: 1981–1991 conflict part of Somali Civil War

05-27.title: World War II
05-27.subtitle: World War II: In the Le Paradis massacre, 99 soldiers from a Royal Norfolk Regiment unit are shot after surrendering to German troops; two survive.
05-27.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

05-27.title: Barack Obama
05-27.subtitle: Barack Obama is the first president of the United States to visit Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park and meet Hibakusha.
05-27.text: Barack Hussein Obama II is an American politician who served as the 44th president of the United States from 2009 to 2017. A member of the Democratic Party, he was the first African American president. Obama previously served as a U.S. senator representing Illinois from 2005 to 2008 and as an Illinois state senator from 1997 to 2004.
05-27.location: President of the United States from 2009 to 2017

05-28.title: Palestine Liberation Organization
05-28.subtitle: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) is founded, with Yasser Arafat elected as its first leader.
05-28.text: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) is founded, with Yasser Arafat elected as its first leader.

05-28.title: Harambe
05-28.subtitle: Harambe, a gorilla, is shot to death after grabbing a three-year-old boy in his enclosure at the Cincinnati Zoo and Bota...
05-28.text: Harambe, a gorilla, is shot to death after grabbing a three-year-old boy in his enclosure at the Cincinnati Zoo and Botanical Garden, resulting in widespread criticism and sparking various internet memes.

05-28.title: Nepal
05-28.subtitle: The first meeting of the Constituent Assembly of Nepal formally declares Nepal a republic, ending the 240-year reign of the Shah dynasty.
05-28.text: The first meeting of the Constituent Assembly of Nepal formally declares Nepal a republic, ending the 240-year reign of the Shah dynasty.

05-28.title: Addis Ababa
05-28.subtitle: The capital city of Addis Ababa falls to the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front, ending both the Derg reg...
05-28.text: The capital city of Addis Ababa falls to the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front, ending both the Derg regime in Ethiopia and the Ethiopian Civil War.

05-28.title: 28 May 1926 coup d'état
05-28.subtitle: The 28 May 1926 coup d'état: Ditadura Nacional is established in Portugal to suppress the unrest of the First Republic.
05-28.text: The 28 May 1926 coup d'état: Ditadura Nacional is established in Portugal to suppress the unrest of the First Republic.

05-31.title: Uniform Monday Holiday Act
05-31.subtitle: In accordance with the Uniform Monday Holiday Act passed by the U.
05-31.text: In accordance with the Uniform Monday Holiday Act passed by the U.S. Congress in 1968, observation of Memorial Day occurs on the last Monday in May for the first time, rather than on the traditional Memorial Day of May 30.

05-31.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-31.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-124 carrying the second portion of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.
05-31.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-124 carrying the second portion of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.

05-31.title: South African Constitution of 1961
05-31.subtitle: The South African Constitution of 1961 becomes effective, thus creating the Republic of South Africa, which remains outs...
05-31.text: The South African Constitution of 1961 becomes effective, thus creating the Republic of South Africa, which remains outside the Commonwealth of Nations until 1 June 1994, when South Africa is returned to Commonwealth membership.

05-31.title: Shayetet 13
05-31.subtitle: Israeli Shayetet 13 commandos boarded the Gaza Freedom Flotilla while still in international waters trying to break the ...
05-31.text: Israeli Shayetet 13 commandos boarded the Gaza Freedom Flotilla while still in international waters trying to break the ongoing blockade of the Gaza Strip; nine Turkish citizens on the flotilla were killed in the ensuing violent affray.

05-31.title: Bicesse Accords
05-31.subtitle: Bicesse Accords in Angola lay out a transition to multi-party democracy under the supervision of the United Nations' UNAVEM II peacekeeping mission.
05-31.text: The Bicesse Accords, also known as the Estoril Accords, laid out a transition to multi-party democracy in Angola under the supervision of the United Nations' UNAVEM II mission. President José Eduardo dos Santos of the MPLA and Jonas Savimbi of UNITA signed the accord in Lisbon, Portugal on May 31, 1991. UNITA rejected the official results of the 1992 presidential election and renewed their guerrilla war.
05-31.location: 1991 agreement to transition to democracy in Angola

05-30.title: Nuclear Testing
05-30.subtitle: Nuclear Testing: Pakistan conducts an underground test in the Kharan Desert.
05-30.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
05-30.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

05-30.title: Donald Trump
05-30.subtitle: Donald Trump is convicted of falsifying business records in his New York trial, the first time a former President of the...
05-30.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
05-30.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

05-30.title: Crew Dragon Demo-2
05-30.subtitle: The Crew Dragon Demo-2 launches from the Kennedy Space Center, becoming the first crewed orbital spacecraft to launch fr...
05-30.text: The Crew Dragon Demo-2 launches from the Kennedy Space Center, becoming the first crewed orbital spacecraft to launch from the United States since 2011 and the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.

05-30.title: Mariner program
05-30.subtitle: Mariner program: Mariner 9 is launched to map 70% of the surface, and to study temporal changes in the atmosphere and surface, of Mars.
05-30.text: Mariner program: Mariner 9 is launched to map 70% of the surface, and to study temporal changes in the atmosphere and surface, of Mars.

05-30.title: Nigeria
05-30.subtitle: The Nigerian Eastern Region declares independence as the Republic of Biafra, sparking a civil war.
05-30.text: The Nigerian Eastern Region declares independence as the Republic of Biafra, sparking a civil war.

06-01.title: Declaration of Conscience
06-01.subtitle: The Declaration of Conscience speech, by U.
06-01.text: The Declaration of Conscience speech, by U.S. Senator from Maine, Margaret Chase Smith, is delivered in response to Joseph R. McCarthy's speech at Wheeling, West Virginia.

06-01.title: General Motors
06-01.subtitle: General Motors files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
06-01.text: General Motors files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. It is the fourth largest United States bankruptcy in history.

06-01.title: Cold War
06-01.subtitle: Cold War: George H.
06-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
06-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

06-01.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
06-01.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour makes its final landing after 25 flights.
06-01.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

06-01.title: Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty
06-01.subtitle: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty comes into effect.
06-01.text: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty was an arms control treaty between the United States and the Soviet Union. US President Ronald Reagan and Soviet General Secretary Mikhail Gorbachev signed the treaty on 8 December 1987. The US Senate approved the treaty on 27 May 1988, and Reagan and Gorbachev ratified it on 1 June 1988.

05-29.title: President of the United States
05-29.subtitle: U.
05-29.text: U.S. President Ronald Reagan begins his first visit to the Soviet Union when he arrives in Moscow for a superpower summit with the Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev.

05-29.title: Edmund Hillary
05-29.subtitle: Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay become the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest, on Tenzing Norgay's (adopted) 39th birthday.
05-29.text: Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay become the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest, on Tenzing Norgay's (adopted) 39th birthday.

05-29.title: Albert Einstein
05-29.subtitle: Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity is tested (later confirmed) by Arthur Eddington and Andrew Claude de la Cherois Crommelin.
05-29.text: Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity is tested (later confirmed) by Arthur Eddington and Andrew Claude de la Cherois Crommelin.

05-29.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery completes the first docking with the International Space Station.
05-29.text: Space Shuttle Discovery completes the first docking with the International Space Station.

05-29.title: 2008 Iceland earthquake
05-29.subtitle: A doublet earthquake, of combined magnitude 6.
05-29.text: A doublet earthquake, of combined magnitude 6.1, strikes Iceland near the town of Selfoss, injuring 30 people.

06-03.title: Maurice Herzog
06-03.subtitle: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.
06-03.text: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.

06-03.title: Operation Blue Star
06-03.subtitle: Operation Blue Star, a military offensive, is launched by the Indian government at Harmandir Sahib, also known as the Go...
06-03.text: Operation Blue Star, a military offensive, is launched by the Indian government at Harmandir Sahib, also known as the Golden Temple, the holiest shrine for Sikhs, in Amritsar. The operation continues until June 6, with casualties, most of them civilians, in excess of 5,000.

06-03.title: Khartoum massacre
06-03.subtitle: Khartoum massacre: In Sudan, over 100 people are killed when security forces accompanied by Janjaweed militiamen storm a...
06-03.text: Khartoum massacre: In Sudan, over 100 people are killed when security forces accompanied by Janjaweed militiamen storm and open fire on a sit-in protest.

06-03.title: Gemini 4
06-03.subtitle: The launch of Gemini 4, the first multi-day space mission by a NASA crew.
06-03.text: The launch of Gemini 4, the first multi-day space mission by a NASA crew. Ed White, a crew member, performs the first American spacewalk.

06-03.title: Soviet Union
06-03.subtitle: A Soviet supersonic Tupolev Tu-144 crashes near Goussainville, France, killing 14, the first crash of a supersonic passenger aircraft.
06-03.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

06-02.title: Surveyor program
06-02.subtitle: Surveyor program: Surveyor 1 lands in Oceanus Procellarum on the Moon, becoming the first U.
06-02.text: The Surveyor program was a NASA program that, from June 1966 through January 1968, sent seven robotic spacecraft to the surface of the Moon. Its primary goal was to demonstrate the feasibility of soft landings on the Moon. The Surveyor craft were the first American spacecraft to achieve soft landing on an extraterrestrial body.
06-02.location: 1960s NASA program to soft-land robotic probes on the Moon

06-02.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
06-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-91, the final mission of the Shuttle-Mir program.
06-02.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-91, the final mission of the Shuttle-Mir program.

06-02.title: Mars
06-02.subtitle: Europe launches its first voyage to another planet, Mars.
06-02.text: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun. It is also known as the "Red Planet", for its orange-red appearance. Mars is a desert-like rocky planet with a tenuous atmosphere that is primarily carbon dioxide. At the average surface level the atmospheric pressure is a few thousandths of Earth's, atmospheric temperature ranges from −153 to 20 °C, and cosmic radiation is high.

06-02.title: Ankara
06-02.subtitle: Following a request from Ankara, the United Nations officially changed the name of the Republic of Turkey in the organiz...
06-02.text: Following a request from Ankara, the United Nations officially changed the name of the Republic of Turkey in the organization from what was previously known as "Turkey" to "Türkiye".

06-02.title: 1962 FIFA World Cup
06-02.subtitle: During the FIFA World Cup, police had to intervene multiple times in fights between Chilean and Italian players in one o...
06-02.text: During the FIFA World Cup, police had to intervene multiple times in fights between Chilean and Italian players in one of the most violent games in football history.

06-06.title: March Against Fear
06-06.subtitle: March Against Fear: African-American civil rights activist James Meredith is wounded in an ambush by white sniper James Aubrey Norvell.
06-06.text: March Against Fear: African-American civil rights activist James Meredith is wounded in an ambush by white sniper James Aubrey Norvell. Meredith and Norvell are photographed by Jack R. Thornell, whose photo will receive the 1967 Pulitzer Prize in Photography, the last one to be awarded in the category.

06-06.title: 1982 Lebanon War
06-06.subtitle: 1982 Lebanon War: The war begins as forces under Israeli Defense Minister Ariel Sharon invade southern Lebanon during Op...
06-06.text: 1982 Lebanon War: The war begins as forces under Israeli Defense Minister Ariel Sharon invade southern Lebanon during Operation Peace for the Galilee, eventually reaching as far north as the capital Beirut.

06-06.title: World War II
06-06.subtitle: World War II: Commencement of Operation Overlord: The Allied invasion of Normandy begins with the execution of Operation...
06-06.text: Operation Overlord was the codename for the Battle of Normandy, the Allied operation that launched the successful liberation of German-occupied Western Europe during World War II. The operation was launched on 6 June 1944 (D-Day) with the Normandy landings. A 1,200-plane airborne assault preceded an amphibious assault involving more than 5,000 vessels. Nearly 160,000 troops crossed the English Channel on 6 June, and more than two million Allied troops were in France by the end of August.
06-06.location: World War II operation in France

06-06.title: Soyuz 11
06-06.subtitle: Soyuz 11 is launched.
06-06.text: Soyuz 11 is launched. The mission ends in disaster when all three cosmonauts, Georgy Dobrovolsky, Vladislav Volkov, and Viktor Patsayev are suffocated by uncontrolled decompression of the capsule during re-entry on 29 June.

06-06.title: 2002 Eastern Mediterranean event
06-06.subtitle: Eastern Mediterranean event.
06-06.text: Eastern Mediterranean event. A near-Earth asteroid estimated at ten meters in diameter explodes over the Mediterranean Sea between Greece and Libya. The explosion is estimated to have a force of 26 kilotons, slightly more powerful than the Nagasaki atomic bomb.

06-07.title: Cohen v. California
06-07.subtitle: The United States Supreme Court overturns the conviction of Paul Cohen for disturbing the peace, setting the precedent t...
06-07.text: The United States Supreme Court overturns the conviction of Paul Cohen for disturbing the peace, setting the precedent that vulgar writing is protected under the First Amendment to the United States Constitution.

06-07.title: Lux Radio Theatre
06-07.subtitle: Lux Radio Theatre signs off the air permanently.
06-07.text: Lux Radio Theatre signs off the air permanently. The show launched in New York in 1934, and featured radio adaptations of Broadway shows and popular films.

06-07.subtitle: Sony launches Betamax, the first videocassette recorder format.
06-07.text: Sony launches Betamax, the first videocassette recorder format.

06-07.text: The United Nations defines the Blue Line as the border between Israel and Lebanon.

06-07.text: The Israeli Air Force destroys Iraq's Osiraq nuclear reactor during Operation Opera.

06-04.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
06-04.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

06-04.title: Dragon Spacecraft Qualification Unit
06-04.subtitle: Falcon 9 Flight 1 is the maiden flight of the SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, which launches from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station Space Launch Complex 40.
06-04.text: Falcon 9 Flight 1 is the maiden flight of the SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, which launches from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station Space Launch Complex 40.

06-04.title: World War II
06-04.subtitle: World War II: The Dunkirk evacuation ends: the British Armed Forces completes evacuation of 338,000 troops from Dunkirk in France.
06-04.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

06-04.title: Solidarity
06-04.subtitle: Solidarity's victory in the 1989 Polish legislative election occurs, the first election since the Communist Polish Unite...
06-04.text: Solidarity's victory in the 1989 Polish legislative election occurs, the first election since the Communist Polish United Workers' Party abandoned its monopoly of power. It sparks off the Revolutions of 1989 in Eastern Europe.

06-04.title: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded
06-04.subtitle: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded: Laura E.
06-04.text: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded: Laura E. Richards, Maude H. Elliott, and Florence Hall receive the first Pulitzer for biography (for Julia Ward Howe). Jean Jules Jusserand receives the first Pulitzer for history for his work With Americans of Past and Present Days. Herbert B. Swope receives the first Pulitzer for journalism for his work for the New York World.

06-08.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
06-08.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-117 carrying two truss segments and solar arrays to the International Space Station.
06-08.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

06-08.title: Vietnam War
06-08.subtitle: Vietnam War: Nine-year-old Phan Thị Kim Phúc is burned by napalm, an event captured by Associated Press photographer Nic...
06-08.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
06-08.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

06-08.title: Lockheed F-104 Starfighter
06-08.subtitle: An F-104 Starfighter collides with XB-70 Valkyrie prototype no.
06-08.text: An F-104 Starfighter collides with XB-70 Valkyrie prototype no. 2, destroying both aircraft during a photo shoot near Edwards Air Force Base. Joseph A. Walker, a NASA test pilot, and Carl Cross, a United States Air Force test pilot, are both killed.

06-08.title: New Zealand
06-08.subtitle: New Zealand's Labour government establishes a national nuclear-free zone under the New Zealand Nuclear Free Zone, Disarm...
06-08.text: New Zealand's Labour government establishes a national nuclear-free zone under the New Zealand Nuclear Free Zone, Disarmament, and Arms Control Act 1987.

06-08.title: Mamoru Takuma
06-08.subtitle: Mamoru Takuma kills eight and injures 15 in a mass stabbing at an elementary school in the Osaka Prefecture of Japan.
06-08.text: Mamoru Takuma kills eight and injures 15 in a mass stabbing at an elementary school in the Osaka Prefecture of Japan.

06-09.subtitle: Kosovo War: The Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and NATO sign a peace treaty.
06-09.text: Kosovo War: The Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and NATO sign a peace treaty.

06-09.subtitle: Vietnam War: The Viet Cong commences combat with the Army of the Republic of Vietnam in the Battle of Đồng Xoài, one of ...
06-09.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.

06-09.subtitle: First ascent of Broad Peak by Fritz Wintersteller, Marcus Schmuck, Kurt Diemberger, and Hermann Buhl.
