06-09.title: USS George Washington
06-09.subtitle: The USS George Washington is launched.
06-09.text: The USS George Washington is launched. It is the first nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine.

06-09.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
06-09.subtitle: U.
06-09.text: Lyndon Baines Johnson, also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States, serving from 1963 to 1969. He became president after the assassination of John F. Kennedy, under whom he had served as the 37th vice president from 1961 to 1963. A Southern Democrat, Johnson previously represented Texas in Congress for over 23 years, first as a U.S. representative from 1937 to 1949, and then as a U.S. senator from 1949 to 1961.
06-09.location: President of the United States from 1963 to 1969

06-11.title: American Civil Rights Movement
06-11.subtitle: American Civil Rights Movement: Governor of Alabama George Wallace defiantly stands at the door of Foster Auditorium at ...
06-11.text: American Civil Rights Movement: Governor of Alabama George Wallace defiantly stands at the door of Foster Auditorium at the University of Alabama in an attempt to block two black students, Vivian Malone and James Hood, from attending that school. Later in the day, accompanied by federalized National Guard troops, they are able to register.

06-11.title: Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope
06-11.subtitle: The Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope is launched into orbit.
06-11.text: The Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope is launched into orbit.

06-11.title: Cologne school massacre
06-11.subtitle: World War II veteran Walter Seifert attacks an elementary school in Cologne, Germany, killing at least eight children an...
06-11.text: World War II veteran Walter Seifert attacks an elementary school in Cologne, Germany, killing at least eight children and two teachers and seriously injuring several more with a home-made flamethrower and a lance.

06-11.title: FIFA World Cup
06-11.subtitle: The first African FIFA World Cup kicks off in South Africa.
06-11.text: The FIFA World Cup, often called the World Cup, is an international association football competition among the senior men's national teams of the members of the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA), the sport's global governing body. The tournament has been held every four years since the inaugural tournament in 1930, with the exception of 1942 and 1946 due to the Second World War.

06-11.title: Antonio Meucci
06-11.subtitle: Antonio Meucci is acknowledged as the first inventor of the telephone by the United States Congress.
06-11.text: Antonio Meucci is acknowledged as the first inventor of the telephone by the United States Congress.

06-10.title: Spirit
06-10.subtitle: The Spirit rover is launched, beginning NASA's Mars Exploration Rover mission.
06-10.text: Spirit, also known as MER-A or MER-2, is a Mars robotic rover, active from 2004 to 2010. Spirit was operational on Mars for 2208 sols or 3.3 Martian years. It was one of two rovers of NASA's Mars Exploration Rover Mission managed by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL).
06-10.location: Former NASA Mars rover, active from 2004 to 2010

06-10.title: World War II
06-10.subtitle: World War II: The Lidice massacre is perpetrated as a reprisal for the assassination of Obergruppenführer Reinhard Heydrich.
06-10.text: World War II: The Lidice massacre is perpetrated as a reprisal for the assassination of Obergruppenführer Reinhard Heydrich.

06-10.title: DF-31
06-10.subtitle: China conducts a nuclear test for DF-31 warhead at Area C (Beishan), Lop Nur, its prominence being due to the Cox Report.
06-10.text: China conducts a nuclear test for DF-31 warhead at Area C (Beishan), Lop Nur, its prominence being due to the Cox Report.

06-10.title: Kosovo War
06-10.subtitle: Kosovo War: NATO suspends its airstrikes after Slobodan Milošević agrees to withdraw Serbian forces from Kosovo.
06-10.text: Kosovo War: NATO suspends its airstrikes after Slobodan Milošević agrees to withdraw Serbian forces from Kosovo.

06-10.title: Pope John Paul II
06-10.subtitle: Pope John Paul II canonizes Lebanon's first female saint, Saint Rafqa.
06-10.text: Pope John Paul II was head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from 16 October 1978 until his death in 2005. He was the first non-Italian pope since Adrian VI in the 16th century, as well as the third-longest-serving pope in history, after St. Peter and Pius IX.
06-10.location: Head of the Catholic Church from 1978 to 2005

06-05.title: Boeing Starliner
06-05.subtitle: The Boeing Starliner is launched on its first crewed flight, carrying astronauts Barry Wilmore and Sunita Williams to the International Space Station.
06-05.text: The Boeing Starliner is launched on its first crewed flight, carrying astronauts Barry Wilmore and Sunita Williams to the International Space Station.

06-05.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
06-05.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-111, carrying the Expedition 5 crew to the International Space Station to replace the Expedition 4 crew.
06-05.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

06-05.title: Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report
06-05.subtitle: The Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports that five people in ...
06-05.text: The Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports that five people in Los Angeles, California, have a rare form of pneumonia seen only in patients with weakened immune systems, in what turns out to be the first recognized cases of AIDS.

06-05.title: Six-Day War
06-05.subtitle: The Six-Day War in Kisangani begins in Kisangani, in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, between Ugandan and Rwandan forces.
06-05.text: The Six-Day War in Kisangani begins in Kisangani, in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, between Ugandan and Rwandan forces. A large part of the city is destroyed.

06-05.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
06-05.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-40, the fifth spacelab mission.
06-05.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

06-13.title: Hayabusa
06-13.subtitle: A capsule of the Japanese spacecraft Hayabusa, containing particles of the asteroid 25143 Itokawa, returns to Earth by l...
06-13.text: A capsule of the Japanese spacecraft Hayabusa, containing particles of the asteroid 25143 Itokawa, returns to Earth by landing in the Australian Outback.

06-13.title: Philadelphia Phillies
06-13.subtitle: In a game versus the Philadelphia Phillies at Veterans Stadium, Los Angeles Dodgers teammates Steve Garvey, Davey Lopes,...
06-13.text: In a game versus the Philadelphia Phillies at Veterans Stadium, Los Angeles Dodgers teammates Steve Garvey, Davey Lopes, Ron Cey and Bill Russell play together as an infield for the first time, going on to set the Major League Baseball record of staying together for 8+1⁄2 years.

06-13.title: Pioneer 10
06-13.subtitle: Pioneer 10 becomes the first man-made object to leave the central Solar System when it passes beyond the orbit of Neptune.
06-13.text: Pioneer 10 becomes the first man-made object to leave the central Solar System when it passes beyond the orbit of Neptune.

06-13.subtitle: U.
06-13.text: Lyndon Baines Johnson, also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States, serving from 1963 to 1969. He became president after the assassination of John F. Kennedy, under whom he had served as the 37th vice president from 1961 to 1963. A Southern Democrat, Johnson previously represented Texas in Congress for over 23 years, first as a U.S. representative from 1937 to 1949, and then as a U.S. senator from 1949 to 1961.

06-13.subtitle: President Kim Dae-jung of South Korea meets Kim Jong-il, leader of North Korea, for the beginning of the first ever inte...
06-13.text: President Kim Dae-jung of South Korea meets Kim Jong-il, leader of North Korea, for the beginning of the first ever inter-Korea summit, in the northern capital of Pyongyang.
06-13.location: President of South Korea from 1998 to 2003

06-12.subtitle: Kokkadichcholai massacre: The Sri Lankan Army massacres 152 minority Tamil civilians in the village of Kokkadichcholai n...
06-12.text: Kokkadichcholai massacre: The Sri Lankan Army massacres 152 minority Tamil civilians in the village of Kokkadichcholai near the Eastern Province town of Batticaloa.

06-12.title: NAACP
06-12.subtitle: NAACP field secretary Medgar Evers is murdered in front of his home in Jackson, Mississippi, by Ku Klux Klan member Byro...
06-12.text: NAACP field secretary Medgar Evers is murdered in front of his home in Jackson, Mississippi, by Ku Klux Klan member Byron De La Beckwith during the civil rights movement.

06-12.subtitle: United States President Donald Trump and Kim Jong-un of North Korea held the first meeting between leaders of their two countries in Singapore.
06-12.text: United States President Donald Trump and Kim Jong-un of North Korea held the first meeting between leaders of their two countries in Singapore.

06-15.title: Mount Pinatubo
06-15.subtitle: In the Philippines, Mount Pinatubo erupts in the second largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century, killing over 800 people.
06-15.text: In the Philippines, Mount Pinatubo erupts in the second largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century, killing over 800 people.

06-15.title: The Troubles
06-15.subtitle: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA) detonates a powerful truck bomb in the middle of Manchester, E...
06-15.text: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA) detonates a powerful truck bomb in the middle of Manchester, England, devastating the city centre and injuring 200 people.

06-15.title: John Alcock
06-15.subtitle: John Alcock and Arthur Brown complete the first nonstop transatlantic flight when they reach Clifden, County Galway, Ireland.
06-15.text: John Alcock and Arthur Brown complete the first nonstop transatlantic flight when they reach Clifden, County Galway, Ireland.

06-15.title: Cathay Pacific Flight 700Z
06-15.subtitle: Cathay Pacific Flight 700Z is destroyed by a bomb over Pleiku, Vietnam (then South Vietnam) and kills 81 people.
06-15.text: Cathay Pacific Flight 700Z is destroyed by a bomb over Pleiku, Vietnam (then South Vietnam) and kills 81 people.

06-15.title: Francisco Franco
06-15.subtitle: After the death of dictator Francisco Franco in 1975, the first democratic elections take place in Spain.
06-15.text: After the death of dictator Francisco Franco in 1975, the first democratic elections take place in Spain.

06-14.title: Albert II
06-14.subtitle: Albert II, a rhesus monkey, rides a V-2 rocket to an altitude of 134 km (83 mi), thereby becoming the first mammal and first monkey in space.
06-14.text: Albert II, a rhesus monkey, rides a V-2 rocket to an altitude of 134 km (83 mi), thereby becoming the first mammal and first monkey in space.

06-14.title: European Space Research Organisation
06-14.subtitle: The European Space Research Organisation is established in Paris – later becoming the European Space Agency.
06-14.text: The European Space Research Organisation is established in Paris – later becoming the European Space Agency.

06-14.title: Disneyland Monorail
06-14.subtitle: Disneyland Monorail System, the first daily operating monorail system in the Western Hemisphere, opens to the public in Anaheim, California.
06-14.text: Disneyland Monorail System, the first daily operating monorail system in the Western Hemisphere, opens to the public in Anaheim, California.

06-14.title: Chile
06-14.subtitle: Chile becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty.
06-14.text: Chile, officially the Republic of Chile, is a country in western South America. It is the southernmost country in the world and the closest to Antarctica, extending along a narrow strip of land between the Andes Mountains and the Pacific Ocean. According to the 2024 census, Chile had an enumerated population of 18.5 million.

06-14.title: Falklands War
06-14.subtitle: Falklands War: Argentine forces in the capital Stanley conditionally surrender to British forces.
06-14.text: The Falklands War was a ten-week undeclared war between Argentina and the United Kingdom in 1982 over two British Overseas Territories in the South Atlantic: the Falkland Islands and its territorial dependency, South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands. The conflict began on 2 April 1982, when Argentina invaded and occupied the Falkland Islands, followed by the invasion of South Georgia the next day.

06-17.title: Space Shuttle program
06-17.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: STS-51-G mission: Space Shuttle Discovery launches carrying Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Sa...
06-17.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

06-17.title: Nuclear weapons testing
06-17.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: China announces a successful test of its first thermonuclear weapon.
06-17.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
06-17.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

06-17.title: Cold War
06-17.subtitle: Cold War: East Germany Workers Uprising: In East Germany, the Soviet Union orders a division of troops into East Berlin to quell a rebellion.
06-17.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
06-17.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

06-17.title: Hawaiian Kingdom
06-17.subtitle: In the Kingdom of Hawaii, Kamehameha III issues the edict of toleration which gives Roman Catholics the freedom to worship in the Hawaiian Islands.
06-17.text: In the Kingdom of Hawaii, Kamehameha III issues the edict of toleration which gives Roman Catholics the freedom to worship in the Hawaiian Islands. The Hawaii Catholic Church and the Cathedral of Our Lady of Peace are established as a result.

06-17.title: Juneteenth
06-17.subtitle: Juneteenth National Independence Day, was signed into law by President Joe Biden, to become the first federal holiday es...
06-17.text: Juneteenth, officially Juneteenth National Independence Day, is a federal holiday in the United States. It is celebrated annually on June 19 to commemorate the end of slavery in the United States. The holiday's name, first used in the 1890s, is a portmanteau of June and nineteenth, referring to June 19, 1865, the day when Major General Gordon Granger ordered the final enforcement of the Emancipation Proclamation in Texas at the end of the American Civil War.
06-17.location: US holiday commemorating the end of slavery in the United States

05-12.text: The Soviet spacecraft Luna 5 crashes on the Moon.

05-12.subtitle: U.
05-12.text: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement conducts the largest-ever raid of a workplace in Postville, Iowa, arresting nearly 400 immigrants for identity theft and document fraud.

World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

05-12.text: The WannaCry ransomware attack impacts over 400,000 computers worldwide, targeting computers of the United Kingdom's National Health Services and Telefónica computers.

05-12.title: Jimmy Carter
05-12.subtitle: Former US President Jimmy Carter arrives in Cuba for a five-day visit with Fidel Castro, becoming the first President of...
05-12.text: James Earl Carter Jr. was an American politician and humanitarian who served as the 39th president of the United States from 1977 to 1981. A member of the Democratic Party, Carter served from 1971 to 1975 as the 76th governor of Georgia and from 1963 to 1967 in the Georgia State Senate. He lived longer than any other president in U.S. history, reaching age 100.
05-12.location: President of the United States from 1977 to 1981

06-18.subtitle: The Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO), a NASA robotic spacecraft is launched.

06-18.text: Titan, a submersible operated by OceanGate Expeditions, imploded while attempting to view the wreck of the Titanic, killing all five people on board including OceanGate co-founder and CEO Stockton Rush in the North Atlantic Ocean.

06-16.text: Revolutions of 1989: Imre Nagy, the former Hungarian prime minister, is reburied in Budapest following the collapse of Communism in Hungary.

06-16.text: US President Ronald Reagan awards the Congressional Gold Medal to Ken Taylor, Canada's former ambassador to Iran, for helping six Americans escape from Iran during the hostage crisis of 1979–81; he is the first foreign citizen bestowed the honor.

06-22.title: The hand of God
