06-22.text: The famous Hand of God goal, scored by Diego Maradona in the quarter-finals of the 1986 FIFA World Cup match between Argentina and England, ignites controversy. This was later followed by the Goal of the Century. Argentina wins 2–1 and later goes on to win the World Cup.

06-22.title: Treaty on Basic Relations Between Japan
06-22.subtitle: The Treaty on Basic Relations between Japan and the Republic of Korea is signed.
06-22.text: The Treaty on Basic Relations between Japan and the Republic of Korea is signed.

06-22.title: Virgin Atlantic
06-22.subtitle: Virgin Atlantic launches with its first flight from London to Newark.
06-22.text: Virgin Atlantic launches with its first flight from London to Newark.

06-22.title: Charon
06-22.subtitle: Charon, the first of Pluto's satellites to be discovered, was first seen at the United States Naval Observatory by James W.
06-22.text: Charon, the first of Pluto's satellites to be discovered, was first seen at the United States Naval Observatory by James W. Christy.

06-22.title: United States
06-22.subtitle: The United States conducts airstrikes on three Iranian nuclear sites in Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
06-22.text: The United States conducts airstrikes on three Iranian nuclear sites in Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.

06-23.title: NASA
06-23.subtitle: NASA's Space Station Processing Facility, a new state-of-the-art manufacturing building for the International Space Stat...
06-23.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

06-23.title: Ashton Eaton
06-23.subtitle: Ashton Eaton breaks the decathlon world record at the United States Olympic Trials.
06-23.text: Ashton Eaton breaks the decathlon world record at the United States Olympic Trials.

06-23.title: 2001 southern Peru earthquake
06-23.subtitle: The 8.
06-23.text: The 8.4 Mw  southern Peru earthquake shakes coastal Peru with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). A destructive tsunami followed, leaving at least 74 people dead, and 2,687 injured.

06-23.title: Nik Wallenda
06-23.subtitle: Nik Wallenda becomes the first man to successfully walk across the Grand Canyon on a tight rope.
06-23.text: Nik Wallenda becomes the first man to successfully walk across the Grand Canyon on a tight rope.

06-23.title: Watergate scandal
06-23.subtitle: Watergate scandal: U.
06-23.text: Watergate scandal: U.S. President Richard M. Nixon and White House Chief of Staff H. R. Haldeman are taped talking about illegally using the Central Intelligence Agency to obstruct the Federal Bureau of Investigation's investigation into the Watergate break-ins.

06-24.title: Jiang Zemin
06-24.subtitle: Jiang Zemin succeeds Zhao Ziyang to become the General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party after the 1989 Tiananmen Square protests and massacre.
06-24.text: Jiang Zemin succeeds Zhao Ziyang to become the General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party after the 1989 Tiananmen Square protests and massacre.

06-24.title: Rugby World Cup
06-24.subtitle: Rugby World Cup: South Africa defeats New Zealand and Nelson Mandela presents Francois Pienaar with the Webb Ellis Cup i...
06-24.text: Rugby World Cup: South Africa defeats New Zealand and Nelson Mandela presents Francois Pienaar with the Webb Ellis Cup in an iconic post-apartheid moment.

06-24.title: Wimbledon Championships
06-24.subtitle: At Wimbledon, John Isner of the United States defeats Nicolas Mahut of France, in the longest match in professional tennis history.
06-24.text: At Wimbledon, John Isner of the United States defeats Nicolas Mahut of France, in the longest match in professional tennis history.

06-24.title: Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization
06-24.subtitle: In Dobbs v.
06-24.text: In Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization, the U.S. Supreme Court rules that the U.S. Constitution does not assign the authority to regulate abortions to the federal government, thereby returning such authority to the individual states. This overturns the prior decisions in Roe v. Wade (1973) and Planned Parenthood v. Casey (1992).

06-24.title: First Indochina War
06-24.subtitle: First Indochina War: Battle of Mang Yang Pass: Viet Minh troops belonging to the 803rd Regiment ambush G.
06-24.text: First Indochina War: Battle of Mang Yang Pass: Viet Minh troops belonging to the 803rd Regiment ambush G.M. 100 of France in An Khê.

06-20.title: World War II
06-20.subtitle: World War II: The Royal Air Force launches Operation Bellicose, the first shuttle bombing raid of the war.
06-20.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

06-20.title: ABC News
06-20.subtitle: ABC News correspondent Bill Stewart is shot dead by a Nicaraguan National Guard soldier under the regime of Anastasio So...
06-20.text: ABC News correspondent Bill Stewart is shot dead by a Nicaraguan National Guard soldier under the regime of Anastasio Somoza Debayle during the Nicaraguan Revolution. The murder is caught on tape and sparks an international outcry against the regime.

06-20.title: Watergate scandal
06-20.subtitle: Watergate scandal: An 18+1⁄2-minute gap appears in the tape recording of the conversations between U.
06-20.text: Watergate scandal: An 18+1⁄2-minute gap appears in the tape recording of the conversations between U.S. President Richard Nixon and his advisers regarding the recent arrests of his operatives while breaking into the Watergate complex.

06-20.title: Sniper
06-20.subtitle: Snipers fire upon left-wing Peronists in Buenos Aires, Argentina, in what is known as the Ezeiza massacre.
06-20.text: A sniper is a military or paramilitary marksman who engages targets from positions of concealment or at distances exceeding the target's detection capabilities. Snipers generally have specialized training and are equipped with telescopic sights. Modern snipers use high-precision rifles and high-magnification optics. They often also serve as scouts or observers feeding tactical information back to their units or command headquarters.

06-20.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
06-20.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-78 to conduct life science and microgravity research aboard the Spacelab module.
06-20.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

06-26.title: Cold War
06-26.subtitle: Cold War: U.
06-26.text: John Fitzgerald Kennedy, also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. He was the youngest person elected president at 43 years, as well as the first Catholic president. Kennedy served at the height of the Cold War, and the majority of his foreign policy concerned relations with the Soviet Union and Cuba.
06-26.location: President of the United States from 1961 to 1963

06-26.title: Supreme Court of the United States
06-26.subtitle: The U.
06-26.text: The U.S. Supreme Court rules, 5–4, that same-sex couples have a constitutional right to marriage under the 14th Amendment to the United States Constitution.

06-26.title: Supreme Court of the United States
06-26.subtitle: The U.
06-26.text: The U.S. Supreme Court rules that the Communications Decency Act violates the First Amendment to the United States Constitution.

06-26.title: Universal Product Code
06-26.subtitle: The Universal Product Code is scanned for the first time to sell a package of Wrigley's chewing gum at the Marsh Supermarket in Troy, Ohio.
06-26.text: The Universal Product Code is scanned for the first time to sell a package of Wrigley's chewing gum at the Marsh Supermarket in Troy, Ohio.

06-26.title: Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani
06-26.subtitle: Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani deposes his father Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani, the Emir of Qatar, in a bloodless coup d'état.
06-26.text: Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani deposes his father Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani, the Emir of Qatar, in a bloodless coup d'état.

06-25.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
06-25.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-50, the first shuttle mission to carry Extended Duration Orbiter hardware.
06-25.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

06-25.title: Cold War
06-25.subtitle: Cold War: Two cryptographers working for the United States National Security Agency left for vacation to Mexico, and fro...
06-25.text: Cold War: Two cryptographers working for the United States National Security Agency left for vacation to Mexico, and from there defected to the Soviet Union.

06-25.title: Rainbow flag
06-25.subtitle: The rainbow flag representing gay pride is flown for the first time during the San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Parade.
06-25.text: The rainbow flag representing gay pride is flown for the first time during the San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Parade.

06-25.title: Progress M-34
06-25.subtitle: An uncrewed Progress spacecraft collides with the Russian space station Mir.
06-25.text: An uncrewed Progress spacecraft collides with the Russian space station Mir.

06-25.title: Breakup of Yugoslavia
06-25.subtitle: The breakup of Yugoslavia begins when Slovenia and Croatia declare their independence from Yugoslavia.
06-25.text: After a period of political and economic crisis in the 1980s, the constituent republics of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia split apart in the early 1990s. Unresolved issues from the breakup caused a series of inter-ethnic Yugoslav Wars from 1991 to 2001 which primarily affected Bosnia and Herzegovina, neighbouring parts of Croatia and, some years later, Kosovo.

06-28.title: Honduras
06-28.subtitle: Honduran president Manuel Zelaya is ousted by a local military coup following a failed request to hold a referendum to r...
06-28.text: Honduras, officially the Republic of Honduras, is a country in Central America. It is bordered to the west by Guatemala, to the southwest by El Salvador, to the southeast by Nicaragua, to the south by the Pacific Ocean at the Gulf of Fonseca, and to the north by the Gulf of Honduras, a large inlet of the Caribbean Sea. Its capital and largest city is Tegucigalpa.

06-28.title: Iraq War
06-28.subtitle: Iraq War: Sovereign power is handed to the interim government of Iraq by the Coalition Provisional Authority, ending the U.
06-28.text: Iraq War: Sovereign power is handed to the interim government of Iraq by the Coalition Provisional Authority, ending the U.S.-led rule of that nation.

06-28.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
06-28.subtitle: Elections are held for the Northern Ireland Assembly, which will lead to power-sharing between unionists and nationalist...
06-28.text: Elections are held for the Northern Ireland Assembly, which will lead to power-sharing between unionists and nationalists in Northern Ireland for the first time.

06-28.title: Supreme Court of the United States
06-28.subtitle: The United States Supreme Court upholds the constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act's individual mandate in Nationa...
06-28.text: The United States Supreme Court upholds the constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act's individual mandate in National Federation of Independent Business v. Sebelius.

06-28.title: Mustard gas
06-28.subtitle: For the first time in military history, a civilian population is targeted for chemical attack when Iraqi warplanes bombe...
06-28.text: For the first time in military history, a civilian population is targeted for chemical attack when Iraqi warplanes bombed the Iranian town of Sardasht.

06-29.title: Sampoong Department Store collapse
06-29.subtitle: The Sampoong Department Store collapses in the Seocho District of Seoul, South Korea, killing 502 and injuring 937.
06-29.text: The Sampoong Department Store collapses in the Seocho District of Seoul, South Korea, killing 502 and injuring 937.

06-29.title: Convair CV-240 family
06-29.subtitle: A Convair CV-580 and De Havilland Canada DHC-6 Twin Otter collide above Lake Winnebago near Appleton, Wisconsin, killing 13.
06-29.text: A Convair CV-580 and De Havilland Canada DHC-6 Twin Otter collide above Lake Winnebago near Appleton, Wisconsin, killing 13.

06-29.title: Soviet Union
06-29.subtitle: Prior to re-entry (following a record-setting stay aboard the Soviet Union's Salyut 1 space station), the crew capsule o...
06-29.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

06-29.title: Apple Inc.
06-29.subtitle: Apple Inc.
06-29.text: Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, in Silicon Valley, best known for its consumer electronics, software and online services. Founded in 1976 as Apple Computer Company by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne, the company was incorporated by Jobs and Wozniak as Apple Computer, Inc. the following year. It was renamed to its current name in 2007 as the company had expanded its focus from computers to consumer electronics.

06-29.title: Space Shuttle program
06-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: STS-71 Mission (Atlantis) docks with the Russian space station Mir for the first time.
06-29.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

06-30.title: June 2013 Egyptian protests
06-30.subtitle: Protests begin around Egypt against President Mohamed Morsi and the ruling Freedom and Justice Party, leading to their o...
06-30.text: Protests begin around Egypt against President Mohamed Morsi and the ruling Freedom and Justice Party, leading to their overthrow during the 2013 Egyptian coup d'état.

06-30.title: Donald Trump
06-30.subtitle: Donald Trump becomes the first sitting US President to visit the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (North Korea).
06-30.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
06-30.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

06-30.title: Soviet Union
06-30.subtitle: The crew of the Soviet Soyuz 11 spacecraft are killed when their air supply escapes through a faulty valve.
06-30.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

06-30.title: Belgian Congo
06-30.subtitle: Belgian Congo gains independence as Republic of the Congo (Léopoldville).
06-30.text: Belgian Congo gains independence as Republic of the Congo (Léopoldville).

06-30.title: National Organization for Women
06-30.subtitle: The National Organization for Women, the United States' largest feminist organization, is founded.
06-30.text: The National Organization for Women, the United States' largest feminist organization, is founded.

06-27.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
06-27.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launched from the Kennedy Space Center on the final research and development flight mission, STS-4.
06-27.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

06-27.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
06-27.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-71, the first space shuttle mission to dock with the Russian space station Mir.
06-27.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

06-27.title: Central Committee of the Chinese Communist
06-27.subtitle: The Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party issues its "Resolution on Certain Questions in the History of Our P...
06-27.text: The Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party issues its "Resolution on Certain Questions in the History of Our Party Since the Founding of the People's Republic of China", laying the blame for the Cultural Revolution on Mao Zedong.

06-27.title: NASA
06-27.subtitle: NASA launches the Interface Region Imaging Spectrograph space probe to observe the Sun.
06-27.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

06-27.title: Obninsk Nuclear Power Plant
06-27.subtitle: The Obninsk Nuclear Power Plant, the Soviet Union's first nuclear power station, opens in Obninsk, near Moscow.
06-27.text: The Obninsk Nuclear Power Plant, the Soviet Union's first nuclear power station, opens in Obninsk, near Moscow.

07-02.title: Provisional Revolutionary Government of the Republic
07-02.subtitle: End of South Vietnam; Communist North Vietnam annexes the former South Vietnam to form the unified Socialist Republic of Vietnam.
07-02.text: End of South Vietnam; Communist North Vietnam annexes the former South Vietnam to form the unified Socialist Republic of Vietnam.

07-02.title: 1966–70 French nuclear tests
07-02.subtitle: France conducts its first nuclear weapon test in the Pacific, on Moruroa Atoll.
07-02.text: France conducts its first nuclear weapon test in the Pacific, on Moruroa Atoll.

07-02.title: Vicente Fox
07-02.subtitle: Vicente Fox Quesada is elected the first President of México from an opposition party, the Partido Acción Nacional, afte...
07-02.text: Vicente Fox Quesada is elected the first President of México from an opposition party, the Partido Acción Nacional, after more than 70 years of continuous rule by the Partido Revolucionario Institucional, in the 2000 Mexican general election.

07-02.title: Live 8
07-02.subtitle: The Live 8 benefit concerts takes place in the G8 states and in South Africa.
07-02.text: The Live 8 benefit concerts takes place in the G8 states and in South Africa. More than 1,000 musicians perform and are broadcast on 182 television networks and 2,000 radio networks.

07-02.title: Aeroflot Flight 2306
07-02.subtitle: Aeroflot Flight 2306 crashes while attempting an emergency landing at Syktyvkar Airport in Syktyvkar, in present-day Kom...
07-02.text: Aeroflot Flight 2306 crashes while attempting an emergency landing at Syktyvkar Airport in Syktyvkar, in present-day Komi Republic, Russia, killing 54 people.

07-04.title: NASA
07-04.subtitle: NASA's Pathfinder space probe lands on the surface of Mars.
07-04.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

07-04.title: Space Shuttle program
07-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Discovery launches STS-121 to the International Space Station.
07-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-04.title: U.S. state
07-04.subtitle: Due to the post-Independence Day admission of Hawaii as the 50th U.
07-04.text: Due to the post-Independence Day admission of Hawaii as the 50th U.S. state on August 21, 1959, the 50-star flag of the United States debuts in Philadelphia, almost ten and a half months later (see Flag Acts (United States)).

07-04.title: Space Shuttle program
07-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Columbia lands at Edwards Air Force Base at the end of the program's final test flight, STS-4.
07-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-04.title: Barbados
07-04.subtitle: Barbados, Guyana, Jamaica, and Trinidad and Tobago sign the Treaty of Chaguaramas in Chaguaramas, Trinidad and Tobago es...
07-04.text: Barbados, Guyana, Jamaica, and Trinidad and Tobago sign the Treaty of Chaguaramas in Chaguaramas, Trinidad and Tobago establishing the Caribbean Community (CARICOM). It replaces the Caribbean Free Trade Association as another step towards Caribbean regional integration.

07-03.title: SS United States
07-03.subtitle: The SS United States sets sail on her maiden voyage to Southampton.
07-03.text: The SS United States sets sail on her maiden voyage to Southampton. During the voyage, the ship takes the Blue Riband away from the RMS Queen Mary.

07-03.title: Constitution of Puerto Rico
07-03.subtitle: The Constitution of Puerto Rico is approved by the United States Congress.
07-03.text: The Constitution of Puerto Rico is approved by the United States Congress.

07-03.title: Jimmy Carter
07-03.subtitle: U.
07-03.text: James Earl Carter Jr. was an American politician and humanitarian who served as the 39th president of the United States from 1977 to 1981. A member of the Democratic Party, Carter served from 1971 to 1975 as the 76th governor of Georgia and from 1963 to 1967 in the Georgia State Senate. He lived longer than any other president in U.S. history, reaching age 100.
07-03.location: President of the United States from 1977 to 1981

07-03.title: Dan-Air Flight 1903
07-03.subtitle: Dan-Air Flight 1903 crashes into the Les Agudes mountain in the Montseny Massif near the village of Arbúcies in Cataloni...
07-03.text: Dan-Air Flight 1903 crashes into the Les Agudes mountain in the Montseny Massif near the village of Arbúcies in Catalonia, Spain, killing all 112 people aboard.

07-03.title: President of Egypt
07-03.subtitle: President of Egypt Mohamed Morsi is removed from office by the military after four days of protests all over the country...
07-03.text: President of Egypt Mohamed Morsi is removed from office by the military after four days of protests all over the country calling for his resignation, to which he did not respond. The president of the Supreme Constitutional Court of Egypt, Adly Mansour, is declared acting president until further elections are held.

07-01.title: Ghana
07-01.subtitle: Ghana becomes a republic and Kwame Nkrumah becomes its first President as Queen Elizabeth II ceases to be its head of state.
07-01.text: Ghana becomes a republic and Kwame Nkrumah becomes its first President as Queen Elizabeth II ceases to be its head of state.

07-01.title: Trust Territory of Somaliland
07-01.subtitle: The Trust Territory of Somaliland (the former Italian Somaliland) gains its independence from Italy.
07-01.text: The Trust Territory of Somaliland (the former Italian Somaliland) gains its independence from Italy. Concurrently, it unites as scheduled with the five-day-old State of Somaliland (the former British Somaliland) to form the Somali Republic.

07-01.title: Space Shuttle program
07-01.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-94, a re-flight of the prematurely-ended STS-83 mission with the same crew.
07-01.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-01.title: Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear
07-01.subtitle: The Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons is signed in Washington, D.
07-01.text: The Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, commonly known as the Non-Proliferation Treaty or NPT, is an international treaty, the objective of which is to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and weapons technology, to promote cooperation in the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, and to further the goal of achieving nuclear disarmament and general and complete disarmament.

07-01.title: People's Liberation Army Rocket Force
07-01.subtitle: The People's Liberation Army Rocket Force (The known as the 2nd Artillery Corps) is founded.
07-01.text: The People's Liberation Army Rocket Force, formerly the Second Artillery Corps, is the strategic and tactical missile force of the People's Republic of China. The PLARF is the 4th branch of the People's Liberation Army (PLA) and controls China's arsenal of land-based ballistic, hypersonic, cruise missiles—both nuclear and conventional.
07-01.location: Strategic and tactical missile force of the Chinese People's Liberation Army

07-06.title: The Late Late Show
07-06.subtitle: The Late Late Show, the world's longest-running chat show by the same broadcaster, airs on RTÉ One for the first time.
07-06.text: The Late Late Show, the world's longest-running chat show by the same broadcaster, airs on RTÉ One for the first time.

07-06.title: John Lennon
07-06.subtitle: John Lennon and Paul McCartney meet for the first time, as teenagers at Woolton Fete, three years before forming the Beatles.
07-06.text: John Lennon and Paul McCartney meet for the first time, as teenagers at Woolton Fete, three years before forming the Beatles.

07-06.title: Republic
07-06.subtitle: Malawi becomes a republic, with Hastings Banda as its first President.
07-06.text: Malawi becomes a republic, with Hastings Banda as its first President.

07-06.title: Yevpatoria
07-06.subtitle: The 70-metre Yevpatoria Planetary Radar sends a METI message (Cosmic Call 2) to five stars: Hip 4872, HD 245409, 55 Canc...
07-06.text: The 70-metre Yevpatoria Planetary Radar sends a METI message (Cosmic Call 2) to five stars: Hip 4872, HD 245409, 55 Cancri (HD 75732), HD 10307 and 47 Ursae Majoris (HD 95128). The messages will arrive to these stars in 2036, 2040, 2044, and 2049, respectively.

07-06.title: World War I
07-06.subtitle: World War I: Arabian troops led by T.
07-06.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

07-09.title: 1956 Amorgos earthquake
07-09.subtitle: The 7.
07-09.text: The 7.7 Mw  Amorgos earthquake shakes the Cyclades island group in the Aegean Sea with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent). The shaking and the destructive tsunami that followed left fifty-three people dead. A damaging M7.2 aftershock occurred minutes after the mainshock.

07-09.title: African Union
07-09.subtitle: The African Union is established in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, replacing the Organisation of African Unity (OAU).
07-09.text: The African Union is established in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, replacing the Organisation of African Unity (OAU). The organization's first chairman is Thabo Mbeki, President of South Africa.

07-09.title: 1958 Lituya Bay earthquake and megatsunami
07-09.subtitle: A 7.
07-09.text: A 7.8 Mw  strike-slip earthquake in Alaska causes a landslide that produces a megatsunami. The runup from the waves reached 525 m (1,722 ft) on the rim of Lituya Bay; five people were killed.

07-09.title: Starfish Prime
07-09.subtitle: Starfish Prime tests the effects of a nuclear explosion at orbital altitudes.
07-09.text: Starfish Prime tests the effects of a nuclear explosion at orbital altitudes.

07-09.title: Russell–Einstein Manifesto
07-09.subtitle: The Russell–Einstein Manifesto calls for a reduction of the risk of nuclear warfare.
07-09.text: The Russell–Einstein Manifesto calls for a reduction of the risk of nuclear warfare.

07-07.title: NASA
07-07.subtitle: NASA Opportunity rover, MER-B or Mars Exploration Rover–B, was launched into space aboard a Delta II rocket.
07-07.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

07-07.title: Ocean liner
07-07.subtitle: The ocean liner SS United States passes Bishop Rock on her maiden voyage, breaking the transatlantic speed record to bec...
07-07.text: An ocean liner is a type of passenger ship primarily used for transportation across seas or oceans. Ocean liners may also carry cargo or mail, and may sometimes be used for other purposes. The Queen Mary 2 is the only active ocean liner in 2026, serving with Cunard Line.

07-07.title: Che Guevara
07-07.subtitle: Ernesto "Che" Guevara sets out on a trip through Bolivia, Peru, Ecuador, Panama, Costa Rica, Nicaragua, Honduras, and El Salvador.
07-07.text: Ernesto "Che" Guevara de la Serna was an Argentine Marxist revolutionary, physician, author, guerrilla leader, diplomat, politician and military theorist. A major figure of the Cuban Revolution, his stylized visage has become a countercultural symbol of rebellion and global insignia in popular culture.

07-07.title: Yugoslav Wars
07-07.subtitle: Yugoslav Wars: The Brioni Agreement ends the ten-day independence war in Slovenia against the rest of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.
07-07.text: Yugoslav Wars: The Brioni Agreement ends the ten-day independence war in Slovenia against the rest of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

07-07.title: Cold War
07-07.subtitle: Cold War: Samantha Smith, a US schoolgirl, flies to the Soviet Union at the invitation of Secretary General Yuri Andropov.
07-07.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
07-07.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

07-08.title: Aeroflot Flight 4225
07-08.subtitle: Aeroflot Flight 4225 crashes near Almaty International Airport in the then Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic (present day...
07-08.text: Aeroflot Flight 4225 crashes near Almaty International Airport in the then Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic (present day Kazakhstan) killing all 166 people on board.

07-08.title: Germany national football team
07-08.subtitle: West Germany win the FIFA World Cup final against defending champions Argentina, with Andreas Brehme scoring the game's only goal.
07-08.text: West Germany win the FIFA World Cup final against defending champions Argentina, with Andreas Brehme scoring the game's only goal.

07-08.title: Brazil national football team
07-08.subtitle: The Brazil national football team suffers its joint-worst defeat, losing 7–1 to Germany in the semi-finals of the FIFA W...
07-08.text: The Brazil national football team suffers its joint-worst defeat, losing 7–1 to Germany in the semi-finals of the FIFA World Cup, in a match dubbed the Mineiraço.

07-08.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
07-08.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on an international science mission.
07-08.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

07-08.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
07-08.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched in the final mission of the U.
07-08.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

07-05.title: Ariane 5
07-05.subtitle: The last Ariane 5 rocket is launched, carrying the Heinrich Hertz and Syracuse 4B satellites.
07-05.text: The last Ariane 5 rocket is launched, carrying the Heinrich Hertz and Syracuse 4B satellites.

07-05.title: Elvis Presley
07-05.subtitle: Elvis Presley records his first single, "That's All Right", at Sun Records in Memphis, Tennessee.
07-05.text: Elvis Aaron Presley was an American singer and actor. Referred to as the "King of Rock and Roll", he is widely regarded as one of the most culturally significant figures of the 20th century. Presley's energetic and sexually provocative performance style, combined with a mix of influences across color lines during a transformative era in race relations, brought both great success and initial controversy.

07-05.title: Armenia
07-05.subtitle: Armenia adopts its constitution, four years after its independence from the Soviet Union.
07-05.text: Armenia adopts its constitution, four years after its independence from the Soviet Union.

07-05.title: Anglo-Saxons
07-05.subtitle: The largest hoard of Anglo-Saxon gold ever discovered in Britain, consisting of more than 1,500 items, is found near the...
07-05.text: The largest hoard of Anglo-Saxon gold ever discovered in Britain, consisting of more than 1,500 items, is found near the village of Hammerwich, near Lichfield, Staffordshire.

07-05.title: Keir Starmer
07-05.subtitle: Keir Starmer is appointed Prime Minister by Charles III, becoming the first Labour prime minister since Gordon Brown in ...
07-05.text: Keir Starmer is appointed Prime Minister by Charles III, becoming the first Labour prime minister since Gordon Brown in 2010 and the first one to win a general election since Tony Blair at the 2005 general election

07-10.title: Association football
07-10.subtitle: In women's association football, the United States defeats China in a penalty shoot-out at the Rose Bowl near Los Angele...
07-10.text: In women's association football, the United States defeats China in a penalty shoot-out at the Rose Bowl near Los Angeles to win the final match of the 1999 FIFA Women's World Cup. Watched by 90,185 spectators, the final sets a new world record for attendance at a women's sporting event.

07-10.title: Telstar 1
07-10.subtitle: Telstar, the world's first communications satellite, is launched into orbit.
07-10.text: Telstar, the world's first communications satellite, is launched into orbit.

07-10.title: Airbus
07-10.subtitle: EADS, the world's second-largest aerospace group is formed by the merger of Aérospatiale-Matra, DASA, and CASA.
07-10.text: EADS, the world's second-largest aerospace group is formed by the merger of Aérospatiale-Matra, DASA, and CASA.

07-10.title: DNA
07-10.subtitle: In London, scientists report the findings of the DNA analysis of a Neanderthal skeleton which supports the "out of Afric...
07-10.text: Deoxyribonucleic acid is a polymer composed of two polynucleotide chains that coil around each other to form a double helix. The polymer carries genetic instructions for the development, functioning, growth and reproduction of all known organisms and many viruses. DNA and ribonucleic acid (RNA) are nucleic acids. Alongside proteins, lipids and complex carbohydrates (polysaccharides), nucleic acids are one of the four major types of macromolecules that are essential for all known forms of life.

07-10.title: Greenpeace
07-10.subtitle: The Greenpeace vessel Rainbow Warrior is bombed and sunk in Auckland harbour by French DGSE agents, killing Fernando Pereira.
07-10.text: Greenpeace is an independent global campaigning network, founded in Canada in 1971 by a group of environmental activists. Greenpeace states its goal is to "ensure the ability of the Earth to nurture life in all its diversity", and focuses its campaigning on worldwide issues such as climate change, deforestation, overfishing, commercial whaling, genetic engineering, anti-war, and anti-nuclear issues. It uses direct action, advocacy, research, and ecotage to achieve its goals.

07-13.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
07-13.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-70 to deploy the TDRS-7 satellite.
07-13.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-70 to deploy the TDRS-7 satellite.

07-13.title: President of the United States
07-13.subtitle: Former president of the United States Donald Trump is injured in an assassination attempt while speaking at an election ...
07-13.text: Former president of the United States Donald Trump is injured in an assassination attempt while speaking at an election campaign rally near Butler, Pennsylvania.

07-13.title: Germany national football team
07-13.subtitle: Germany wins the FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina in the final 1–0 after extra time.
07-13.text: Germany wins the FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina in the final 1–0 after extra time.

07-13.title: Vuoristorata
07-13.subtitle: Vuoristorata, one of the oldest still-operating wooden roller coasters in Europe, is opened at the Linnanmäki amusement park in Helsinki, Finland.
07-13.text: Vuoristorata, one of the oldest still-operating wooden roller coasters in Europe, is opened at the Linnanmäki amusement park in Helsinki, Finland.

07-13.title: Dartmouth workshop
07-13.subtitle: The Dartmouth workshop is the first conference on artificial intelligence.
07-13.text: The Dartmouth workshop is the first conference on artificial intelligence.

07-14.title: NASA
07-14.subtitle: NASA's New Horizons probe performs the first flyby of Pluto, and thus completes the initial survey of the Solar System.
07-14.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

07-14.title: Scuderia Ferrari
07-14.subtitle: Ferrari take their first Formula One grand prix victory at the British Grand Prix at Silverstone.
07-14.text: Ferrari take their first Formula One grand prix victory at the British Grand Prix at Silverstone.

07-14.title: Mariner 4
07-14.subtitle: Mariner 4 flyby of Mars takes the first close-up photos of another planet.
07-14.text: Mariner 4 flyby of Mars takes the first close-up photos of another planet. The photographs take approximately six hours to be transmitted back to Earth.

07-14.title: Jacques Chirac
07-14.subtitle: French president Jacques Chirac escapes an assassination attempt from Maxime Brunerie during a Bastille Day parade at Champs-Élysées.
07-14.text: French president Jacques Chirac escapes an assassination attempt from Maxime Brunerie during a Bastille Day parade at Champs-Élysées.

07-14.title: Rawya Ateya
07-14.subtitle: Rawya Ateya takes her seat in the National Assembly of Egypt, thereby becoming the first female parliamentarian in the Arab world.
07-14.text: Rawya Ateya takes her seat in the National Assembly of Egypt, thereby becoming the first female parliamentarian in the Arab world.

07-12.title: Space Shuttle program
07-12.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on mission STS-104, carrying the Quest Joint Airlock to the International Space Station.
07-12.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-12.title: World War II
07-12.subtitle: World War II: Battle of Kursk: German and Soviet forces engage in the Battle of Prokhorovka, one of the largest armored engagements of all time.
07-12.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

07-12.title: United States Army
07-12.subtitle: U.
07-12.text: U.S. Army Apache helicopters engage in airstrikes against armed insurgents in Baghdad, Iraq, where civilians are killed; footage from the cockpit is later leaked to the Internet.

07-12.title: Soviet–Lithuanian Peace Treaty
07-12.subtitle: The Soviet–Lithuanian Peace Treaty is signed, by which Soviet Russia recognizes the independence of Lithuania.
07-12.text: The Soviet–Lithuanian Peace Treaty is signed, by which Soviet Russia recognizes the independence of Lithuania.

07-12.title: France national football team
07-12.subtitle: France win their first World Cup title, defeating defending champions Brazil 3–0.
07-12.text: The France national football team represents France in men's international football. It is controlled by the French Football Federation, the governing body for football in France. It is a member of UEFA in Europe and FIFA in global competitions. The team's colours and imagery reference two national symbols: the French blue-white-red tricolour and Gallic rooster. The team is colloquially known as Les Bleus.

07-11.title: Project Apollo
07-11.subtitle: Project Apollo: At a press conference, NASA announces lunar orbit rendezvous as the means to land astronauts on the Moon, and return them to Earth.
07-11.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

07-11.title: Skylab
07-11.subtitle: America's first space station, Skylab, is destroyed as it re-enters the Earth's atmosphere over the Indian Ocean.
07-11.text: America's first space station, Skylab, is destroyed as it re-enters the Earth's atmosphere over the Indian Ocean.

07-11.title: Spain national football team
07-11.subtitle: In Johannesburg, Spain defeat the Netherlands 1–0 after extra time to win their first FIFA World Cup title.
07-11.text: In Johannesburg, Spain defeat the Netherlands 1–0 after extra time to win their first FIFA World Cup title.

07-11.title: Virgin Galactic
07-11.subtitle: Virgin Galactic launches its founder, Richard Branson, into space, the first company ever to do so.
07-11.text: Virgin Galactic launches its founder, Richard Branson, into space, the first company ever to do so.

07-17.title: Apollo–Soyuz
07-17.subtitle: Apollo–Soyuz Test Project: An American Apollo and a Soviet Soyuz spacecraft dock with each other in orbit marking the fi...
07-17.text: Apollo–Soyuz Test Project: An American Apollo and a Soviet Soyuz spacecraft dock with each other in orbit marking the first such link-up between spacecraft from the two nations.

07-17.title: 1976 Summer Olympics
07-17.subtitle: The opening of the Summer Olympics in Montreal is marred by 25 African teams boycotting the games because of New Zealand's participation.
07-17.text: The opening of the Summer Olympics in Montreal is marred by 25 African teams boycotting the games because of New Zealand's participation. Contrary to rulings by other international sports organizations, the IOC had declined to exclude New Zealand because of their participation in South African sporting events during apartheid.

07-17.title: Brazil national football team
07-17.subtitle: Brazil win their fourth World Cup title, defeating Italy 3–2 on penalties.
07-17.text: Brazil win their fourth World Cup title, defeating Italy 3–2 on penalties.

07-17.title: 1998 Papua New Guinea earthquake
07-17.subtitle: The 7.
07-17.text: The 1998 Papua New Guinea earthquake occurred on July 17 with a moment magnitude of 7.0 and a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). The event occurred on a reverse fault near the north coast region of Papua New Guinea, 25 km (16 mi) from the coast near Aitape and caused a large submarine landslide which caused a tsunami that hit the coast, killing between at least 2,183 and 2,700 people and injuring thousands.
07-17.location: Severe earthquake and tsunami near Papua New Guinea

07-17.title: 2006 Pangandaran earthquake and tsunami
07-17.subtitle: The 7.
07-17.text: The 7.7 Mw  Pangandaran tsunami earthquake severely affects the Indonesian island of Java, killing 668 people, and leaving more than 9,000 injured.

07-15.title: Nobel Prize
07-15.subtitle: Eighteen Nobel laureates sign the Mainau Declaration against nuclear weapons, later co-signed by thirty-four others.
07-15.text: Eighteen Nobel laureates sign the Mainau Declaration against nuclear weapons, later co-signed by thirty-four others.

07-15.title: Nicosia
07-15.subtitle: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Greek junta-sponsored nationalists launch a coup d'état, deposing Makarios III and installing Nikos ...
07-15.text: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Greek junta-sponsored nationalists launch a coup d'état, deposing Makarios III and installing Nikos Sampson as the president of Cyprus.

07-15.title: Space Race
07-15.subtitle: Space Race: Apollo–Soyuz Test Project features the dual launch of an Apollo spacecraft and a Soyuz spacecraft on the fir...
07-15.text: The Space Race was a 20th-century competition between the Cold War rivals, the United States and the Soviet Union, to achieve superior spaceflight capability. It had its origins in the ballistic missile-based nuclear arms race between the two nations following World War II and the onset of the Cold War.

07-15.title: Space Shuttle program
07-15.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Endeavour is launched on STS-127 to complete assembly of the International Space Station's Kibō module.
07-15.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-15.title: X
07-15.subtitle: Twitter, later one of the largest social media platforms in the world, is launched.
07-15.text: X, formerly known as Twitter, is an American microblogging and social networking service, headquartered in Bastrop, Texas. It is one of the world's largest social media platforms and one of the most-visited websites. Users can share short text messages, images, and videos in short posts and like other users' content. The platform also includes direct messaging, video and audio calling, bookmarks, lists, communities, Grok chatbot integration, job search, and a social audio feature.

07-20.title: Central Intelligence Agency
07-20.subtitle: The Central Intelligence Agency releases documents under the Freedom of Information Act revealing it had engaged in mind-control experiments.
07-20.text: The Central Intelligence Agency is a civilian foreign intelligence service of the federal government of the United States tasked with advancing national security through collecting and analyzing intelligence from around the world and conducting covert operations. The agency is headquartered in the George Bush Center for Intelligence in Langley, Virginia, and is sometimes metonymously called "Langley".
07-20.location: U.S. intelligence and covert action agency

07-20.title: Cold War
07-20.subtitle: Cold War: In Philadelphia, Harry Gold pleads guilty to spying for the Soviet Union by passing secrets from atomic scientist Klaus Fuchs.
07-20.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
07-20.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

07-20.title: Soyuz MS-13
07-20.subtitle: Soyuz MS-13 is launched to the International Space Station on the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 moon landing.
07-20.text: Soyuz MS-13 is launched to the International Space Station on the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 moon landing.

07-20.title: Special Olympics
07-20.subtitle: The first International Special Olympics Summer Games are held at Soldier Field in Chicago, with about 1,000 athletes with intellectual disabilities.
07-20.text: The first International Special Olympics Summer Games are held at Soldier Field in Chicago, with about 1,000 athletes with intellectual disabilities.

07-20.title: Apollo program
07-20.subtitle: Apollo program: Apollo 11's crew successfully makes the first human landing on the Moon in the Sea of Tranquility.
07-20.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

07-16.title: 1990 Luzon earthquake
07-16.subtitle: The Luzon earthquake strikes the Philippines with an intensity of 7.
07-16.text: The Luzon earthquake strikes the Philippines with an intensity of 7.7, affecting Benguet, Pangasinan, Nueva Ecija, La Union, Aurora, Bataan, Zambales and Tarlac.

07-16.title: 2007 Chūetsu offshore earthquake
07-16.subtitle: An earthquake of magnitude 6.
07-16.text: An earthquake of magnitude 6.8 and 6.6 aftershock occurs off the Niigata coast of Japan killing eight people, injuring at least 800 and damaging a nuclear power plant.

07-16.title: Millennium Park
07-16.subtitle: Millennium Park, considered Chicago's first and most ambitious early 21st-century architectural project, is opened to the public by Mayor Richard M.
07-16.text: Millennium Park, considered Chicago's first and most ambitious early 21st-century architectural project, is opened to the public by Mayor Richard M. Daley.

07-16.title: Manhattan Project
07-16.subtitle: Manhattan Project: The Atomic Age begins when the United States successfully detonates a plutonium-based test nuclear we...
07-16.text: Manhattan Project: The Atomic Age begins when the United States successfully detonates a plutonium-based test nuclear weapon near Alamogordo, New Mexico.

07-16.title: Apollo 11
07-16.subtitle: The Apollo 11 lunar landing mission is launched from Cape Kennedy in Florida, USA.
07-16.text: The Apollo 11 lunar landing mission is launched from Cape Kennedy in Florida, USA.

07-18.title: Human spaceflight
07-18.subtitle: Human spaceflight: Gemini 10 is launched from Cape Kennedy on a 70-hour mission that includes docking with an orbiting Agena target vehicle.
07-18.text: Human spaceflight: Gemini 10 is launched from Cape Kennedy on a 70-hour mission that includes docking with an orbiting Agena target vehicle.

07-18.title: Nadia Comăneci
07-18.subtitle: Nadia Comăneci becomes the first person in Olympic Games history to score a perfect 10 in gymnastics at the 1976 Summer Olympics.
07-18.text: Nadia Elena Comăneci Conner is a retired Romanian gymnast. She is a five-time Olympic gold medalist, all in individual events. In 1976, at age 14, Comăneci was the first gymnast to be awarded a perfect score of 10.0 at the Olympic Games. At the same Games, she earned six more perfect 10s for events en route to winning three gold medals. At the 1980 Summer Olympics in Moscow, Comăneci won two more gold medals and achieved two more perfect 10s.

07-18.title: Les Horribles Cernettes
07-18.subtitle: A picture of Les Horribles Cernettes was taken, which became the first ever photo posted to the World Wide Web.
07-18.text: Les Horribles Cernettes was an all-female parody pop group, self-labelled "the one and only High Energy Rock Band", which was founded by employees of CERN and performed at CERN and other HEP-related events. Their main claim to fame is that a photograph of them was the earliest photographic image shared on the World Wide Web.

07-18.title: Guatemala
07-18.subtitle: Two hundred sixty-eight Guatemalan campesinos ("peasants" or "country people") are slain in the Plan de Sánchez massacre.
07-18.text: Two hundred sixty-eight Guatemalan campesinos ("peasants" or "country people") are slain in the Plan de Sánchez massacre.

07-18.title: Government of Detroit
07-18.subtitle: The Government of Detroit, with up to $20 billion in debt, files for the largest municipal bankruptcy in U.
07-18.text: The Government of Detroit, with up to $20 billion in debt, files for the largest municipal bankruptcy in U.S. history.

07-19.title: Guinea
07-19.subtitle: Guinean President Alpha Condé survives an attempted assassination and coup d'état at his residence in Conakry.
07-19.text: Guinean President Alpha Condé survives an attempted assassination and coup d'état at his residence in Conakry.

07-19.title: Hezbollah
07-19.subtitle: In one of the first militant attacks by Hezbollah, David S.
07-19.text: In one of the first militant attacks by Hezbollah, David S. Dodge, president of the American University of Beirut, is kidnapped.

07-19.title: Joseph A. Walker
07-19.subtitle: Joe Walker flies a North American X-15 to a record altitude of 106,010 meters (347,800 feet) on X-15 Flight 90.
07-19.text: Joe Walker flies a North American X-15 to a record altitude of 106,010 meters (347,800 feet) on X-15 Flight 90. Exceeding an altitude of 100 km, this flight qualifies as a human spaceflight under international convention.

07-19.title: Global Positioning System
07-19.subtitle: The world's first Global Positioning System (GPS) signal was transmitted from Navigation Technology Satellite 2 (NTS-2) ...
07-19.text: The world's first Global Positioning System (GPS) signal was transmitted from Navigation Technology Satellite 2 (NTS-2) and received at Rockwell Collins in Cedar Rapids, Iowa, at 12:41 a.m. Eastern time (ET).

07-19.title: Dhofar Rebellion
07-19.subtitle: Dhofar Rebellion: British SAS units help the Omani government against Popular Front for the Liberation of Oman rebels in the Battle of Mirbat.
07-19.text: Dhofar Rebellion: British SAS units help the Omani government against Popular Front for the Liberation of Oman rebels in the Battle of Mirbat.

07-22.title: Chandrayaan-2
07-22.subtitle: Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 ...
07-22.text: Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 is launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre in a GSLV Mark III M1. It consists of a lunar orbiter, and also included the Vikram lander, and the Pragyan lunar rover.

07-22.title: Soviet space dogs
07-22.subtitle: Soviet space dogs: Dezik and Tsygan were launched into a sub-orbital spaceflight from Kapustin Yar and became the first ...
07-22.text: Soviet space dogs: Dezik and Tsygan were launched into a sub-orbital spaceflight from Kapustin Yar and became the first dogs to fly in space and the first to safely return.

07-22.title: Iraq War
07-22.subtitle: Iraq War: Members of 101st Airborne of the United States, aided by Special Forces, attack a compound in Iraq, killing Sa...
07-22.text: Iraq War: Members of 101st Airborne of the United States, aided by Special Forces, attack a compound in Iraq, killing Saddam Hussein's sons Uday and Qusay, along with Mustapha Hussein, Qusay's 14-year-old son, and a bodyguard.

07-22.title: Mariner program
07-22.subtitle: Mariner program: Mariner 1 spacecraft flies erratically several minutes after launch and has to be destroyed.
07-22.text: Mariner program: Mariner 1 spacecraft flies erratically several minutes after launch and has to be destroyed.

07-22.title: Norway attacks
07-22.subtitle: Norway attacks: A bomb explodes, targeted at government buildings in central Oslo, followed by a massacre at a youth camp on the island of Utøya.
07-22.text: On 22 July 2011, far-right extremist Anders Behring Breivik committed two domestic terrorist attacks in Norway against the government, the civilian population, and a Workers' Youth League (AUF) summer camp, in which a total of 77 people were killed.
07-22.location: Two domestic terrorist attacks in Norway

07-21.title: NASA
07-21.subtitle: NASA's Space Shuttle program ends with the landing of Space Shuttle Atlantis on mission STS-135 at NASA's Kennedy Space Center.
07-21.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

07-21.title: Apollo program
07-21.subtitle: Apollo program: At 02:56 UTC, astronaut Neil Armstrong becomes the first person to walk on the Moon, followed 19 minutes later by Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin.
07-21.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

07-21.title: Jay Silverheels
07-21.subtitle: Jay Silverheels, a Mohawk actor, becomes the first Native American to have a star commemorated in the Hollywood Walk of Fame.
07-21.text: Jay Silverheels, a Mohawk actor, becomes the first Native American to have a star commemorated in the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

07-21.title: Barbenheimer
07-21.subtitle: The Barbenheimer phenomenon begins as two major motion pictures, Greta Gerwig's fantasy comedy Barbie and Christopher No...
07-21.text: The Barbenheimer phenomenon begins as two major motion pictures, Greta Gerwig's fantasy comedy Barbie and Christopher Nolan's epic biographical thriller Oppenheimer, are released in theaters on the same day and audiences, instead of creating a rivalry between the extremely dissimilar films, instead attend and praise both as an informal, surreal double feature.

07-21.title: NS Savannah
07-21.subtitle: NS Savannah, the first nuclear-powered cargo-passenger ship, is launched as a showcase for Dwight D.
07-21.text: NS Savannah was the first nuclear-powered merchant ship, launched on July 21, 1959, two years after the Soviet ice-breaker Lenin, the first nuclear-powered civilian vessel. A demonstration project for the potential peacetime uses of nuclear energy, she was built in the late 1950s at a cost of $46.9 million.

07-23.title: Phạm Tuân
07-23.subtitle: Phạm Tuân becomes the first Vietnamese citizen and the first Asian in space when he flies aboard the Soyuz 37 mission as...
07-23.text: Phạm Tuân becomes the first Vietnamese citizen and the first Asian in space when he flies aboard the Soyuz 37 mission as an Intercosmos Research Cosmonaut.

07-23.title: Telstar
07-23.subtitle: Telstar relays the first publicly transmitted, live trans-Atlantic television program, featuring Walter Cronkite.
07-23.text: Telstar relays the first publicly transmitted, live trans-Atlantic television program, featuring Walter Cronkite.

07-23.title: Landsat 1
07-23.subtitle: The United States launches Landsat 1, the first Earth-resources satellite.
07-23.text: The United States launches Landsat 1, the first Earth-resources satellite.

07-23.subtitle: NASA announces discovery of Kepler-452b by the Kepler space telescope.
07-23.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

07-23.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
07-23.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-93, with Eileen Collins becoming the first female space shuttle commander.
07-23.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

07-25.title: Egypt–Israel peace treaty
07-25.subtitle: In accord with the Egypt–Israel peace treaty, Israel begins its withdrawal from the Sinai Peninsula.
07-25.text: In accord with the Egypt–Israel peace treaty, Israel begins its withdrawal from the Sinai Peninsula.

07-25.title: Black July
07-25.subtitle: Black July: Thirty-seven Tamil political prisoners at the Welikada high security prison in Colombo are massacred by the fellow Sinhalese prisoners.
07-25.text: Black July: Thirty-seven Tamil political prisoners at the Welikada high security prison in Colombo are massacred by the fellow Sinhalese prisoners.

07-25.subtitle: Salyut 7 cosmonaut Svetlana Savitskaya becomes the first woman to perform a space walk.
07-25.text: Salyut 7 cosmonaut Svetlana Savitskaya becomes the first woman to perform a space walk.

07-25.title: Cold War
07-25.subtitle: Cold War: In a speech John F.
07-25.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
07-25.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

07-25.title: Soviet Union
07-25.subtitle: Soviet Mars 5 space probe is launched.
07-25.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

07-24.title: American National Exhibition
07-24.subtitle: At the opening of the American National Exhibition in Moscow, U.
07-24.text: At the opening of the American National Exhibition in Moscow, U.S. vice president Richard Nixon and Soviet premier Nikita Khrushchev have a "Kitchen Debate".

07-24.title: Quietly Confident Quartet
07-24.subtitle: The Quietly Confident Quartet of Australia wins the men's 4 x 100 metre medley relay at the Moscow Olympics, the only ti...
07-24.text: The Quietly Confident Quartet of Australia wins the men's 4 x 100 metre medley relay at the Moscow Olympics, the only time the United States has not won the swimming event at Olympic level.

07-24.title: Watergate scandal
07-24.subtitle: Watergate scandal: The United States Supreme Court unanimously ruled that President Richard Nixon did not have the autho...
07-24.text: Watergate scandal: The United States Supreme Court unanimously ruled that President Richard Nixon did not have the authority to withhold subpoenaed White House tapes and they order him to surrender the tapes to the Watergate special prosecutor.

07-24.title: Apollo program
07-24.subtitle: Apollo program: Apollo 11 splashes down safely in the Pacific Ocean.
07-24.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

07-24.subtitle: The ship Bluenose II was launched in Lunenburg, Nova Scotia.
07-24.text: The ship Bluenose II was launched in Lunenburg, Nova Scotia. The schooner is a major Canadian symbol.

07-27.title: Byelorussian Soviet Socialist Republic
07-27.subtitle: The Byelorussian Soviet Republic declares independence from the Soviet Union, becoming the Republic of Belarus.
07-27.text: The Byelorussian Soviet Republic declares independence from the Soviet Union, becoming the Republic of Belarus. Until 1996, the day is celebrated as the Independence Day of Belarus; after a referendum held that year, the celebration of independence is moved to June 3.

07-27.text: Watergate scandal: The House of Representatives Judiciary Committee votes 27 to 11 to recommend the first article of impeachment (for obstruction of justice) against President Richard Nixon.

07-27.subtitle: The Austrian State Treaty restores Austrian sovereignty.
07-27.text: The Austrian State Treaty restores Austrian sovereignty.

07-27.subtitle: Black July: Eighteen Tamil political prisoners at the Welikada high security prison in Colombo are massacred by Sinhales...
07-27.text: Black July: Eighteen Tamil political prisoners at the Welikada high security prison in Colombo are massacred by Sinhalese prisoners, the second such massacre in two days.

07-27.title: STS-114

07-27.text: After an incident during STS-114, NASA grounds the Space Shuttle, pending an investigation of the continuing problem with the shedding of foam insulation from the external fuel tank.

07-30.text: England defeats West Germany to win the FIFA World Cup at Wembley Stadium 4–2 after extra time.

07-30.text: A magnitude 8.8 earthquake hits Russia, causing tsunamis over the Pacific Ocean.

07-30.text: The Trans-Canada Highway, the then longest national highway in the world, is officially opened.

07-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Challenger launches on STS-51-F.
07-29.text: Space Shuttle Challenger launches on STS-51-F. The shuttle ends up in a lower orbit than planned due to an engine failure during ascent.

07-29.title: Olympic Games
07-29.subtitle: Olympic Games: The Games of the XIV Olympiad: After a hiatus of 12 years caused by World War II, the first Summer Olympi...
07-29.text: Olympic Games: The Games of the XIV Olympiad: After a hiatus of 12 years caused by World War II, the first Summer Olympics to be held since the 1936 Summer Olympics in Berlin, open in London.

07-29.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
07-29.subtitle: U.
07-29.text: U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower signs into law the National Aeronautics and Space Act, which creates the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA).

07-29.title: International Atomic Energy Agency
07-29.subtitle: The International Atomic Energy Agency is established.
07-29.text: The International Atomic Energy Agency is established.

07-29.title: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370
07-29.subtitle: The first piece of suspected debris from Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 is discovered on Réunion Island.
07-29.text: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 was an international passenger flight operated by Malaysia Airlines that disappeared from radar on 8 March 2014, while flying from Kuala Lumpur International Airport in Malaysia to its planned destination, Beijing Capital International Airport in China. The cause of its disappearance has not been determined. It is widely regarded as the greatest mystery in aviation history, and remains the single deadliest case of aircraft disappearance.

07-31.title: Discovery Program
07-31.subtitle: Discovery Program: Lunar Prospector: NASA intentionally crashes the spacecraft into the Moon, thus ending its mission to...
07-31.text: Discovery Program: Lunar Prospector: NASA intentionally crashes the spacecraft into the Moon, thus ending its mission to detect frozen water on the Moon's surface.

07-31.title: Space Shuttle program
07-31.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Atlantis is launched on STS-46 to deploy the European Retrievable Carrier and the Tethered Satellite System.
07-31.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

07-31.subtitle: Apollo program: the Apollo 15 astronauts become the first to ride in a lunar rover.

Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

07-31.title: The Troubles
07-31.subtitle: The Troubles: In Operation Motorman, the British Army re-takes the urban no-go areas of Northern Ireland.
07-31.text: The Troubles: In Operation Motorman, the British Army re-takes the urban no-go areas of Northern Ireland. It is the biggest British military operation since the Suez Crisis of 1956, and the biggest in Ireland since the Irish War of Independence. Later that day, nine civilians are killed by car bombs in the village of Claudy.

07-31.title: Ranger program
07-31.subtitle: Ranger program: Ranger 7 sends back the first close-up photographs of the moon, with images 1,000 times clearer than any...
07-31.text: Ranger program: Ranger 7 sends back the first close-up photographs of the moon, with images 1,000 times clearer than anything ever seen from earth-bound telescopes.

07-28.title: 1976 Tangshan earthquake
07-28.subtitle: The Tangshan earthquake measuring between 7.
07-28.text: The Tangshan earthquake measuring between 7.8 and 8.2 moment magnitude flattens Tangshan in the People's Republic of China, killing 242,769 and injuring 164,851.

07-28.title: Airblue Flight 202
07-28.subtitle: Airblue Flight 202 crashes into the Margalla Hills north of Islamabad, Pakistan, killing all 152 people aboard.
07-28.text: Airblue Flight 202 crashes into the Margalla Hills north of Islamabad, Pakistan, killing all 152 people aboard. It is the deadliest aviation accident in Pakistan history and the first involving an Airbus A321.

07-28.title: Prehistory
07-28.subtitle: The remains of a prehistoric man are discovered near Kennewick, Washington.
07-28.text: The remains of a prehistoric man are discovered near Kennewick, Washington. Such remains will be known as the Kennewick Man.

08-04.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
08-04.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

08-04.text: The civil rights movement was a social movement in the United States from 1954 to 1968 which aimed to abolish legalized racial segregation, discrimination, and disenfranchisement in the country, which most commonly affected African Americans. The movement had origins in the Reconstruction era in the late 19th century, and modern roots in the 1940s.

08-05.title: Red Guards

08-05.text: A group of red guards at Experimental High in Beijing, including Deng Rong and Liu Pingping, daughters of Deng Xiaoping and Liu Shaoqi respectively, beat the deputy vice principal, Bian Zhongyun, to death with sticks after accusing her of counter-revolutionary revisionism, producing one of the first fatalities of the Cultural Revolution.

08-05.text: Australia's second most populous state Victoria enters its sixth COVID-19 lockdown, enacting stage four restrictions statewide in reaction to six new COVID-19 cases recorded that morning.

08-05.text: Watergate scandal: President Richard Nixon, under orders of the US Supreme Court, releases the "Smoking Gun" tape, recorded on June 23, 1972, clearly revealing his actions in covering up and interfering investigations into the break-in. His political support vanishes completely.

08-06.title: World War II

08-06.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

08-06.title: Gulf War
08-06.subtitle: Gulf War: The United Nations Security Council orders a global trade embargo against Iraq in response to Iraq's invasion of Kuwait.
08-06.text: The Gulf War was an armed conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition led by the United States. The coalition's efforts were in two phases: Operation Desert Shield, which marked the military buildup from August 1990 to January 1991; and Operation Desert Storm, from the bombing campaign against Iraq on 17 January until the American-led liberation of Kuwait on 28 February.

08-01.title: Cultural Revolution

08-01.text: The Cultural Revolution, formally known as the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution, was a sociopolitical movement in the People's Republic of China (PRC). It was launched by CCP chairman Mao Zedong in 1966 and lasted until his death in 1976. Its stated goal was to preserve Chinese communism by purging remnants of capitalist and traditional elements from Chinese society.
08-01.location: Period of sociopolitical turmoil in China (1966–1976)
