08-08.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: STS-28 Mission: Space Shuttle Columbia takes off on a secret five-day military mission.
08-08.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

08-02.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
08-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-43 to deploy the TDRS-5 satellite.
08-02.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

08-02.title: Iraqi invasion of Kuwait
08-02.subtitle: Iraqi invasion of Kuwait: Iraq invades Kuwait and temporarily establishes the Republic of Kuwait puppet state on the ord...
08-02.text: Iraqi invasion of Kuwait: Iraq invades Kuwait and temporarily establishes the Republic of Kuwait puppet state on the orders of Saddam Hussein, eventually leading to the Gulf War.

08-02.title: Helsinki Metro
08-02.subtitle: The Helsinki Metro, the first rapid transit system of Finland, is opened to the general public.
08-02.text: The Helsinki Metro, the first rapid transit system of Finland, is opened to the general public.

08-02.title: Pakistan
08-02.subtitle: Pakistan is re-admitted to the Commonwealth of Nations after having restored democracy for the first time since 1972.
08-02.text: Pakistan is re-admitted to the Commonwealth of Nations after having restored democracy for the first time since 1972.

08-02.title: 1989 Valvettiturai massacre
08-02.subtitle: A massacre is carried out by an Indian Peace Keeping Force in Sri Lanka killing 64 ethnic Tamil civilians.
08-02.text: A massacre is carried out by an Indian Peace Keeping Force in Sri Lanka killing 64 ethnic Tamil civilians.

08-09.title: Watergate scandal
08-09.subtitle: As a direct result of the Watergate scandal, Richard Nixon becomes the first president of the United States to resign from office.
08-09.text: As a direct result of the Watergate scandal, Richard Nixon becomes the first president of the United States to resign from office. Vice President Gerald Ford becomes president.

08-09.title: World War II
08-09.subtitle: World War II: Continuation War: The Vyborg–Petrozavodsk Offensive, the largest offensive launched by Soviet Union agains...
08-09.text: World War II: Continuation War: The Vyborg–Petrozavodsk Offensive, the largest offensive launched by Soviet Union against Finland during the Second World War, ends to a strategic stalemate. Both Finnish and Soviet troops at the Finnish front dug to defensive positions, and the front remains stable until the end of the war.

08-09.title: Tate–LaBianca murders
08-09.subtitle: Tate–LaBianca murders: Followers of Charles Manson murder pregnant actress Sharon Tate (wife of Roman Polanski), coffee ...
08-09.text: Tate–LaBianca murders: Followers of Charles Manson murder pregnant actress Sharon Tate (wife of Roman Polanski), coffee heiress Abigail Folger, Polish actor Wojciech Frykowski, men's hairstylist Jay Sebring and recent high-school graduate Steven Parent.

08-09.title: World War II
08-09.subtitle: World War II: Nagasaki is devastated when an atomic bomb, Fat Man, is dropped by the United States B-29 Bockscar.
08-09.text: World War II: Nagasaki is devastated when an atomic bomb, Fat Man, is dropped by the United States B-29 Bockscar. Thirty-five thousand people are killed outright, including 23,200–28,200 Japanese war workers, 2,000 Korean forced workers, and 150 Japanese soldiers.

08-09.title: The Troubles
08-09.subtitle: The Troubles: In Northern Ireland, the British authorities launch Operation Demetrius.
08-09.text: The Troubles: In Northern Ireland, the British authorities launch Operation Demetrius. The operation involves the mass arrest and internment without trial of individuals suspected of being affiliated with the Irish Republican Army (PIRA). Mass riots follow, and thousands of people flee or are forced out of their homes.

08-10.title: World War I
08-10.subtitle: World War I: Ottoman sultan Mehmed VI's representatives sign the Treaty of Sèvres that divides up the Ottoman Empire between the Allies.
08-10.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

08-10.title: Murder of Adam Walsh
08-10.subtitle: Murder of Adam Walsh: The head of John Walsh's son is found.
08-10.text: Murder of Adam Walsh: The head of John Walsh's son is found. This inspires the creation of the television series America's Most Wanted and the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children.

08-10.title: Space Shuttle program
08-10.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-105 to the International Space Station, carrying t...
08-10.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

08-10.title: Japanese American internment
08-10.subtitle: Japanese American internment: U.
08-10.text: During World War II, the United States forcibly relocated and incarcerated about 120,000 people of Japanese descent in ten concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority (WRA), mostly in the western interior of the country. About two-thirds were U.S. citizens. These actions were initiated by Executive Order 9066, issued by President Franklin D. Roosevelt on February 19, 1942, following Imperial Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941. About 127,000 Japanese Americans then lived in the continental U.S.
08-10.location: Mass incarceration in the U.S. during WWII

08-10.title: First Indochina War
08-10.subtitle: First Indochina War: The French Union withdraws its forces from Operation Camargue against the Viet Minh in central Vietnam.
08-10.text: First Indochina War: The French Union withdraws its forces from Operation Camargue against the Viet Minh in central Vietnam.

08-11.title: Vostok 3 and 4
08-11.subtitle: Vostok 3 launches from the Baikonur Cosmodrome and cosmonaut Andrian Nikolayev becomes the first person to float in microgravity.
08-11.text: Vostok 3 launches from the Baikonur Cosmodrome and cosmonaut Andrian Nikolayev becomes the first person to float in microgravity.

08-11.title: Apollo 11
08-11.subtitle: The Apollo 11 astronauts are released from a three-week quarantine following their liftoff from the Moon.
08-11.text: The Apollo 11 astronauts are released from a three-week quarantine following their liftoff from the Moon.

08-11.title: East Timor
08-11.subtitle: East Timor: Governor Mário Lemos Pires of Portuguese Timor abandons the capital Dili, following a coup by the Timorese D...
08-11.text: East Timor: Governor Mário Lemos Pires of Portuguese Timor abandons the capital Dili, following a coup by the Timorese Democratic Union (UDT) and the outbreak of civil war between UDT and Fretilin.

08-11.title: Latvian–Soviet Peace Treaty
08-11.subtitle: The Latvian–Soviet Peace Treaty, which relinquished Russia's authority and pretenses to Latvia, is signed, ending the Latvian War of Independence.
08-11.text: The Latvian–Soviet Peace Treaty, which relinquished Russia's authority and pretenses to Latvia, is signed, ending the Latvian War of Independence.

08-11.title: NATO
08-11.subtitle: NATO takes over command of the peacekeeping force in Afghanistan, marking its first major operation outside Europe in its 54-year-history.
08-11.text: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) is an intergovernmental military alliance between 32 member states—30 in Europe and 2 in North America. Founded in the aftermath of World War II, NATO was established with the signing of the North Atlantic Treaty in 1949. The organization serves as a system of collective security, whereby its independent member states agree to mutual defence in response to an attack by any outside party.

08-12.title: Project Echo
08-12.subtitle: Echo 1A, NASA's first successful communications satellite, is launched.
08-12.text: Echo 1A, NASA's first successful communications satellite, is launched.

08-12.title: Sue
08-12.subtitle: Sue, the largest and most complete Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton found to date, is discovered by Sue Hendrickson in South Dakota.
08-12.text: Sue, the largest and most complete Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton found to date, is discovered by Sue Hendrickson in South Dakota.

08-12.title: South Africa
08-12.subtitle: South Africa is banned from the Olympic Games due to the country's racist policies.
08-12.text: South Africa is banned from the Olympic Games due to the country's racist policies.

08-12.title: Approach and Landing Tests
08-12.subtitle: The first free flight of the Space Shuttle Enterprise.
08-12.text: The first free flight of the Space Shuttle Enterprise.

08-12.title: Thermonuclear bomb testing
08-12.subtitle: Thermonuclear bomb testing: The Soviet atomic bomb project continues with the detonation of "RDS-6s" (Joe 4) using a "layered" scheme.
08-12.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
08-12.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

08-14.title: 2021 Haiti earthquake
08-14.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
08-14.text: A magnitude 7.2 earthquake strikes southwestern Haiti, killing at least 2,248 people and causing a humanitarian crisis.

08-14.title: Helios Airways Flight 522
08-14.subtitle: Helios Airways Flight 522, en route from Larnaca, Cyprus to Prague, Czech Republic via Athens, crashes in the hills near...
08-14.text: Helios Airways Flight 522, en route from Larnaca, Cyprus to Prague, Czech Republic via Athens, crashes in the hills near Grammatiko, Greece, killing 121 passengers and crew.

08-14.title: Lebanon War
08-14.subtitle: Lebanon War: A ceasefire takes effect three days after the United Nations Security Council's approval of United Nations ...
08-14.text: Lebanon War: A ceasefire takes effect three days after the United Nations Security Council's approval of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701, formally ending hostilities between Lebanon and Israel.

08-14.title: Pakistan
08-14.subtitle: Pakistan gains independence from the British Empire as the Dominion of Pakistan, due to the partition of India.
08-14.text: Pakistan gains independence from the British Empire as the Dominion of Pakistan, due to the partition of India.

08-14.title: Ponte Morandi collapse
08-14.subtitle: The collapse of the Ponte Morandi bridge in Genoa, Italy, left 16 people injured and 43 people killed.
08-14.text: The collapse of the Ponte Morandi bridge in Genoa, Italy, left 16 people injured and 43 people killed.

08-13.title: Radio Pakistan
08-13.subtitle: Radio Pakistan broadcasts the "Qaumī Tarāna", the national anthem of Pakistan for the first time.
08-13.text: Radio Pakistan broadcasts the "Qaumī Tarāna", the national anthem of Pakistan for the first time.

08-13.title: Cold War
08-13.subtitle: Cold War: East Germany closes the border between the eastern and western sectors of Berlin to thwart its inhabitants' at...
08-13.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
08-13.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

08-13.title: Apollo 11
08-13.subtitle: The Apollo 11 astronauts enjoy a ticker-tape parade in New York City.
08-13.text: The Apollo 11 astronauts enjoy a ticker-tape parade in New York City. That evening, at a state dinner in Los Angeles, they are awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by U.S. President Richard Nixon.

08-13.title: Democratic Republic of the Congo
08-13.subtitle: One hundred fifty-six Congolese Tutsi refugees are massacred at the Gatumba refugee camp in Burundi.
08-13.text: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), also known as the DR Congo, Congo-Kinshasa, or simply the Congo, or less often Zaire, is a country in Central Africa. By land area, it is the second-largest country in Africa and the 11th-largest in the world. With a population of around 112 million people, the Democratic Republic of the Congo is the fourth-most populous in Africa and most populous Francophone country in the world.

08-13.title: Central African Republic
08-13.subtitle: The Central African Republic declares independence from France.
08-13.text: The Central African Republic declares independence from France.

08-16.title: Vietnam War
08-16.subtitle: Vietnam War: A coup d'état replaces Dương Văn Minh with General Nguyễn Khánh as President of South Vietnam.
08-16.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
08-16.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

08-16.title: Commonwealth Games
08-16.subtitle: The first British Empire Games are opened in Hamilton, Ontario, by the Governor General of Canada, the Viscount Willingdon.
08-16.text: The first British Empire Games are opened in Hamilton, Ontario, by the Governor General of Canada, the Viscount Willingdon.

08-16.title: Joseph Kittinger
08-16.subtitle: Joseph Kittinger parachutes from a balloon over New Mexico, United States, at 102,800 feet (31,300 m), setting three rec...
08-16.text: Joseph Kittinger parachutes from a balloon over New Mexico, United States, at 102,800 feet (31,300 m), setting three records that held until 2012: High-altitude jump, free fall, and highest speed by a human without an aircraft.

08-16.title: Coup d'état
08-16.subtitle: In an unsuccessful coup d'état attempt, the Royal Moroccan Air Force fires upon Hassan II of Morocco's plane while he is traveling back to Rabat.
08-16.text: In an unsuccessful coup d'état attempt, the Royal Moroccan Air Force fires upon Hassan II of Morocco's plane while he is traveling back to Rabat.

08-16.title: Solar particle event
08-16.subtitle: A solar particle event affects computers at the Toronto Stock Exchange, forcing a halt to trading.
08-16.text: A solar particle event affects computers at the Toronto Stock Exchange, forcing a halt to trading.

08-15.title: Takeo Miki
08-15.subtitle: Takeo Miki makes the first official pilgrimage to Yasukuni Shrine by an incumbent prime minister on the anniversary of the end of World War II.
08-15.text: Takeo Miki makes the first official pilgrimage to Yasukuni Shrine by an incumbent prime minister on the anniversary of the end of World War II.
08-15.location: Prime Minister of Japan from 1974 to 1976

08-15.title: Ohio State University Radio Observatory
08-15.subtitle: The Big Ear, a radio telescope operated by Ohio State University as part of the SETI project, receives a radio signal fr...
08-15.text: The Big Ear, a radio telescope operated by Ohio State University as part of the SETI project, receives a radio signal from deep space; the event is named the "Wow! signal" from the notation made by a volunteer on the project.

08-15.title: Hirohito
08-15.subtitle: Emperor Hirohito broadcasts his declaration of surrender following the effective surrender of Japan in World War II; Kor...
08-15.text: Emperor Hirohito broadcasts his declaration of surrender following the effective surrender of Japan in World War II; Korea gains independence from the Empire of Japan.

08-15.title: Yuk Young-soo
08-15.subtitle: Yuk Young-soo, First Lady of South Korea, is killed during an apparent assassination attempt upon President Park Chung Hee.
08-15.text: Yuk Young-soo, First Lady of South Korea, is killed during an apparent assassination attempt upon President Park Chung Hee.

08-15.image_credit: 800px-陆英修参加红十字缝纫志愿者活动.jpg - Wikimedia Commons

08-15.title: Smithsonian Institution
08-15.subtitle: The Smithsonian announces the discovery of the olinguito, the first new carnivorous species found in the Americas in 35 years.
08-15.text: The Smithsonian announces the discovery of the olinguito, the first new carnivorous species found in the Americas in 35 years.

08-20.title: NS Savannah
08-20.subtitle: The NS Savannah, the world's first nuclear-powered civilian ship, embarks on its maiden voyage.
08-20.text: NS Savannah was the first nuclear-powered merchant ship, launched on July 21, 1959, two years after the Soviet ice-breaker Lenin, the first nuclear-powered civilian vessel. A demonstration project for the potential peacetime uses of nuclear energy, she was built in the late 1950s at a cost of $46.9 million.

08-20.title: Viking program
08-20.subtitle: Viking program: NASA launches the Viking 1 planetary probe toward Mars.
08-20.text: Viking program: NASA launches the Viking 1 planetary probe toward Mars.

08-20.title: Voyager program
08-20.subtitle: Voyager program: NASA launches the Voyager 2 spacecraft.
08-20.text: Voyager program: NASA launches the Voyager 2 spacecraft.

08-20.title: 1998 United States embassy bombings
08-20.subtitle: U.
08-20.text: U.S. embassy bombings: The United States launches cruise missile attacks against alleged al-Qaeda camps in Afghanistan and a suspected chemical weapons plant in Sudan in retaliation for the August 7 bombings of American embassies in Kenya and Tanzania.

08-20.title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
08-20.subtitle: Dissolution of the Soviet Union, August Coup: More than 100,000 people rally outside the Soviet Union's parliament build...
08-20.text: Dissolution of the Soviet Union, August Coup: More than 100,000 people rally outside the Soviet Union's parliament building protesting the coup aiming to depose President Mikhail Gorbachev.

08-17.title: Soviet Union Venera program
08-17.subtitle: Soviet Union Venera program: Venera 7 launched.
08-17.text: Soviet Union Venera program: Venera 7 launched. It will become the first spacecraft to successfully transmit data from the surface of another planet (Venus).

08-17.title: Pioneer 0
08-17.subtitle: Pioneer 0, America's first attempt at lunar orbit, is launched using the first Thor-Able rocket and fails.
08-17.text: Pioneer 0, America's first attempt at lunar orbit, is launched using the first Thor-Able rocket and fails. Notable as one of the first attempted launches beyond Earth orbit by any country.

08-17.title: 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake
08-17.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
08-17.text: The 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake and tsunami occurred on August 17, 1976, at 00:11 local time near the islands of Mindanao and Sulu, in the Philippines. It measured 8.0 on the moment magnitude scale occurring at a depth of 20 km (12 mi). The earthquake was accompanied by a destructive tsunami that resulted in a majority of the estimated 5,000 to 8,000 fatalities. It was the deadliest and strongest earthquake in the Philippines in 58 years since the 1918 Celebes Sea earthquake.

08-17.title: Sukarno
08-17.subtitle: Sukarno and Mohammad Hatta proclaim the independence of Indonesia, igniting the Indonesian National Revolution against the Dutch Empire.
08-17.text: Sukarno and Mohammad Hatta proclaim the independence of Indonesia, igniting the Indonesian National Revolution against the Dutch Empire.

08-17.title: World War II
08-17.subtitle: World War II: The U.
08-17.text: The Seventh Army was a United States army created during World War II that evolved into the United States Army Europe (USAREUR) during the 1950s and 1960s. It served in North Africa and Italy in the Mediterranean Theater of Operations and France and Germany in the European Theater between 1942 and 1945.
08-17.location: Part of the U.S. Army, active intermittently between 1943 and 2010

08-21.title: NASA
08-21.subtitle: NASA loses contact with the Mars Observer spacecraft.
08-21.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

08-21.title: Lebanese Civil War
08-21.subtitle: Lebanese Civil War: The first troops of a multinational force lands in Beirut to oversee the Palestine Liberation Organi...
08-21.text: Lebanese Civil War: The first troops of a multinational force lands in Beirut to oversee the Palestine Liberation Organization's withdrawal from Lebanon.

08-21.title: Socialist Republic of Romania
08-21.subtitle: The Socialist Republic of Romania is proclaimed, following the adoption of a new constitution.
08-21.text: The Socialist Republic of Romania is proclaimed, following the adoption of a new constitution.

08-21.title: Xá Lợi Pagoda raids
08-21.subtitle: Xá Lợi Pagoda raids: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam Special Forces loyal to Ngô Đình Nhu, brother of President Ngo ...
08-21.text: Xá Lợi Pagoda raids: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam Special Forces loyal to Ngô Đình Nhu, brother of President Ngo Dinh Diem, vandalizes Buddhist pagodas across the country, arresting thousands and leaving an estimated hundreds dead.

08-21.title: Cold War
08-21.subtitle: Cold War: Nicolae Ceaușescu, leader of the Socialist Republic of Romania, publicly condemns the Soviet-led Warsaw Pact i...
08-21.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
08-21.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

08-18.title: Brojen Das
08-18.subtitle: Brojen Das from Bangladesh swims across the English Channel in a competition as the first Bengali and the first Asian to...
08-18.text: Brojen Das from Bangladesh swims across the English Channel in a competition as the first Bengali and the first Asian to do so, placing first among the 39 competitors.

08-18.title: Steve Biko
08-18.subtitle: Steve Biko is arrested at a police roadblock under Terrorism Act No.
08-18.text: Steve Biko is arrested at a police roadblock under Terrorism Act No. 83 of 1967 in King William's Town, South Africa. He later dies from injuries sustained during this arrest, bringing attention to South Africa's apartheid policies.

08-18.title: 2005 Java–Bali blackout
08-18.subtitle: A massive power blackout hits the Indonesian island of Java; affecting almost 100 million people, it is one of the large...
08-18.text: A massive power blackout hits the Indonesian island of Java; affecting almost 100 million people, it is one of the largest and most widespread power outages in history.

08-18.title: Civil rights movement
08-18.subtitle: Civil rights movement: James Meredith becomes the first African American to graduate from the University of Mississippi.
08-18.text: The civil rights movement was a social movement in the United States from 1954 to 1968 which aimed to abolish legalized racial segregation, discrimination, and disenfranchisement in the country, which most commonly affected African Americans. The movement had origins in the Reconstruction era in the late 19th century, and modern roots in the 1940s.

08-18.title: Vietnam War
08-18.subtitle: Vietnam War: Operation Starlite begins: United States Marines destroy a Viet Cong stronghold on the Van Tuong peninsula ...
08-18.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
08-18.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

08-22.title: Nicaraguan Revolution
08-22.subtitle: Nicaraguan Revolution: The FSLN seizes the National Congress of Nicaragua, along with over a thousand hostages.
08-22.text: Nicaraguan Revolution: The FSLN seizes the National Congress of Nicaragua, along with over a thousand hostages.

08-22.title: Juan Marichal
08-22.subtitle: Juan Marichal, pitcher for the San Francisco Giants, strikes John Roseboro, catcher for the Los Angeles Dodgers, on the ...
08-22.text: Juan Marichal, pitcher for the San Francisco Giants, strikes John Roseboro, catcher for the Los Angeles Dodgers, on the head with a bat, sparking a 14-minute brawl, one of the most violent on-field incidents in sports history.

08-22.title: Iceland
08-22.subtitle: Iceland is the first nation in the world to recognize the independence of the Baltic states.
08-22.text: Iceland is a Nordic island country between the Arctic Ocean and the North Atlantic Ocean, located on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge between Europe and North America. It is culturally and politically linked with Europe and is the region's westernmost and most sparsely populated country. Its capital and largest city is Reykjavík, which is home to about 36% of the country's roughly 390,000 residents. The official language of the country is Icelandic.
08-22.location: Island country in the Atlantic Ocean

08-22.title: Pope Paul VI
08-22.subtitle: Pope Paul VI arrives in Bogotá, Colombia.
08-22.text: Pope Paul VI was head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from 21 June 1963 until his death on 6 August 1978. Succeeding John XXIII, he continued the Second Vatican Council, which he closed in 1965, implementing its numerous reforms. He fostered improved ecumenical relations with Eastern Orthodox and Protestant churches, which resulted in many historic meetings and agreements.
08-22.location: Head of the Catholic Church from 1963 to 1978

08-22.title: Nolan Ryan
08-22.subtitle: Nolan Ryan strikes out Rickey Henderson to become the first Major League Baseball pitcher to record 5,000 strikeouts.
08-22.text: Nolan Ryan strikes out Rickey Henderson to become the first Major League Baseball pitcher to record 5,000 strikeouts.

08-24.title: 2014 South Napa earthquake
08-24.subtitle: A magnitude 6.
08-24.text: A magnitude 6.0 earthquake strikes the San Francisco Bay Area; it is the largest in that area since 1989.

08-24.title: Buddhist crisis
08-24.subtitle: Buddhist crisis: As a result of the Xá Lợi Pagoda raids, the US State Department cables the United States Embassy, Saigo...
08-24.text: Buddhist crisis: As a result of the Xá Lợi Pagoda raids, the US State Department cables the United States Embassy, Saigon to encourage Army of the Republic of Vietnam generals to launch a coup against President Ngô Đình Diệm if he did not remove his brother Ngô Đình Nhu.

08-24.title: List of government space agencies
08-24.subtitle: The National Space Agency of Taiwan successfully launches the observation satellite Formosat-5 into space.
08-24.text: The National Space Agency of Taiwan successfully launches the observation satellite Formosat-5 into space.

08-24.title: Discharge of radioactive water of the
08-24.subtitle: Japan officially begins discharging treated radioactive water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the Pa...
08-24.text: Japan officially begins discharging treated radioactive water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the Pacific Ocean, sparking international concerns and condemnation.

08-24.title: Microsoft
08-24.subtitle: Microsoft releases Windows 95 to the public in North America.
08-24.text: Microsoft releases Windows 95 to the public in North America.

08-25.title: Victory over Japan Day
08-25.subtitle: Ten days after World War II ends with Japan announcing its surrender, armed supporters of the Chinese Communist Party kill U.
08-25.text: Ten days after World War II ends with Japan announcing its surrender, armed supporters of the Chinese Communist Party kill U.S. intelligence officer John Birch, regarded by some of the American right as the first victim of the Cold War.

08-25.title: Voyager 2
08-25.subtitle: Voyager 2 spacecraft makes its closest approach to Neptune, the last planet in the Solar System at the time, due to Plut...
08-25.text: Voyager 2 is a space probe launched by NASA on August 20, 1977, as a part of the Voyager program. It was launched on a trajectory towards the gas giants and enabled further encounters with the ice giants. The only spacecraft to have visited either of the ice giant planets, it was the third of five spacecraft to achieve Solar escape velocity, which allowed it to leave the Solar System.

08-25.title: Voyager 2
08-25.subtitle: Voyager 2 spacecraft makes its closest approach to Saturn.
08-25.text: Voyager 2 is a space probe launched by NASA on August 20, 1977, as a part of the Voyager program. It was launched on a trajectory towards the gas giants and enabled further encounters with the ice giants. The only spacecraft to have visited either of the ice giant planets, it was the third of five spacecraft to achieve Solar escape velocity, which allowed it to leave the Solar System.

08-25.title: NASA
08-25.subtitle: NASA successfully launches the Spitzer Space Telescope into space.
08-25.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

08-25.title: Voyager 1
08-25.subtitle: Voyager 1 spacecraft enters interstellar space, becoming the first man-made object to do so.
08-25.text: Voyager 1 spacecraft enters interstellar space, becoming the first man-made object to do so.

08-26.title: Kidnapping of Jaycee Dugard
08-26.subtitle: Kidnapping victim Jaycee Dugard is discovered alive in California after being missing for over 18 years.
08-26.text: Kidnapping victim Jaycee Dugard is discovered alive in California after being missing for over 18 years. Her captors, Phillip and Nancy Garrido are apprehended.

08-26.title: Delta III
08-26.subtitle: The first flight of the Boeing Delta III ends in disaster 75 seconds after liftoff resulting in the loss of the Galaxy X  communications satellite.
08-26.text: The first flight of the Boeing Delta III ends in disaster 75 seconds after liftoff resulting in the loss of the Galaxy X  communications satellite.

08-26.title: World War I
08-26.subtitle: World War I: The German colony of Togoland surrenders to French and British forces after a 20-day campaign.
08-26.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

08-26.title: Second Chechen War
08-26.subtitle: Russia begins the Second Chechen War in response to the Invasion of Dagestan by the Islamic International Peacekeeping Brigade.
08-26.text: Russia begins the Second Chechen War in response to the Invasion of Dagestan by the Islamic International Peacekeeping Brigade.

08-26.title: World War II
08-26.subtitle: World War II: Chad becomes the first French colony to join the Allies under the administration of Félix Éboué, France's first black colonial governor.
08-26.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

08-23.title: World War II
08-23.subtitle: World War II: King Michael of Romania dismisses the pro-Nazi government of Marshal Antonescu, who is later arrested.
08-23.text: World War II: King Michael of Romania dismisses the pro-Nazi government of Marshal Antonescu, who is later arrested. Romania switches sides from the Axis to the Allies.

08-23.title: Chandrayaan-3
08-23.subtitle: Chandrayaan-3 mission initiated first Moon landing in Indian history.
08-23.text: Chandrayaan-3 mission initiated first Moon landing in Indian history.

08-23.title: Singing Revolution
08-23.subtitle: Singing Revolution: Two million people from Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania stand on the Vilnius–Tallinn road, holding hands.
08-23.text: Singing Revolution: Two million people from Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania stand on the Vilnius–Tallinn road, holding hands.

08-23.title: Mexican Americans
08-23.subtitle: Organized by Mexican American labor union leader César Chávez, the Salad Bowl strike, the largest farm worker strike in U.
08-23.text: Organized by Mexican American labor union leader César Chávez, the Salad Bowl strike, the largest farm worker strike in U.S. history, begins.

08-23.title: Lunar Orbiter 1
08-23.subtitle: Lunar Orbiter 1 takes the first photograph of Earth from orbit around the Moon.
08-23.text: Lunar Orbiter 1 takes the first photograph of Earth from orbit around the Moon.

08-19.title: Syncom
08-19.subtitle: Syncom 3, the first geostationary communication satellite, is launched.
08-19.text: Syncom 3, the first geostationary communication satellite, is launched. Two months later, it would enable live coverage of the 1964 Summer Olympics.

08-19.title: Sputnik program
08-19.subtitle: Sputnik program: Korabl-Sputnik 2: The Soviet Union launches the satellite with the dogs Belka and Strelka, 40 mice, two rats and a variety of plants.
08-19.text: Sputnik program: Korabl-Sputnik 2: The Soviet Union launches the satellite with the dogs Belka and Strelka, 40 mice, two rats and a variety of plants.

08-19.title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
08-19.subtitle: Dissolution of the Soviet Union: The August Coup begins when Soviet President Mikhail Gorbachev is placed under house ar...
08-19.text: Dissolution of the Soviet Union: The August Coup begins when Soviet President Mikhail Gorbachev is placed under house arrest while on holiday in the town of Foros, Ukraine.

08-19.title: Prime Minister of Japan
08-19.subtitle: Japanese prime minister Eisaku Satō becomes the first post-World War II sitting prime minister to visit Okinawa Prefecture.
08-19.text: The prime minister of Japan  is the head of government of Japan. The prime minister chairs the Cabinet of Japan and has the ability to select and dismiss its ministers of state. The prime minister also serves as the commander-in-chief of the Japan Self Defence Forces.

08-19.title: Cold War
08-19.subtitle: Cold War: The CIA and MI6 help to overthrow the government of Mohammad Mosaddegh in Iran and reinstate the Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.
08-19.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
08-19.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

08-28.title: United States Senate
08-28.subtitle: U.
08-28.text: U.S. Senator Strom Thurmond begins a filibuster to prevent the United States Senate from voting on the Civil Rights Act of 1957; he stopped speaking 24 hours and 18 minutes later. This would remain the longest speech ever conducted by a single Senator until Cory Booker spoke for 25 hours and five minutes, beginning on March 31, 2025.

08-28.title: ISRO
08-28.subtitle: The first experimental mission of ISRO's Scramjet Engine towards the realisation of an Air Breathing Propulsion System i...
08-28.text: The first experimental mission of ISRO's Scramjet Engine towards the realisation of an Air Breathing Propulsion System is successfully conducted from Satish Dhawan Space Centre SHAR, Sriharikota.

08-28.title: NASA
08-28.subtitle: NASA's Galileo probe performs a flyby of the asteroid 243 Ida.
08-28.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

08-28.title: Chicago Seven
08-28.subtitle: Chicago Seven defendant David Dellinger, antiwar activist Bradford Lyttle, Civil Rights Movement historian Randy Kryn, a...
08-28.text: Chicago Seven defendant David Dellinger, antiwar activist Bradford Lyttle, Civil Rights Movement historian Randy Kryn, and eight others are arrested by the Federal Protective Service while protesting in a demonstration at the Kluczynski Federal Building in downtown Chicago during that year's Democratic National Convention.

08-28.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
08-28.subtitle: NASA's Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-128.
08-28.text: NASA's Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-128.

08-30.title: STS-41-D
08-30.subtitle: STS-41-D: The Space Shuttle Discovery takes off on its maiden voyage.
08-30.text: STS-41-D: The Space Shuttle Discovery takes off on its maiden voyage.

08-30.title: NAMC YS-11
08-30.subtitle: Japan conducts a test of the NAMC YS-11, its first aircraft since World War II and its only successful commercial aircra...
08-30.text: Japan conducts a test of the NAMC YS-11, its first aircraft since World War II and its only successful commercial aircraft from before or after the war.

08-30.title: Thurgood Marshall
08-30.subtitle: Thurgood Marshall is confirmed as the first African American Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.
08-30.text: Thurgood Marshall is confirmed as the first African American Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

08-30.title: Second Congo War
08-30.subtitle: Second Congo War: Armed forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and their Angolan and Zimbabwean allies rec...
08-30.text: Second Congo War: Armed forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and their Angolan and Zimbabwean allies recapture Matadi and the Inga dams in the western DRC from RCD and Rwandan troops.

08-30.title: STS-8
08-30.subtitle: STS-8: The Space Shuttle Challenger takes off on the first night launch of the shuttle program.
08-30.text: STS-8 was the eighth NASA Space Shuttle mission and the third flight of the Space Shuttle Challenger. It launched on August 30, 1983, and landed on September 5, 1983, conducting the first night launch and night landing of the Space Shuttle program. It also carried the first African-American astronaut to go into space, Guion Bluford.

08-29.title: El Tacnazo
08-29.subtitle: El Tacnazo:  Francisco Morales Bermúdez, Peruvian Prime Minister carries out a coup d'état in the city of Tacna, forcing...
08-29.text: El Tacnazo:  Francisco Morales Bermúdez, Peruvian Prime Minister carries out a coup d'état in the city of Tacna, forcing the sitting President of Peru, Juan Velasco Alvarado, to resign and assuming his place as the new President.

08-29.title: Meitnerium
08-29.subtitle: Meitnerium, a synthetic chemical element with the atomic number 109, is first synthesized at the Gesellschaft für Schwer...
08-29.text: Meitnerium, a synthetic chemical element with the atomic number 109, is first synthesized at the Gesellschaft für Schwerionenforschung in Darmstadt, Germany.

08-29.title: Netflix
08-29.subtitle: Netflix is launched as an internet DVD rental service.
08-29.text: Netflix is launched as an internet DVD rental service.

08-29.title: Gemini 5
08-29.subtitle: The Gemini V spacecraft returns to Earth, landing in the Atlantic Ocean.
08-29.text: The Gemini V spacecraft returns to Earth, landing in the Atlantic Ocean.

08-29.title: Sayyid
08-29.subtitle: Sayed Ayatollah Mohammed Baqir al-Hakim, the Shia Muslim leader in Iraq, is assassinated in a terrorist bombing, along w...
08-29.text: Sayyid/Syed/Sayit is an honorific title of Hasanid and Husaynid lineage, recognized as descendants of the Islamic prophet Muhammad through his daughter Fatima and Ali's sons Hasan and Husayn. The title may also refer to the descendants of the family of the Bani Hashim through the Prophet's great-grandfather Hashim, and others including Hamza, Abbas, Abu Talib, and Asad ibn Hashim.
08-29.location: Nobility title in the Islamic world

09-01.title: David Dacko
09-01.subtitle: Central African President David Dacko is ousted from power in a bloodless military coup led by General André Kolingba.
09-01.text: Central African President David Dacko is ousted from power in a bloodless military coup led by General André Kolingba.

09-01.subtitle: The United States Air Force Space Command is founded.
09-01.text: The United States Air Force Space Command is founded.

09-01.title: Titanic
09-01.subtitle: The wreck of the Titanic is discovered by an American-French expedition led by Robert Ballard and Jean-Louis Michel.
09-01.text: The wreck of the Titanic is discovered by an American-French expedition led by Robert Ballard and Jean-Louis Michel.

09-01.title: Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird
09-01.subtitle: The SR-71 Blackbird sets (and holds) the record for flying from New York to London in the time of one hour, 54 minutes and 56.
09-01.text: The SR-71 Blackbird sets (and holds) the record for flying from New York to London in the time of one hour, 54 minutes and 56.4 seconds at a speed of 1,435.587 miles per hour (2,310.353 km/h).

08-31.title: Letter bomb
08-31.subtitle: A parcel bomb sent by Ngô Đình Nhu, younger brother and chief adviser of South Vietnamese President Ngô Đình Diệm, fails...
08-31.text: A parcel bomb sent by Ngô Đình Nhu, younger brother and chief adviser of South Vietnamese President Ngô Đình Diệm, fails to kill King Norodom Sihanouk of Cambodia.

08-31.title: 2025 Kunar earthquake
08-31.subtitle: An earthquake in eastern Afghanistan kills over 1400 people.
08-31.text: An earthquake in eastern Afghanistan kills over 1400 people. 25

08-31.title: Nazi Germany
08-31.subtitle: Nazi Germany mounts a false flag attack on the Gleiwitz radio station, creating an excuse to attack Poland the following...
08-31.text: Nazi Germany mounts a false flag attack on the Gleiwitz radio station, creating an excuse to attack Poland the following day, thus starting World War II in Europe.

08-31.title: Kyrgyzstan
08-31.subtitle: Kyrgyzstan declares its independence from the Soviet Union.
08-31.text: Kyrgyzstan declares its independence from the Soviet Union.

08-31.title: Federation of Malaya
08-31.subtitle: The Federation of Malaya (now Malaysia) gains its independence from the United Kingdom.
08-31.text: The Federation of Malaya (now Malaysia) gains its independence from the United Kingdom.

08-27.title: Six-party talks
08-27.subtitle: The first six-party talks, involving South and North Korea, the United States, China, Japan and Russia, convene to find ...
08-27.text: The first six-party talks, involving South and North Korea, the United States, China, Japan and Russia, convene to find a peaceful resolution to the security concerns of the North Korean nuclear weapons program.

08-27.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
08-27.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-51-I to deploy three communication satellites and repair a fourth malfunctioning one.
08-27.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-51-I to deploy three communication satellites and repair a fourth malfunctioning one.

08-27.title: The Troubles
08-27.subtitle: The Troubles: Eighteen British soldiers are killed at the Warrenpoint ambush by the Provisional Irish Republican Army ne...
08-27.text: The Troubles: Eighteen British soldiers are killed at the Warrenpoint ambush by the Provisional Irish Republican Army near Warrenpoint, Northern Ireland, in the deadliest attack on British forces during Operation Banner. An IRA bomb also kills British royal family member Lord Mountbatten and three others on his boat at Mullaghmore, Republic of Ireland.

08-27.title: Mariner 2
08-27.subtitle: The Mariner 2 unmanned space mission is launched to Venus by NASA.
08-27.text: The Mariner 2 unmanned space mission is launched to Venus by NASA.

08-27.title: South Korean presidential election
08-27.subtitle: South Korean presidential election: After successfully staging the Coup d'état of May Seventeenth, General Chun Doo-hwan...
08-27.text: South Korean presidential election: After successfully staging the Coup d'état of May Seventeenth, General Chun Doo-hwan, running unopposed, has the National Conference for Unification elect him President of the Fourth Republic of Korea.

09-04.title: San Francisco
09-04.subtitle: The first live transcontinental television broadcast takes place in San Francisco, United States, from the Japanese Peace Treaty Conference.
09-04.text: The first live transcontinental television broadcast takes place in San Francisco, United States, from the Japanese Peace Treaty Conference.

09-04.title: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
09-04.subtitle: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, who founded the Republic of Turkey, gathers a congress in Sivas to make decisions as to the future of Anatolia and Thrace.
09-04.text: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, who founded the Republic of Turkey, gathers a congress in Sivas to make decisions as to the future of Anatolia and Thrace.

09-04.title: Google
09-04.subtitle: Google is founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin, two PhD students at Stanford University.
09-04.text: Google LLC is an American multinational technology corporation focused on information technology, online advertising, search engine technology, email, cloud computing, software, quantum computing, e-commerce, consumer electronics, and artificial intelligence (AI). It has been referred to as "the most powerful company in the world" by the BBC, and is one of the world's most valuable brands. Google's parent company Alphabet Inc. has been described as a Big Tech company.

09-04.title: Darlington Raceway
09-04.subtitle: Darlington Raceway is the site of the inaugural Southern 500, the first 500-mile NASCAR race.
09-04.text: Darlington Raceway is the site of the inaugural Southern 500, the first 500-mile NASCAR race.

09-04.title: Buckminsterfullerene
09-04.subtitle: The discovery of Buckminsterfullerene, the first fullerene molecule of carbon.
09-04.text: The discovery of Buckminsterfullerene, the first fullerene molecule of carbon.

09-03.title: People's Liberation Army
09-03.subtitle: The People's Liberation Army begins shelling the Republic of China-controlled islands of Quemoy, starting the First Taiwan Strait Crisis.
09-03.text: The People's Liberation Army begins shelling the Republic of China-controlled islands of Quemoy, starting the First Taiwan Strait Crisis.

09-03.title: Rhodesian Bush War
09-03.subtitle: During the Rhodesian Bush War a group of ZIPRA guerrillas shot down civilian Vickers Viscount aircraft (Air Rhodesia Fli...
09-03.text: During the Rhodesian Bush War a group of ZIPRA guerrillas shot down civilian Vickers Viscount aircraft (Air Rhodesia Flight 825) with a Soviet-made SAM Strela-2; of 56 passengers and crew 38 people died in crash, 10 were massacred by the guerrillas at the site.

09-03.title: 1987 Burundian coup d'état
09-03.subtitle: In a coup d'état in Burundi, President Jean-Baptiste Bagaza is deposed by Major Pierre Buyoya.
09-03.text: In a coup d'état in Burundi, President Jean-Baptiste Bagaza is deposed by Major Pierre Buyoya.

09-03.title: Viking program
09-03.subtitle: Viking program: The American Viking 2 spacecraft lands at Utopia Planitia on Mars.
09-03.text: Viking program: The American Viking 2 spacecraft lands at Utopia Planitia on Mars.

09-03.title: Varig Flight 254
09-03.subtitle: Varig Flight 254 crashes in the Amazon rainforest near São José do Xingu in Brazil, killing 12.
09-03.text: Varig Flight 254 crashes in the Amazon rainforest near São José do Xingu in Brazil, killing 12.

09-05.title: Munich massacre
09-05.subtitle: Munich massacre: A Palestinian terrorist group called "Black September" attacks and takes hostage 11 Israeli athletes at the Munich Olympic Games.
09-05.text: Munich massacre: A Palestinian terrorist group called "Black September" attacks and takes hostage 11 Israeli athletes at the Munich Olympic Games. Two die in the attack and nine are murdered the following day.

09-05.title: Voyager Program
09-05.subtitle: Voyager Program: NASA launches the Voyager 1 spacecraft.
09-05.text: Voyager Program: NASA launches the Voyager 1 spacecraft.

09-05.title: STS-41-D
09-05.subtitle: STS-41-D: The Space Shuttle Discovery lands after its maiden voyage.
09-05.text: STS-41-D: The Space Shuttle Discovery lands after its maiden voyage.

09-05.title: Greenham Common Women's Peace Camp
09-05.subtitle: The first women arrive at what becomes Greenham Common Women's Peace Camp in the UK.
09-05.text: The first women arrive at what becomes Greenham Common Women's Peace Camp in the UK.

09-05.title: Iva Toguri D'Aquino
09-05.subtitle: Iva Toguri D'Aquino, a Japanese American who broadcast under the name Orphan Annie during World War II, is arrested in Yokohama by U.
09-05.text: Iva Toguri D'Aquino, a Japanese American who broadcast under the name Orphan Annie during World War II, is arrested in Yokohama by U.S. military authorities amid widespread speculation of her being wartime radio propagandist Tokyo Rose.

09-07.title: Federal takeover of Fannie Mae and
09-07.subtitle: The United States government takes control of the two largest mortgage financing companies in the US, Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
09-07.text: The United States government takes control of the two largest mortgage financing companies in the US, Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

09-07.title: 2017 Chiapas earthquake
09-07.subtitle: The 8.
09-07.text: The 8.2 Mw  2017 Chiapas earthquake strikes southern Mexico, killing at least 60 people.

09-07.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
09-07.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-69, the second flight of the Wake Shield Facility.
09-07.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

09-07.title: Chile
09-07.subtitle: Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet survives an assassination attempt by the FPMR; five of Pinochet's bodyguards are killed.
09-07.text: Chile, officially the Republic of Chile, is a country in western South America. It is the southernmost country in the world and the closest to Antarctica, extending along a narrow strip of land between the Andes Mountains and the Pacific Ocean. According to the 2024 census, Chile had an enumerated population of 18.5 million.

09-07.title: Canada–Iran relations
09-07.subtitle: Canada officially cuts diplomatic ties with Iran by closing its embassy in Tehran and orders the expulsion of Iranian di...
09-07.text: Canada and Iran have had no formal diplomatic relations since 2012. In the absence of diplomatic representation, Italy acts as the protecting power for Canada in Iran and Switzerland acts as Iran's protecting power in Canada.

09-02.title: NASA
09-02.subtitle: NASA announces the cancellation of two Apollo missions to the Moon, Apollo 15 (the designation is re-used by a later mission), and Apollo 19.
09-02.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-02.title: Israel-Palestinian conflict
09-02.subtitle: Israel-Palestinian conflict: the 2010 Israeli-Palestinian peace talks are launched by the United States.
09-02.text: Israel-Palestinian conflict: the 2010 Israeli-Palestinian peace talks are launched by the United States.

09-02.title: 1992 Nicaragua earthquake
09-02.subtitle: The 7.
09-02.text: The 7.7 Mw  Nicaragua earthquake affected the west coast of Nicaragua. With a Ms–Mw disparity of half a unit, this tsunami earthquake triggered a tsunami that caused most of the damage and casualties, with at least 116 killed. Typical runup heights were 3–8 meters (9.8–26.2 ft).

09-02.title: India's first solar observation mission
09-02.subtitle: India's first solar observation mission: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launches Aditya-L1 f...
09-02.text: India's first solar observation mission: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launches Aditya-L1 from Satish Dhawan space centre.

09-02.title: CBS Evening News
09-02.subtitle: CBS Evening News becomes U.
09-02.text: CBS Evening News becomes U.S. network television's first half-hour weeknight news broadcast, when the show is lengthened from 15 to 30 minutes.

09-10.title: Abebe Bikila
09-10.subtitle: At the Summer Olympics in Rome, Abebe Bikila becomes the first sub-Saharan African to win a gold medal, winning the marathon in bare feet.
09-10.text: At the Summer Olympics in Rome, Abebe Bikila becomes the first sub-Saharan African to win a gold medal, winning the marathon in bare feet.

09-10.title: Polaris Dawn
09-10.subtitle: Polaris Dawn, the first private crewed spaceflight to involve a spacewalk, is launched from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
09-10.text: Polaris Dawn, the first private crewed spaceflight to involve a spacewalk, is launched from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

09-10.title: Nawaz Sharif
09-10.subtitle: Former Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif returns to Pakistan after seven years in exile, following a military coup in October 1999.
09-10.text: Former Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif returns to Pakistan after seven years in exile, following a military coup in October 1999.

09-10.title: Reapers' War
09-10.subtitle: Reapers' War: Junta de Braços (Assembly of Estates) of the Principality of Catalonia summoned.
09-10.text: Reapers' War: Junta de Braços (Assembly of Estates) of the Principality of Catalonia summoned. It assumes the sovereignty and enacts a series of revolutionary measures which will lead to the Catalan Republic.

09-10.title: World War II
09-10.subtitle: World War II: The submarine HMS Oxley is mistakenly sunk by the submarine HMS Triton near Norway and becomes the Royal N...
09-10.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

09-09.title: Space Shuttle Program
09-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle Program: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-115 to resume assembling the International Space Station.
09-09.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-09.title: Space Shuttle program
09-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-64.
09-09.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

09-09.title: 2025 Russian drone incursion into Poland
09-09.subtitle: Several Russian drones incur on Polish airspace and are subsequently shot down, marking the first time a NATO member has...
09-09.text: Several Russian drones incur on Polish airspace and are subsequently shot down, marking the first time a NATO member has engaged Russian military assets.

09-09.title: ISRO
09-09.subtitle: The Indian space agency puts into orbit its heaviest foreign satellite yet, in a streak of 21 consecutive successful PSLV launches.
09-09.text: The Indian space agency puts into orbit its heaviest foreign satellite yet, in a streak of 21 consecutive successful PSLV launches.

09-09.title: World War II
09-09.subtitle: World War II: The Fatherland Front takes power in Bulgaria through a military coup in the capital and armed rebellion in the country.
09-09.text: World War II: The Fatherland Front takes power in Bulgaria through a military coup in the capital and armed rebellion in the country. A new pro-Soviet government is established.

09-11.title: People's Liberation Army
09-11.subtitle: China's People's Liberation Army (PLA) launched an attack on Indian posts at Nathu La, Sikkim, India, which resulted in military clashes.
09-11.text: China's People's Liberation Army (PLA) launched an attack on Indian posts at Nathu La, Sikkim, India, which resulted in military clashes.

09-11.title: Classical World Chess Championship 1995
09-11.subtitle: The first game of the PCA World Chess Championship 1995, pitting incumbent champion Garry Kasparov against challenger Vi...
09-11.text: The first game of the PCA World Chess Championship 1995, pitting incumbent champion Garry Kasparov against challenger Viswanathan Anand, takes place on the 107th floor of the World Trade Center's South Tower in New York City.

09-11.title: 1973 Chilean coup d'état
09-11.subtitle: A coup in Chile, headed by General Augusto Pinochet, topples the democratically elected president Salvador Allende.
09-11.text: A coup in Chile, headed by General Augusto Pinochet, topples the democratically elected president Salvador Allende.

09-11.title: Chilean Constitution of 1980
09-11.subtitle: A new constitution of Chile is established under the influence of then Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet, which is subje...
09-11.text: The Political Constitution of the Republic of Chile of 1980 is the fundamental law in force in Chile. It was approved and promulgated under the military dictatorship headed by Augusto Pinochet, being ratified by the Chilean citizenry through a referendum on September 11, 1980, although being held under restrictions and without electoral registers. While 69% of the population was reported to have voted yes, the vote was questioned by hundreds of denunciations of irregularities and fraud.

09-11.title: Palestinian refugees
09-11.subtitle: The international forces that were guaranteeing the safety of Palestinian refugees following Israel's 1982 Invasion of Lebanon leave Beirut.
09-11.text: The international forces that were guaranteeing the safety of Palestinian refugees following Israel's 1982 Invasion of Lebanon leave Beirut. Five days later, several thousand refugees are massacred in the Sabra and Shatila refugee camps by Phalange forces.

09-08.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
09-08.subtitle: NASA launches Space Shuttle Atlantis on STS-106 to resupply the International Space Station.
09-08.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-08.title: Huntsville, Alabama
09-08.subtitle: In Huntsville, Alabama, US President Dwight D.
09-08.text: In Huntsville, Alabama, US President Dwight D. Eisenhower formally dedicates the Marshall Space Flight Center (NASA had already activated the facility on July 1).

09-08.title: Genesis
09-08.subtitle: NASA's uncrewed spacecraft Genesis crash-lands when its parachute fails to open.
09-08.text: Genesis was a NASA sample-return probe that collected a sample of solar wind particles and returned them to Earth for analysis. It was the first NASA sample-return mission to return material since the Apollo program, and the first to return material from beyond the orbit of the Moon. Genesis was launched on August 8, 2001, and the sample return capsule crash-landed in Utah on September 8, 2004, after a design flaw prevented the deployment of its drogue parachute.

09-08.title: 2023 Rugby World Cup
09-08.subtitle: The 2023 Rugby World Cup, the tenth men's Rugby World Cup is held in France.
09-08.text: The 2023 Rugby World Cup, the tenth men's Rugby World Cup is held in France. The opening ceremony, directed and written by Jean Dujardin, Olivier Ferracci and Nora Matthey, took place at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis, before the opening match between France and New Zealand, which saw the host nation winning 27 to 13.

09-08.title: OSIRIS-REx
09-08.subtitle: NASA launches OSIRIS-REx, its first asteroid sample return mission.
09-08.text: NASA launches OSIRIS-REx, its first asteroid sample return mission. The probe visited 101955 Bennu and returned with samples in September 2023.

09-06.title: Cold War
09-06.subtitle: Cold War: Soviet Air Defence Forces pilot Viktor Belenko lands a Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-25 jet fighter at Hakodate in Japa...
09-06.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
09-06.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

09-06.title: Minotaur V
09-06.subtitle: The first Minotaur V rocket is launched from the Mid-Atlantic Regional Spaceport on Wallops Island, carrying NASA's LADEE spacecraft.
09-06.text: The first Minotaur V rocket is launched from the Mid-Atlantic Regional Spaceport on Wallops Island, carrying NASA's LADEE spacecraft.

09-06.title: Munich massacre
09-06.subtitle: Munich massacre: Nine Israeli athletes die (along with a German policeman) at the hands of the Palestinian "Black Septem...
09-06.text: Munich massacre: Nine Israeli athletes die (along with a German policeman) at the hands of the Palestinian "Black September" terrorist group after being taken hostage at the Munich Olympic Games. Two other Israeli athletes were slain in the initial attack the previous day.

09-06.title: Soviet Union
09-06.subtitle: The Soviet Union recognizes the independence of the Baltic states Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania.
09-06.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

09-06.title: Blackfriars shipwrecks
09-06.subtitle: Archaeologist Peter Marsden discovers the first of the Blackfriars Ships dating back to the second century AD in the Bla...
09-06.text: Archaeologist Peter Marsden discovers the first of the Blackfriars Ships dating back to the second century AD in the Blackfriars area of the banks of the River Thames in London.

09-13.title: IBM 305 RAMAC
09-13.subtitle: The IBM 305 RAMAC is introduced, the first commercial computer to use disk storage.
09-13.text: The IBM 305 RAMAC is introduced, the first commercial computer to use disk storage.

09-13.title: Mao Zedong
09-13.subtitle: Chairman Mao Zedong's second in command and successor Marshal Lin Biao flees China after the failure of an alleged coup.
09-13.text: Chairman Mao Zedong's second in command and successor Marshal Lin Biao flees China after the failure of an alleged coup. His plane crashes in Mongolia, killing all aboard.

09-13.title: Army of the Republic of Vietnam

09-13.text: South Vietnamese Generals Lâm Văn Phát and Dương Văn Đức fail in a coup attempt against General Nguyễn Khánh.

09-13.title: Apartheid
09-13.subtitle: Largest anti-Apartheid march in South Africa, led by Desmond Tutu.
09-13.text: Apartheid was a system of institutionalised racial segregation that existed in South Africa and South West Africa from 1948 to the early 1990s. It was characterised by an authoritarian political culture based on baasskap, which ensured that South Africa was dominated politically, socially, and economically by the nation's minority white population. Under this minoritarian system, white citizens held the highest status, followed by Indians, Coloureds and black Africans, in that order.
09-13.location: South African system of racial segregation

09-13.title: Yitzhak Rabin
09-13.subtitle: Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin shakes hands with Palestine Liberation Organization chairman Yasser Arafat at the W...
09-13.text: Yitzhak Rabin was an Israeli statesman and general who was the prime minister of Israel, having served two terms in office, 1974–1977, and from 1992 until his assassination in 1995. He was the first prime minister to have been born in Palestine.

09-12.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
09-12.subtitle: NASA launches Space Shuttle Discovery on STS-48 to deploy the Upper Atmosphere Research Satellite.
09-12.text: NASA launches Space Shuttle Discovery on STS-48 to deploy the Upper Atmosphere Research Satellite.

09-12.title: NASA
09-12.subtitle: NASA confirms that its Voyager 1 probe has become the first manmade object to enter interstellar space.
09-12.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-12.title: Gemini 11
09-12.subtitle: Gemini 11, the penultimate mission of NASA's Gemini program, and the current human altitude record holder (except for the Apollo lunar missions).
09-12.text: Gemini 11, the penultimate mission of NASA's Gemini program, and the current human altitude record holder (except for the Apollo lunar missions).

Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-14.title: Death of Queen Elizabeth II
09-14.subtitle: Death of Queen Elizabeth II: The Queen's coffin is taken from Buckingham Palace, placed on a gun carriage of The King's ...
09-14.text: Death of Queen Elizabeth II: The Queen's coffin is taken from Buckingham Palace, placed on a gun carriage of The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery and moved in a procession to Westminster Hall for her lying in state over the next four days with the queue of mourners stretching for miles along the River Thames.

09-15.title: Lehman Brothers
09-15.subtitle: Lehman Brothers files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the largest bankruptcy filing in U.
09-15.text: Lehman Brothers files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the largest bankruptcy filing in U.S. history.

09-15.title: Caesars Superdome
09-15.subtitle: At the Superdome in New Orleans, Muhammad Ali outpoints Leon Spinks in a rematch to become the first boxer to win the wo...
09-15.text: At the Superdome in New Orleans, Muhammad Ali outpoints Leon Spinks in a rematch to become the first boxer to win the world heavyweight title three times.

09-15.title: United States Senate Committee on the
09-15.subtitle: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approves Sandra Day O'Connor to become the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.
09-15.text: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approves Sandra Day O'Connor to become the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

09-15.title: Soviet Union
09-15.subtitle: The Soviet Zond 5 spaceship is launched, becoming the first spacecraft to fly around the Moon and re-enter the Earth's atmosphere.
09-15.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

09-15.title: Greenpeace
09-15.subtitle: The first Greenpeace ship departs from Vancouver to protest against the upcoming Cannikin nuclear weapon test in Alaska.
09-15.text: Greenpeace is an independent global campaigning network, founded in Canada in 1971 by a group of environmental activists. Greenpeace states its goal is to "ensure the ability of the Earth to nurture life in all its diversity", and focuses its campaigning on worldwide issues such as climate change, deforestation, overfishing, commercial whaling, genetic engineering, anti-war, and anti-nuclear issues. It uses direct action, advocacy, research, and ecotage to achieve its goals.

09-17.title: Linux
09-17.subtitle: The first version of the Linux kernel (0.
09-17.text: The first version of the Linux kernel (0.01) is released to the Internet.

09-17.text: Fourpeaked Mountain in Alaska erupts, marking the first eruption for the volcano in at least 10,000 years.

09-18.text: After 72 years on radio and television, CBS Television broadcasts the final episode of Guiding Light, the longest-running soap opera in American history.

09-19.text: Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan sign the Indus Waters Treaty for the control and management of the Indus, Chenab, Jhelum, Ravi, Sutlej and Beas rivers.

09-19.text: Azerbaijan launches a military offensive against the Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region; this leads to the flight of the Armenian population.

09-19.title: 2006 Thai coup d'état
09-19.subtitle: The Thai army stages a coup.
09-19.text: The Thai army stages a coup. The Constitution is revoked and martial law is declared.

09-19.title: Cumbre Vieja
09-19.subtitle: The Cumbre Vieja volcano, on the island of La Palma in the Canary Islands, erupts.
09-19.text: The Cumbre Vieja volcano, on the island of La Palma in the Canary Islands, erupts. The eruption lasts for almost three months, ending on December 13.

09-19.subtitle: A strong earthquake kills 2 and injures over 30 in Mexico's state of Michoacán.
09-19.text: A strong earthquake kills 2 and injures over 30 in Mexico's state of Michoacán.

09-22.text: During a military exercise, a Turkish Air Force Northrop F-5 crashes in Babaeski as a result of pilot error, killing one crew member and also 65 soldiers on the ground.

09-22.subtitle: Red Dye No.
09-22.text: Red Dye No. 4 is banned by the US Food and Drug Administration after it is discovered that it causes tumors in the bladders of dogs.

09-20.text: Irish War of Independence: British police known as "Black and Tans" burn the town of Balbriggan and kill two local men in revenge for an IRA assassination.

09-20.title: Hurricane Irene–Olivia
09-20.subtitle: Having weakened after making landfall in Nicaragua the previous day, Hurricane Irene regains enough strength to be renam...
09-20.text: Having weakened after making landfall in Nicaragua the previous day, Hurricane Irene regains enough strength to be renamed Hurricane Olivia, making it the first known hurricane to cross from the Atlantic Ocean into the Pacific.

09-20.title: South Ossetia
09-20.subtitle: South Ossetia declares its independence from Georgia.
09-20.text: South Ossetia, formally known as the State of Alania since 2017, or originally the Republic of South Ossetia is a partially recognised state in the South Caucasus. It has an officially stated population of just over 56,500 people (2022), who live in an area of 3,900 square kilometres (1,500 sq mi), with 33,000 living in the capital city, Tskhinvali. It borders only Russia and Georgia.
09-20.location: Partially recognized state in the South Caucasus

09-23.title: Little Rock schools integration crisis
09-23.subtitle: Little Rock schools integration crisis: President Dwight D.
09-23.text: Little Rock schools integration crisis: President Dwight D. Eisenhower sends the 101st Airborne Division to Little Rock, Arkansas, and federalizes the Arkansas National Guard, ordering both to support the integration of Little Rock Central High School.

09-23.title: 2019 Papua protests
09-23.subtitle: Twenty people die on the first of two days of rioting in Papua and West Papua over an alleged racist incident.
09-23.text: The 2019 Papua protests were a series of protests by Papuans in Indonesia that began on 19 August 2019 and mainly took place across Indonesian Papua region in response to the arrests of 43 Papuan students in Surabaya, East Java for alleged disrespect of the Indonesian flag. Many of the protests involved thousands of participants, and some grew from local protests in Surabaya to demanding an independence referendum for the whole region.
09-23.location: Series of protests by Papuans in Indonesian Papua

09-23.title: Korean War
09-23.subtitle: Korean War: The Battle of Hill 282 is the first US friendly-fire incident on British military personnel since World War II.
09-23.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

09-23.title: John F. Kennedy
09-23.subtitle: U.
09-23.text: John Fitzgerald Kennedy, also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. He was the youngest person elected president at 43 years, as well as the first Catholic president. Kennedy served at the height of the Cold War, and the majority of his foreign policy concerned relations with the Soviet Union and Cuba.
09-23.location: President of the United States from 1961 to 1963

09-23.title: Richard Nixon
09-23.subtitle: After being accused of financial improprieties, Senator Richard Nixon delivers his "Checkers speech" nationwide on telev...
09-23.text: Richard Milhous Nixon was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974. A member of the Republican Party, he represented California in both houses of the United States Congress before serving as the 36th vice president under President Dwight D. Eisenhower from 1953 to 1961. His presidency saw the reduction of U.S.
09-23.location: President of the United States from 1969 to 1974

09-21.title: Galileo
09-21.subtitle: The Galileo spacecraft is terminated by sending it into Jupiter's atmosphere.
09-21.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
09-21.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

09-21.title: The Gambia
09-21.subtitle: The Gambia, Maldives and Singapore are admitted as members of the United Nations.
