09-21.text: Bahrain, Bhutan and Qatar join the United Nations.

09-21.title: 2019 Albania earthquake
09-21.subtitle: A 5.
09-21.text: On 26 November 2019 at 03:54 CET (UTC+1), northwestern Albania was struck by a magnitude 6.4 earthquake with an epicentre 16 kilometres (9.9 mi) west-southwest of Mamurras. The earthquake lasted at least 50 seconds and was felt in Albania's capital Tirana, and in places as far away as Bari, Taranto and Belgrade, 370 kilometres (230 mi) northeast of the epicentre. The maximum felt intensity was VIII (Severe) on the Modified Mercalli intensity scale.

09-21.title: Boris Yeltsin
09-21.subtitle: Russian president Boris Yeltsin triggers a constitutional crisis when he suspends parliament and scraps the constitution.
09-21.text: Russian president Boris Yeltsin triggers a constitutional crisis when he suspends parliament and scraps the constitution.

09-26.subtitle: Soviet Air Force officer Stanislav Petrov identifies a report of an incoming nuclear missile as a computer error and not...
09-26.text: Soviet Air Force officer Stanislav Petrov identifies a report of an incoming nuclear missile as a computer error and not an American first strike, thus preventing nuclear war.

09-26.subtitle: Albert Einstein publishes the third of his Annus Mirabilis papers, introducing the special theory of relativity.
09-26.text: Albert Einstein publishes the third of his Annus Mirabilis papers, introducing the special theory of relativity.

09-26.title: Hurricane Helene
09-26.subtitle: Hurricane Helene makes landfall in Perry, Florida as a Category 4 hurricane, killing over 250 people, causing US$78.
09-26.text: Hurricane Helene was a powerful and devastating tropical cyclone that caused widespread catastrophic damage and numerous fatalities across the Southeastern United States in late September 2024. It was the strongest hurricane on record to strike the Big Bend region of Florida, the deadliest Atlantic hurricane since Hurricane Maria in 2017, and the deadliest to strike the mainland U.S. since Hurricane Katrina in 2005.

09-26.title: Soyuz 7K-ST No.16L
09-26.subtitle: Soyuz 7K-ST No.
09-26.text: Soyuz 7K-ST No. 16L, intended to launch a crew to the Salyut 7 space station, explodes on the launch pad. The launch escape system is activated before the Soyuz-U rocket explodes, saving the crew.

09-26.title: 1997 Umbria and Marche earthquakes
09-26.subtitle: An earthquake strikes the Italian regions of Umbria and the Marche, causing part of the Basilica of St.
09-26.text: An earthquake strikes the Italian regions of Umbria and the Marche, causing part of the Basilica of St. Francis at Assisi to collapse.

09-25.title: PauknAir Flight 4101
09-25.subtitle: PauknAir Flight 4101, a British Aerospace 146, crashes near Melilla Airport in Melilla, Spain, killing 38 people.
09-25.text: PauknAir Flight 4101, a British Aerospace 146, crashes near Melilla Airport in Melilla, Spain, killing 38 people.

09-25.subtitle: TAT-1, the first submarine transatlantic telephone cable system, is inaugurated.
09-25.text: TAT-1, the first submarine transatlantic telephone cable system, is inaugurated.

09-25.text: Fijian Governor-General Penaia Ganilau is overthrown in a coup d'état led by Lieutenant colonel Sitiveni Rabuka.

Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-25.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-24.subtitle: USS Enterprise, the world's first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier, is launched.

09-24.subtitle: The Mars Orbiter Mission makes India the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit, and the first nation in the world to do so in its first attempt.
09-24.text: The Mars Orbiter Mission makes India the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit, and the first nation in the world to do so in its first attempt.

09-24.title: Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty
09-24.subtitle: Representatives of 71 nations sign the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty at the United Nations.
09-24.text: The Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty (CTBT) is a multilateral treaty to ban nuclear weapons test explosions and any other nuclear explosions, for both civilian and military purposes, in all environments. It was adopted by the United Nations General Assembly on 10 September 1996, but has not entered into force, as it has not been ratified by all the required states. The treaty will establish the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Organization to oversee monitoring, which is currently done by a Preparatory Commission.
09-24.location: 1996 treaty banning all nuclear weapons testing

09-24.title: NASA
09-24.subtitle: NASA's OSIRIS-REx capsule containing samples from the asteroid 101955 Bennu successfully lands back on Earth.
09-24.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-24.title: Saffron Revolution
09-24.subtitle: Between 30,000 and 100,000 people take part in anti-government protests in Yangon, Burma, the largest in 20 years.
09-24.text: Between 30,000 and 100,000 people take part in anti-government protests in Yangon, Burma, the largest in 20 years.

09-28.title: 1961 Syrian coup d'état
09-28.subtitle: A military coup in Damascus effectively ends the United Arab Republic, the union between Egypt and Syria.
09-28.text: A military coup in Damascus effectively ends the United Arab Republic, the union between Egypt and Syria.

09-28.title: 2018 Sulawesi earthquake and tsunami
09-28.subtitle: The 7.
09-28.text: The 7.5 Mw 2018 Sulawesi earthquake, which triggered a large tsunami, leaves 4,340 dead and 10,679 injured.

09-28.title: Color television
09-28.subtitle: CBS makes the first color televisions available for sale to the general public, but the product is discontinued less than a month later.
09-28.text: CBS makes the first color televisions available for sale to the general public, but the product is discontinued less than a month later.

09-28.title: Falcon 1
09-28.subtitle: Falcon 1 becomes the first privately developed liquid-fuel ground-launched vehicle to put a payload into orbit by the RatSat mission.
09-28.text: Falcon 1 becomes the first privately developed liquid-fuel ground-launched vehicle to put a payload into orbit by the RatSat mission.

09-28.title: 2008 Singapore Grand Prix
09-28.subtitle: The Singapore Grand Prix is held as Formula One's inaugural night race, with Fernando Alonso winning the event.
09-28.text: The Singapore Grand Prix is held as Formula One's inaugural night race, with Fernando Alonso winning the event. Almost a year later it was revealed that Alonso's team-mate Nelson Piquet Jr. had been ordered to crash his car to help bring out the safety car and give Alonso the advantage and win.

09-30.title: Botswana
09-30.subtitle: Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.
09-30.text: Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.

09-30.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
09-30.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-68.
09-30.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

09-30.title: USS Nautilus
09-30.subtitle: The U.
09-30.text: The U.S. Navy submarine USS Nautilus is commissioned as the world's first nuclear-powered vessel.

09-30.title: 2009 Sumatra earthquakes
09-30.subtitle: The 7.
09-30.text: The 7.6 Mw  Sumatra earthquake leaves 1,115 people dead.

09-30.title: Tokaimura nuclear accidents
09-30.subtitle: The Tokaimura nuclear accident causes the deaths of two technicians in Japan's second-worst nuclear accident.
09-30.text: The Tokaimura nuclear accident causes the deaths of two technicians in Japan's second-worst nuclear accident.

09-27.title: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War
09-27.subtitle: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nag...
09-27.text: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region, inhabited predominantly by ethnic Armenians.

09-27.title: Zhai Zhigang
09-27.subtitle: CNSA astronaut Zhai Zhigang becomes the first Chinese person to perform a spacewalk.
09-27.text: CNSA astronaut Zhai Zhigang becomes the first Chinese person to perform a spacewalk.

09-27.title: Battle of Kabul
09-27.subtitle: The Battle of Kabul ends in a Taliban victory; an Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan is established.
09-27.text: The Battle of Kabul ends in a Taliban victory; an Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan is established.

09-27.title: National League for Democracy
09-27.subtitle: The National League for Democracy is formed by Aung San Suu Kyi and others to fight dictatorship in Myanmar.
09-27.text: The National League for Democracy is formed by Aung San Suu Kyi and others to fight dictatorship in Myanmar.

09-27.title: Google
09-27.subtitle: The Google internet search engine retroactively claims this date as its birthday.
09-27.text: Google LLC is an American multinational technology corporation focused on information technology, online advertising, search engine technology, email, cloud computing, software, quantum computing, e-commerce, consumer electronics, and artificial intelligence (AI). It has been referred to as "the most powerful company in the world" by the BBC, and is one of the world's most valuable brands. Google's parent company Alphabet Inc. has been described as a Big Tech company.

10-01.title: 2017 Catalan independence referendum
10-01.subtitle: An independence referendum, later declared illegal by the Constitutional Court of Spain, takes place in Catalonia.
10-01.text: An independence referendum, later declared illegal by the Constitutional Court of Spain, takes place in Catalonia.

10-01.title: CTV Television Network
10-01.subtitle: The CTV Television Network, Canada's first private television network, is launched.
10-01.text: The CTV Television Network, Canada's first private television network, is launched.

10-01.title: Israel-Palestinian conflict
10-01.subtitle: Israel-Palestinian conflict: Israel attacks the Palestine Liberation Organization's Tunisia headquarters during Operation Wooden Leg.
10-01.text: Israel-Palestinian conflict: Israel attacks the Palestine Liberation Organization's Tunisia headquarters during Operation Wooden Leg.

10-01.title: Defense Intelligence Agency
10-01.subtitle: The United States Defense Intelligence Agency is formed, becoming the country's first centralized military intelligence organization.
10-01.text: The United States Defense Intelligence Agency is formed, becoming the country's first centralized military intelligence organization.

10-01.title: Indonesian Army
10-01.subtitle: At dawn, seven Indonesian Army officers and a police inspector were assassinated by the September 30 Movement at Lubang Buaya, East Jakarta.
10-01.text: At dawn, seven Indonesian Army officers and a police inspector were assassinated by the September 30 Movement at Lubang Buaya, East Jakarta. In addition, two more officers were killed in Yogyakarta. The Army pinned the murders to the Communist Party of Indonesia, resulting in mass killings of suspected leftists.

09-29.title: STS-26
09-29.subtitle: NASA launches STS-26, the first Space Shuttle mission since the Challenger disaster.
09-29.text: NASA launches STS-26, the first Space Shuttle mission since the Challenger disaster.

09-29.title: Burt Rutan
09-29.subtitle: Burt Rutan's Ansari SpaceShipOne performs a successful spaceflight, the first of two required to win the Ansari X Prize.
09-29.text: Burt Rutan's Ansari SpaceShipOne performs a successful spaceflight, the first of two required to win the Ansari X Prize.

09-29.title: Calder Hall nuclear power station
09-29.subtitle: Calder Hall, the world's first commercial nuclear power station, is demolished in a controlled explosion.
09-29.text: Calder Hall, the world's first commercial nuclear power station, is demolished in a controlled explosion.

09-29.title: Kyshtym disaster
09-29.subtitle: The Kyshtym disaster is the third-worst nuclear accident ever recorded.
09-29.text: The Kyshtym disaster is the third-worst nuclear accident ever recorded.

09-29.title: Stock market crash
09-29.subtitle: The stock market crashes with the Dow Jones dropping a then record 778 points after the United States House of Represent...
09-29.text: The stock market crashes with the Dow Jones dropping a then record 778 points after the United States House of Representatives vote on the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act fails during the beginning stages of the Great Recession.

10-02.title: Thurgood Marshall
10-02.subtitle: Thurgood Marshall is sworn in as the first African-American justice of the United States Supreme Court.
10-02.text: Thurgood Marshall is sworn in as the first African-American justice of the United States Supreme Court.

10-02.title: Michael Myers
10-02.subtitle: Michael Myers becomes the first member of either chamber of Congress to be expelled since the Civil War.
10-02.text: Michael Myers becomes the first member of either chamber of Congress to be expelled since the Civil War.

10-02.title: Gustavo Díaz Ordaz
10-02.subtitle: Mexican President Gustavo Díaz Ordaz orders soldiers to suppress a demonstration of unarmed students, ten days before th...
10-02.text: Mexican President Gustavo Díaz Ordaz orders soldiers to suppress a demonstration of unarmed students, ten days before the start of the 1968 Summer Olympics.

10-02.title: Ukrainian War of Independence
10-02.subtitle: Ukrainian War of Independence: Mikhail Frunze orders the Red Army to immediately cease hostilities with the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.
10-02.text: Ukrainian War of Independence: Mikhail Frunze orders the Red Army to immediately cease hostilities with the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.

10-02.title: Parkrun
10-02.subtitle: The first parkrun, then known as the Bushy Park Time Trial, takes place in Bushy Park, London, UK.
10-02.text: Parkrun is a collection of 5-kilometre (3.1 mi) events for runners, walkers and volunteers that take place every Saturday morning at more than 2,000 locations in 23 countries across five continents.

10-03.title: Svante Pääbo
10-03.subtitle: Svante Pääbo is awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.
10-03.text: Svante Pääbo is awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

10-03.title: Nadine Gordimer
10-03.subtitle: Nadine Gordimer is announced as the winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature.
10-03.text: Nadine Gordimer is announced as the winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature.

10-03.title: East Germany
10-03.subtitle: The German Democratic Republic is abolished and becomes part of the Federal Republic of Germany; the event is afterwards...
10-03.text: The German Democratic Republic is abolished and becomes part of the Federal Republic of Germany; the event is afterwards celebrated as German Unity Day.

10-03.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
10-03.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Atlantis makes its maiden flight, carrying two DSCS-III Satellites on STS-51-J.
10-03.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

10-03.title: Project Mercury
10-03.subtitle: Project Mercury: US astronaut Wally Schirra, in Sigma 7, is launched from Cape Canaveral for a six-orbit flight.
10-03.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

10-05.title: James Bond
10-05.subtitle: The first of the James Bond film series, based on the novels by Ian Fleming, Dr.
10-05.text: The first of the James Bond film series, based on the novels by Ian Fleming, Dr. No, is released in Britain.

10-05.title: PBS
10-05.subtitle: The Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) is founded in the United States.
10-05.text: The Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) is founded in the United States.[citation needed]

10-05.title: The Beatles
10-05.subtitle: The first Beatles single "Love Me Do" is released in Britain.
10-05.text: The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960. The core lineup of the band comprised John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr. They are widely regarded as the most influential band in popular music and were integral to the development of 1960s counterculture and the recognition of popular music as an art form.

10-05.title: James Cross
10-05.subtitle: The British Trade Commissioner, James Cross, is kidnapped by members of the Front de libération du Québec, triggering the October Crisis in Canada.
10-05.text: The British Trade Commissioner, James Cross, is kidnapped by members of the Front de libération du Québec, triggering the October Crisis in Canada.

10-05.title: Mekong River massacre
10-05.subtitle: In the Mekong River massacre, two Chinese cargo boats are hijacked and 13 crew members murdered.
10-05.text: In the Mekong River massacre, two Chinese cargo boats are hijacked and 13 crew members murdered.

10-07.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
10-07.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-112 to continue assembly of the International Space Station.
10-07.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

10-07.title: Ales Bialiatski
10-07.subtitle: Ales Bialiatski, along with two organisations, Memorial & Center for Civil Liberties, are awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.
10-07.text: Ales Bialiatski, along with two organisations, Memorial & Center for Civil Liberties, are awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

10-07.title: Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty
10-07.subtitle: President Kennedy signs the ratification of the Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty.
10-07.text: President Kennedy signs the ratification of the Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty.

10-07.title: Luna 3
10-07.subtitle: The Soviet probe Luna 3 transmits the first-ever photographs of the far side of the Moon.
10-07.text: The Soviet probe Luna 3 transmits the first-ever photographs of the far side of the Moon.

10-07.title: Hamas
10-07.subtitle: Hamas and several other Palestinian militant groups launch an attack into Israel, which results in the deaths of around ...
10-07.text: Hamas and several other Palestinian militant groups launch an attack into Israel, which results in the deaths of around 1,200, mostly civilians, and the taking of 251 hostages, including civilians and soldiers. The attack initiated the Gaza war and the larger Middle Eastern crisis.

10-04.title: Protocol on Environmental Protection to the
10-04.subtitle: The Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty is opened for signature.
10-04.text: The Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty is opened for signature.

10-04.title: SpaceShipOne
10-04.subtitle: SpaceShipOne wins the Ansari X Prize for private spaceflight.
10-04.text: SpaceShipOne wins the Ansari X Prize for private spaceflight.

10-04.title: Battle of Mogadishu
10-04.subtitle: Battle of Mogadishu occurs killing 18 U.
10-04.text: Battle of Mogadishu occurs killing 18 U.S. Special Forces, two UN Peacekeepers and at least 600 Somalian militia men and civilians.

10-04.title: Sputnik 1
10-04.subtitle: Sputnik 1 becomes the first artificial satellite to orbit the Earth.
10-04.text: Sputnik 1, often referred to as simply Sputnik, was the first artificial Earth satellite. It was launched into an elliptical low Earth orbit by the Soviet Union on 4 October 1957 as part of the Soviet space program. It sent a radio signal back to Earth for three weeks before its three silver-zinc batteries became depleted. Aerodynamic drag caused it to fall back into the atmosphere on 4 January 1958.

10-04.title: Richard Noble
10-04.subtitle: Richard Noble sets a new land speed record of 633.
10-04.text: Richard Noble sets a new land speed record of 633.468 miles per hour (1,019.468 km/h) at the Black Rock Desert in Nevada.

10-08.title: 2005 Kashmir earthquake
10-08.subtitle: The 7.
10-08.text: The 7.6 Mw  Kashmir earthquake leaves 86,000–87,351 people dead, 69,000–75,266 injured, and 2.8 million homeless.

10-08.title: Franklin National Bank
10-08.subtitle: Franklin National Bank collapses due to fraud and mismanagement; at the time it is the largest bank failure in the history of the United States.
10-08.text: Franklin National Bank collapses due to fraud and mismanagement; at the time it is the largest bank failure in the history of the United States.

10-08.title: Extinction Rebellion
10-08.subtitle: About 200 Extinction Rebellion activists block the gates of Leinster House (parliament) in the Republic of Ireland.
10-08.text: About 200 Extinction Rebellion activists block the gates of Leinster House (parliament) in the Republic of Ireland.

10-08.title: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
10-08.subtitle: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn wins the Nobel Prize in literature.
10-08.text: Aleksandr Isayevich Solzhenitsyn was a Soviet and Russian author and dissident who helped to raise global awareness of political repression in the Soviet Union, especially the Gulag prison system. He was awarded the 1970 Nobel Prize in Literature "for the ethical force with which he has pursued the indispensable traditions of Russian literature". His nonfiction work The Gulag Archipelago "amounted to a head-on challenge to the Soviet state" and sold tens of millions of copies.

10-08.title: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War
10-08.subtitle: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan twice deliberately targeted the Church of the Holy Savior Ghazanchetsots of Shusha.
10-08.text: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan twice deliberately targeted the Church of the Holy Savior Ghazanchetsots of Shusha.

10-06.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
10-06.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-41, and deploys the Ulysses space probe to study the Sun's polar regions.
10-06.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-41, and deploys the Ulysses space probe to study the Sun's polar regions.

10-06.title: 51 Pegasi b
10-06.subtitle: The first planet orbiting another sun, 51 Pegasi b, is discovered.
10-06.text: The first planet orbiting another sun, 51 Pegasi b, is discovered.

10-06.title: Annie Ernaux
10-06.subtitle: Annie Ernaux is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature.
10-06.text: Annie Ernaux is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature.

10-06.title: 6 October 1976 massacre
10-06.subtitle: Dozens are killed by Thai police and right-wing paramilitaries in the Thammasat University massacre; afterwards, the Sen...
10-06.text: Dozens are killed by Thai police and right-wing paramilitaries in the Thammasat University massacre; afterwards, the Seni Pramoj government is toppled in a military coup led by Sangad Chaloryu.

10-06.title: World War I
10-06.subtitle: World War I: Combined Austro-Hungarian and German Central Powers, reinforced by the recently joined Bulgaria launched a ...
10-06.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

10-11.title: Apollo 7
10-11.subtitle: NASA launches Apollo 7, the first successful crewed Apollo mission.
10-11.text: NASA launches Apollo 7, the first successful crewed Apollo mission.

10-11.title: STS-92
10-11.subtitle: NASA launches STS-92, the 100th Space Shuttle mission.
10-11.text: NASA launches STS-92, the 100th Space Shuttle mission.

10-11.title: Pioneer 1
10-11.subtitle: NASA launches Pioneer 1, its first space probe, although it fails to achieve a stable orbit.
10-11.text: NASA launches Pioneer 1, its first space probe, although it fails to achieve a stable orbit.

10-11.title: Kathryn D. Sullivan
10-11.subtitle: Aboard the Space Shuttle Challenger, astronaut Kathryn D.
10-11.text: Aboard the Space Shuttle Challenger, astronaut Kathryn D. Sullivan becomes the first American woman to perform a space walk.

10-11.title: Field-sequential color system
10-11.subtitle: CBS's field-sequential color system for television is the first to be licensed for broadcast by the U.
10-11.text: CBS's field-sequential color system for television is the first to be licensed for broadcast by the U.S. Federal Communications Commission.

10-10.title: 1964 Summer Olympics
10-10.subtitle: The Tokyo Summer Olympics opening ceremony is the first to be relayed live by satellites.
10-10.text: The Tokyo Summer Olympics opening ceremony is the first to be relayed live by satellites.

10-10.title: Sheikh Muszaphar Shukor
10-10.subtitle: Sheikh Muszaphar Shukor becomes the first Malaysian in space on board Soyuz TMA-11.
10-10.text: Sheikh Muszaphar Shukor becomes the first Malaysian in space on board Soyuz TMA-11.

10-10.title: Farabundo Martí National Liberation Front
10-10.subtitle: The Farabundo Martí National Liberation Front is founded in El Salvador.
10-10.text: The Farabundo Martí National Liberation Front is a Salvadoran political party and former guerrilla rebel group.
10-10.location: Salvadoran political party and former guerilla organization

10-10.title: Ben Bernanke
10-10.subtitle: Ben S.
10-10.text: Ben S. Bernanke, Douglas W. Diamond and Philip H. Dybvig are jointly awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences.

10-10.title: Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty
10-10.subtitle: The Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty comes into effect.
10-10.text: The Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty comes into effect.

10-12.title: Abkhazia
10-12.subtitle: The former Autonomous Soviet Republic of Abkhazia declares its independence from Georgia.
10-12.text: The former Autonomous Soviet Republic of Abkhazia declares its independence from Georgia.

10-12.title: New Zealand
10-12.subtitle: New Zealand holds its first general election under the new mixed-member proportional representation system, which led to...
10-12.text: New Zealand holds its first general election under the new mixed-member proportional representation system, which led to Jim Bolger's National Party forming a coalition government with Winston Peters's New Zealand First.

10-12.title: Shenzhou 6
10-12.subtitle: The second Chinese human spaceflight, Shenzhou 6, is launched, carrying two cosmonauts in orbit for five days.
10-12.text: The second Chinese human spaceflight, Shenzhou 6, is launched, carrying two cosmonauts in orbit for five days.

10-12.title: European Union
10-12.subtitle: The European Union wins the 2012 Nobel Peace Prize.
10-12.text: The European Union wins the 2012 Nobel Peace Prize.

10-12.title: Voskhod 1
10-12.subtitle: The Soviet Union launches the Voskhod 1 into Earth orbit as the first spacecraft with a multi-person crew, and the first...
10-12.text: Voskhod 1 was the seventh crewed Soviet space flight. Flown by cosmonauts Vladimir Komarov, Konstantin Feoktistov, and Boris Yegorov, it launched 12 October 1964, and returned on the 13th. Voskhod 1 was the first human spaceflight to carry more than one crewman into orbit, the first flight without the use of spacesuits, and the first to carry either an engineer or a physician into outer space. It also set a crewed spacecraft altitude record of 336 km (209 mi).

10-15.title: Baltic states
10-15.subtitle: The leaders of the Baltic States, Arnold Rüütel of Estonia, Anatolijs Gorbunovs of Latvia and Vytautas Landsbergis of Li...
10-15.text: The leaders of the Baltic States, Arnold Rüütel of Estonia, Anatolijs Gorbunovs of Latvia and Vytautas Landsbergis of Lithuania, signed the OSCE Final Act in Helsinki, Finland.

10-15.title: Mikhail Gorbachev
10-15.subtitle: Soviet Union leader Mikhail Gorbachev is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for his efforts to lessen Cold War tensions and open up his nation.
10-15.text: Soviet Union leader Mikhail Gorbachev is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for his efforts to lessen Cold War tensions and open up his nation.

10-15.title: Galileo
10-15.subtitle: NASA's Galileo spacecraft passes within 112 miles (180 km) of Jupiter's moon Io.
10-15.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
10-15.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

10-15.title: Oh-My-God particle
10-15.subtitle: The "Oh-My-God particle", an ultra-high-energy cosmic ray measured at 40,000,000 times that of the highest energy proton...
10-15.text: The "Oh-My-God particle", an ultra-high-energy cosmic ray measured at 40,000,000 times that of the highest energy protons produced in a particle accelerator, is observed at the University of Utah HiRes observatory in Dugway Proving Ground, Utah.

10-15.title: Shenzhou 5
10-15.subtitle: China launches Shenzhou 5, its first crewed space mission.
10-15.text: China launches Shenzhou 5, its first crewed space mission.

10-09.title: The Phantom of the Opera
10-09.subtitle: The Phantom of the Opera, eventually the second longest running musical in London, opens at Her Majesty's Theatre.
10-09.text: The Phantom of the Opera, eventually the second longest running musical in London, opens at Her Majesty's Theatre.

10-09.title: Arakan Rohingya Salvation Army
10-09.subtitle: The Arakan Rohingya Salvation Army launches its first attack on Myanmar security forces along the Bangladesh–Myanmar border.
10-09.text: The Arakan Rohingya Salvation Army launches its first attack on Myanmar security forces along the Bangladesh–Myanmar border.

10-09.title: Thomas & Friends
10-09.subtitle: The popular children's television show Thomas The Tank Engine & Friends, based on The Railway Series by the Reverend Wilbert Awdry, premieres on ITV.
10-09.text: The popular children's television show Thomas The Tank Engine & Friends, based on The Railway Series by the Reverend Wilbert Awdry, premieres on ITV.

10-09.title: Lunar Precursor Robotic Program
10-09.subtitle: First lunar impact of NASA's Lunar Precursor Robotic Program.
10-09.text: First lunar impact of NASA's Lunar Precursor Robotic Program.

10-09.title: Vietnam War
10-09.subtitle: Vietnam War: the Republic of Korea Army commits the Binh Tai Massacre.
10-09.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-09.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

10-14.title: Yasser Arafat
10-14.subtitle: Yasser Arafat, Yitzhak Rabin and Shimon Peres receive the Nobel Peace Prize for their role in the establishment of the O...
10-14.text: Yasser Arafat, Yitzhak Rabin and Shimon Peres receive the Nobel Peace Prize for their role in the establishment of the Oslo Accords and the framing of future Palestinian self government.

10-14.title: Apollo program
10-14.subtitle: Apollo program: The first live television broadcast by American astronauts in orbit is performed by the Apollo 7 crew.
10-14.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

10-14.title: Martin Luther King Jr.
10-14.subtitle: Martin Luther King Jr.
10-14.text: Martin Luther King Jr. receives the Nobel Peace Prize for combating racial inequality through nonviolence.

10-14.title: 2023 Australian Indigenous Voice referendum
10-14.subtitle: Australians vote to reject a constitutional amendment that would have established an Indigenous Voice to Parliament.
10-14.text: Australians vote to reject a constitutional amendment that would have established an Indigenous Voice to Parliament.

10-14.title: Aung San Suu Kyi
10-14.subtitle: Burmese opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.
10-14.text: Aung San Suu Kyi is a Burmese politician, diplomat and author who served as State Counsellor of Myanmar and Minister of Foreign Affairs from 2016 to 2021. She has served as the general secretary of the National League for Democracy (NLD) since the party's founding in 1988 and was registered as its chairperson while it was a legal party from 2011 to 2023. She played a vital role in Myanmar's transition from military junta to partial democracy in the 2010s.

10-13.title: Lufthansa Flight 181
10-13.subtitle: Hijacking of Lufthansa Flight 181 by the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine.
10-13.text: Hijacking of Lufthansa Flight 181 by the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine.

10-13.title: Micrograph
10-13.subtitle: The first electron micrograph of an Ebola virus is taken at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention by Dr.
10-13.text: The first electron micrograph of an Ebola virus is taken at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention by Dr. F. A. Murphy.

10-13.title: 2010 Copiapó mining accident
10-13.subtitle: The mining accident in Copiapó, Chile ends as all 33 trapped miners arrive at the surface after a record 69 days underground.
10-13.text: The mining accident in Copiapó, Chile ends as all 33 trapped miners arrive at the surface after a record 69 days underground.

10-13.title: Lloyd Aéreo Boliviano
10-13.subtitle: A Lloyd Aéreo Boliviano Boeing 707 crashes after takeoff from El Trompillo Airport in Santa Cruz de la Sierra, Bolivia, killing 91.
10-13.text: A Lloyd Aéreo Boliviano Boeing 707 crashes after takeoff from El Trompillo Airport in Santa Cruz de la Sierra, Bolivia, killing 91.

10-13.title: Brigid Kosgei
10-13.subtitle: Kenyan Brigid Kosgei sets a new world record for a woman runner with a time of 2:14:04 at the 2019 Chicago Marathon.
10-13.text: Kenyan Brigid Kosgei sets a new world record for a woman runner with a time of 2:14:04 at the 2019 Chicago Marathon.

10-19.title: 1988–1994 British broadcasting voice restrictions
10-19.subtitle: The British government imposes a broadcasting ban on television and radio interviews with members of Sinn Féin and eleve...
10-19.text: The British government imposes a broadcasting ban on television and radio interviews with members of Sinn Féin and eleven Irish republican and Ulster loyalist paramilitary groups.

10-19.title: Korean War
10-19.subtitle: Korean War: The Battle of Pyongyang ends in a United Nations victory.
10-19.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

10-19.title: House of Lords
10-19.subtitle: Members of Parliament met at the House of Lords to discuss the United Kingdom's Brexit deal, this was the first Saturday...
10-19.text: Members of Parliament met at the House of Lords to discuss the United Kingdom's Brexit deal, this was the first Saturday sitting in Parliament since 3 April 1982 during the Falklands War.

10-19.title: Hurricane Wilma
10-19.subtitle: Hurricane Wilma becomes the most intense Atlantic hurricane on record with a minimum pressure of 882 mb.
10-19.text: Hurricane Wilma was the most intense tropical cyclone in the Atlantic basin and the second-most intense tropical cyclone in the Western Hemisphere, both based on barometric pressure, after Hurricane Patricia in 2015. Wilma's rapid intensification led to a 24-hour pressure drop of 97 mbar (2.9 inHg), setting a new basin record. At its peak, Hurricane Wilma's eye contracted to a record minimum diameter of 2.3 mi (3.7 km).

10-19.title: Juan Federico Ponce Vaides
10-19.subtitle: A coup is launched against Juan Federico Ponce Vaides, beginning the ten-year Guatemalan Revolution.
10-19.text: A coup is launched against Juan Federico Ponce Vaides, beginning the ten-year Guatemalan Revolution.

10-17.title: 17 October affair
10-17.subtitle: Indonesian Army elements surrounded the Merdeka Palace demanding President Sukarno disband the Provisional People's Representative Council.
10-17.text: Indonesian Army elements surrounded the Merdeka Palace demanding President Sukarno disband the Provisional People's Representative Council.

10-17.title: Israeli apartheid
10-17.subtitle: The first attempt of the apartheid analogy by Ahmad Shukeiri.
10-17.text: The first attempt of the apartheid analogy by Ahmad Shukeiri.

10-17.title: Sellafield
10-17.subtitle: The first commercial nuclear power station is officially opened by Queen Elizabeth II in Sellafield, England.
10-17.text: The first commercial nuclear power station is officially opened by Queen Elizabeth II in Sellafield, England.

10-17.title: Syrian civil war
10-17.subtitle: Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) capture the last foothold of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Leva...
10-17.text: The Syrian civil war was an armed conflict that began with the Syrian revolution in March 2011, when popular discontent with the Ba'athist regime ruled by Bashar al-Assad triggered large-scale protests and pro-democracy rallies across Syria, as part of the wider Arab Spring. The Assad regime responded to the protests with lethal force, which led to a series of defections, the emergence of armed opposition groups, and the civilian uprising descending into a civil war.

10-17.title: Maurice Papon
10-17.subtitle: Directed by their chief Maurice Papon, Paris police massacre scores of Algerian protesters.
10-17.text: Directed by their chief Maurice Papon, Paris police massacre scores of Algerian protesters.

10-16.title: Desmond Tutu
10-16.subtitle: Desmond Tutu is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.
10-16.text: Desmond Tutu is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

10-16.title: Henry Kissinger
10-16.subtitle: Henry Kissinger and Lê Đức Thọ are awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.
10-16.text: Henry Kissinger and Lê Đức Thọ are awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

10-16.title: Cuba
10-16.subtitle: Cuban revolutionary Fidel Castro delivers his "History Will Absolve Me" speech, and is sentenced to 15 years' imprisonme...
10-16.text: Cuba, officially the Republic of Cuba, is an island country in the Caribbean. It comprises the eponymous main island as well as 4,195 islands, islets, and cays. Situated at the convergence of the Caribbean Sea, Gulf of Mexico, and Atlantic Ocean, Cuba is located east of the Yucatán Peninsula, south of both Florida and the Bahamas, west of Hispaniola, and north of Jamaica and the Cayman Islands. Havana is the largest city and capital.

10-16.title: Yasunari Kawabata
10-16.subtitle: Yasunari Kawabata becomes the first Japanese person to be awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature.
10-16.text: Yasunari Kawabata becomes the first Japanese person to be awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature.

10-16.title: October 1978 conclave
10-16.subtitle: Cardinal Karol Wojtyła is elected to the papacy as Pope John Paul II, he becomes the first non-Italian pontiff since 1523.
10-16.text: Cardinal Karol Wojtyła is elected to the papacy as Pope John Paul II, he becomes the first non-Italian pontiff since 1523.

10-18.title: NASA Astronaut Corps
10-18.subtitle: NASA Astronauts Jessica Meir and Christina Koch take part in the first all-female spacewalk when they venture out of the...
10-18.text: NASA Astronauts Jessica Meir and Christina Koch take part in the first all-female spacewalk when they venture out of the International Space Station to replace a power controller.

10-18.title: Soviet Union
10-18.subtitle: The Soviet probe Venera 4 reaches Venus and becomes the first spacecraft to measure the atmosphere of another planet.
10-18.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

10-18.title: Félicette
10-18.subtitle: Félicette, a black and white female Parisian stray cat, becomes the first cat launched into space.
10-18.text: Félicette, a black and white female Parisian stray cat, becomes the first cat launched into space.

10-18.title: Regency TR-1
10-18.subtitle: Texas Instruments announces the Regency TR-1, the first mass-produced transistor radio.
10-18.text: Texas Instruments announces the Regency TR-1, the first mass-produced transistor radio.

10-18.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
10-18.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-34 to deploy the Jupiter-bound Galileo space probe.
10-18.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

10-20.title: Soviet Navy
10-20.subtitle: The Soviet Navy performs the first armed test of a submarine-launched ballistic missile, launching an R-13 from a Golf-class submarine.
10-20.text: The Soviet Navy performs the first armed test of a submarine-launched ballistic missile, launching an R-13 from a Golf-class submarine.

10-20.title: Sloan Great Wall
10-20.subtitle: The Sloan Great Wall, once the largest cosmic structures known to humanity, is discovered by students at Princeton University.
10-20.text: The Sloan Great Wall, once the largest cosmic structures known to humanity, is discovered by students at Princeton University.

10-20.title: Libyan Crisis
10-20.subtitle: Libyan Crisis: Rebel forces capture Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi and his son Mutassim in his hometown of Sirte and ki...
10-20.text: Libyan Crisis: Rebel forces capture Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi and his son Mutassim in his hometown of Sirte and kill them shortly thereafter, ending the first Libyan civil war.

10-20.title: Brink's
10-20.subtitle: Two police officers and a Brink's armored car guard are killed during an armed robbery carried out by members of the Bla...
10-20.text: Two police officers and a Brink's armored car guard are killed during an armed robbery carried out by members of the Black Liberation Army and Weather Underground in Nanuet, New York.

10-20.title: Watergate scandal
10-20.subtitle: Watergate scandal: "Saturday Night Massacre": United States President Richard Nixon fires U.
10-20.text: The Watergate scandal, or simply Watergate, was a political scandal in the United States involving the administration of President Richard Nixon. On June 17, 1972, operatives associated with Nixon's 1972 re-election campaign were caught burglarizing and planting listening devices in the Democratic National Committee headquarters at Washington, D.C.'s Watergate complex. Nixon's efforts to conceal his administration's involvement led to an impeachment process and his resignation in August 1974.

10-21.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
10-21.subtitle: President Dwight D.
10-21.text: President Dwight D. Eisenhower approves the transfer of all US Army space-related activities to NASA, including most of the Army Ballistic Missile Agency.

10-21.title: Canada
10-21.subtitle: In Canada, the 2019 Canadian federal election ends, resulting in incumbent Prime Minister Justin Trudeau remaining in of...
10-21.text: In Canada, the 2019 Canadian federal election ends, resulting in incumbent Prime Minister Justin Trudeau remaining in office, albeit with the Liberal Party in a minority government.

10-21.title: Honduras
10-21.subtitle: In Honduras, 131 people are killed when a Boeing 727 crashes on approach to Toncontín International Airport near the nation's capital Tegucigalpa.
10-21.text: Honduras, officially the Republic of Honduras, is a country in Central America. It is bordered to the west by Guatemala, to the southwest by El Salvador, to the southeast by Nicaragua, to the south by the Pacific Ocean at the Gulf of Fonseca, and to the north by the Gulf of Honduras, a large inlet of the Caribbean Sea. Its capital and largest city is Tegucigalpa.

10-21.title: Niki Lauda
10-21.subtitle: Niki Lauda claims his third and final Formula One Drivers' Championship Title by half a point ahead of McLaren team-mate...
10-21.text: Niki Lauda claims his third and final Formula One Drivers' Championship Title by half a point ahead of McLaren team-mate Alain Prost at the Portuguese Grand Prix.

10-21.title: Jaffna hospital massacre
10-21.subtitle: The Jaffna hospital massacre is carried out by Indian peacekeeping forces in Sri Lanka, killing 70 Tamil patients, doctors and nurses.
10-21.text: The Jaffna hospital massacre is carried out by Indian peacekeeping forces in Sri Lanka, killing 70 Tamil patients, doctors and nurses.

10-22.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
10-22.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-52 to deploy the LAGEOS-2 satellite and microgravity experiments.
10-22.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

10-22.title: Jean-Paul Sartre
10-22.subtitle: Jean-Paul Sartre is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, though he does not accept the prize.
10-22.text: Jean-Paul Sartre is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, though he does not accept the prize.

10-22.title: Cuban Missile Crisis
10-22.subtitle: Cuban Missile Crisis: President Kennedy, after internal counsel from Dwight D.
10-22.text: Cuban Missile Crisis: President Kennedy, after internal counsel from Dwight D. Eisenhower, announces that American reconnaissance planes have discovered Soviet nuclear weapons in Cuba, and that he has ordered a naval "quarantine" of the Communist nation.

10-22.title: Australian Capital Territory
10-22.subtitle: The Australian Capital Territory becomes the first Australian jurisdiction to legalize same-sex marriage with the Marria...
10-22.text: The Australian Capital Territory becomes the first Australian jurisdiction to legalize same-sex marriage with the Marriage Equality (Same Sex) Act 2013.

10-22.title: Exploration of the Moon
10-22.subtitle: India launches its first uncrewed lunar probe mission Chandrayaan-1.
10-22.text: India launches its first uncrewed lunar probe mission Chandrayaan-1.

10-24.title: Nedelin catastrophe
10-24.subtitle: Nedelin catastrophe: An R-16 ballistic missile explodes on the launch pad at the Soviet Union's Baikonur Cosmodrome spac...
10-24.text: Nedelin catastrophe: An R-16 ballistic missile explodes on the launch pad at the Soviet Union's Baikonur Cosmodrome space facility, killing over 100 people, including Field Marshal Mitrofan Nedelin.

10-24.title: China National Space Administration
10-24.subtitle: The China National Space Administration launches an experimental lunar mission, Chang'e 5-T1, which will loop behind the Moon and return to Earth.
10-24.text: The China National Space Administration launches an experimental lunar mission, Chang'e 5-T1, which will loop behind the Moon and return to Earth.

10-24.title: Deep Space 1
10-24.subtitle: Deep Space 1 is launched to explore the asteroid belt and test new spacecraft technologies.
10-24.text: Deep Space 1 is launched to explore the asteroid belt and test new spacecraft technologies.

10-24.title: Chang'e 1
10-24.subtitle: Chang'e 1, the first satellite in the Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, is launched from Xichang Satellite Launch Center.
10-24.text: Chang'e 1, the first satellite in the Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, is launched from Xichang Satellite Launch Center.

10-24.title: Annexation of Tibet by the People's
10-24.subtitle: Annexation of Tibet by the People's Republic of China: The People's Liberation Army ceases all military operations in Ti...
10-24.text: Annexation of Tibet by the People's Republic of China: The People's Liberation Army ceases all military operations in Tibet, ending the Battle of Chamdo.

10-27.title: Saturn I SA-1
10-27.subtitle: NASA tests the first Saturn I rocket in Mission Saturn-Apollo 1.
10-27.text: NASA tests the first Saturn I rocket in Mission Saturn-Apollo 1.

10-27.title: Ronald Reagan
10-27.subtitle: Ronald Reagan delivers a speech on behalf of the Republican candidate for president, Barry Goldwater.
10-27.text: Ronald Reagan delivers a speech on behalf of the Republican candidate for president, Barry Goldwater. The speech launches his political career and comes to be known as "A Time for Choosing".

10-27.title: Leicester City F.C.
10-27.subtitle: Leicester City F.
10-27.text: Leicester City F.C. owner Vichai Srivaddhanaprabha dies in a helicopter crash along with four others after a Premier League match against West Ham United at the King Power Stadium in Leicester, England.

10-27.title: Iskander Mirza
10-27.subtitle: Iskander Mirza, the first President of Pakistan, is deposed by General Ayub Khan, who had been appointed the enforcer of...
10-27.text: Iskander Ali Mirza was a Pakistani politician and military general who served as the fourth and last governor-general of Pakistan from 1955 to 1956, and then as the first president of Pakistan from the promulgation of the first constitution in 1956 until his overthrow in a coup d'état in 1958, following his declaration of martial law and unilateral abrogation of the constitution.

10-27.title: Benjamin O. Davis Jr.
10-27.subtitle: Benjamin O.
10-27.text: Benjamin O. Davis, Jr. becomes the first African-American general in the United States Air Force.

10-23.title: Gary Gabelich
10-23.subtitle: Gary Gabelich sets a land speed record in a rocket-powered automobile called the Blue Flame, fueled with natural gas.
10-23.text: Gary Gabelich sets a land speed record in a rocket-powered automobile called the Blue Flame, fueled with natural gas.

10-23.title: Belgium
10-23.subtitle: Belgian artist Peyo's comic characters, the Smurfs, make their debut in Spirou magazine.
10-23.text: Belgian artist Peyo's comic characters, the Smurfs, make their debut in Spirou magazine.

10-23.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
10-23.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-120, with Pamela Melroy becoming the second female space shuttle commander.
10-23.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-120, with Pamela Melroy becoming the second female space shuttle commander.

10-23.title: Vietnam War
10-23.subtitle: Vietnam War: The 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile), in conjunction with the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, launches an ...
10-23.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-23.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

10-23.title: Myanmar Air Force
10-23.subtitle: Myanmar Air Force airstrikes a concert in Hpakant Township, Kachin state killing at least 80 people, including senior Ka...
10-23.text: The Myanmar Air Force is the aerial branch of the Tatmadaw, the armed forces of Myanmar. The primary mission of the Myanmar Air Force (MAF) since its inception has been to provide air bases force protection, anti-aircraft warfare, close air support (CAS), logistical, and transport to the Myanmar Army in counterinsurgency operations. It is mainly used in internal conflicts in Myanmar, and, on a smaller scale, in relief missions, especially after the deadly Cyclone Nargis of May 2008.
10-23.location: Aerial branch of Myanmar's armed forces

10-26.title: Hungarian Revolution
10-26.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution: In the towns of Mosonmagyaróvár and Esztergom, Hungarian secret police forces massacre civilians.
10-26.text: Hungarian Revolution: In the towns of Mosonmagyaróvár and Esztergom, Hungarian secret police forces massacre civilians. As rebel strongholds in Budapest hold, fighting spreads throughout the country.

10-26.title: Space Race
10-26.subtitle: Space Race: The Soyuz 3 mission achieves the first Soviet space rendezvous.
10-26.text: The Space Race was a 20th-century competition between the Cold War rivals, the United States and the Soviet Union, to achieve superior spaceflight capability. It had its origins in the ballistic missile-based nuclear arms race between the two nations following World War II and the onset of the Cold War.

10-26.title: Microsoft
10-26.subtitle: Microsoft made a public release of Windows 8 and made it available on new PCs.
10-26.text: Microsoft made a public release of Windows 8 and made it available on new PCs.

10-26.title: Austrian State Treaty
10-26.subtitle: After the last Allied troops have left the country, and following the provisions of the Austrian Independence Treaty, Au...
10-26.text: After the last Allied troops have left the country, and following the provisions of the Austrian Independence Treaty, Austria declares that it will never join a military alliance.

10-26.title: October 2015 Hindu Kush earthquake
10-26.subtitle: A 7.
10-26.text: A 7.5 magnitude earthquake strikes in the Hindu Kush mountain range in South Asia, killing 399 people and leaving 2,536 people injured.

10-25.title: 2010 Mentawai earthquake and tsunami
10-25.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
10-25.text: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake strikes off Indonesia's Mentawai Islands, triggering a tsunami that kills at least 400 people.

10-25.title: China
10-25.subtitle: The People's Republic of China replaces the Republic of China at the United Nations.
10-25.text: The People's Republic of China replaces the Republic of China at the United Nations.

10-25.title: Microsoft
10-25.subtitle: Microsoft releases Windows XP, which becomes one of Microsoft's most successful operating systems.
10-25.text: Microsoft releases Windows XP, which becomes one of Microsoft's most successful operating systems.

10-25.title: 1911 Revolution
10-25.subtitle: The Xinhai Revolution spreads to Guangzhou, where the Qing general Fengshan is assassinated by the Chinese Assassination Corps.
10-25.text: The Xinhai Revolution spreads to Guangzhou, where the Qing general Fengshan is assassinated by the Chinese Assassination Corps.

10-25.title: Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic
10-25.subtitle: The Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic declares its sovereignty from the Soviet Union.
10-25.text: The Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic declares its sovereignty from the Soviet Union.

10-29.title: Computer
10-29.subtitle: The first-ever computer-to-computer link is established on ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet.
10-29.text: The first-ever computer-to-computer link is established on ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet.

10-29.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
10-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year-old John Glenn on board, making him the oldest person to go into space at that time.
10-29.text: Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year-old John Glenn on board, making him the oldest person to go into space at that time.

10-29.title: Galileo
10-29.subtitle: The American Galileo spacecraft makes its closest approach to 951 Gaspra, becoming the first probe to visit an asteroid.
10-29.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
10-29.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

10-29.title: Boeing 737 MAX
10-29.subtitle: A Boeing 737 MAX plane crashes after taking off from Jakarta, Indonesia killing 189 people on board.
10-29.text: A Boeing 737 MAX plane crashes after taking off from Jakarta, Indonesia killing 189 people on board.  This is the first of two crashes that will lead to the plane being grounded worldwide.

10-29.title: ATSC standards
10-29.subtitle: ATSC HDTV broadcasting in the United States is inaugurated with live coverage of the launch of the STS-95 space shuttle mission.
10-29.text: Advanced Television Systems Committee (ATSC) standards are an international set of standards for broadcast and digital television transmission over terrestrial, cable and satellite networks. It is largely a replacement for the analog NTSC standard. Like NTSC, ATSC is used mostly in the United States, Mexico, Canada, South Korea, and Trinidad and Tobago.
10-29.location: Standards for digital television in the US

10-28.title: 2023 Rugby World Cup final
10-28.subtitle: The 2023 Rugby World Cup final is held at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis, France.
10-28.text: The 2023 Rugby World Cup final is held at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis, France. It saw South Africa defeat New Zealand 12 to 11, claiming their fourth Webb Ellis Cup, becoming the first nation to do so.

10-28.title: Jair Bolsonaro
10-28.subtitle: Jair Bolsonaro is elected president of Brazil with 57 million votes, with Workers' Party candidate Fernando Haddad as the runner-up.
10-28.text: Jair Bolsonaro is elected president of Brazil with 57 million votes, with Workers' Party candidate Fernando Haddad as the runner-up. It is the first time in 16 years that a Workers' Party candidate is not elected president.

10-28.title: NASA
10-28.subtitle: NASA successfully launches the Ares I-X mission, the only rocket launch for its short-lived Constellation program.
10-28.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

10-28.title: Cygnus Orb-3
10-28.subtitle: A rocket carrying NASA's Cygnus CRS Orb-3 resupply mission to the International Space Station explodes seconds after tak...
10-28.text: A rocket carrying NASA's Cygnus CRS Orb-3 resupply mission to the International Space Station explodes seconds after taking off from the Mid-Atlantic Regional Spaceport in Wallops Island, Virginia.

10-28.title: Prospero
10-28.subtitle: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.
10-28.text: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.

10-30.title: Hungarian Revolution
10-30.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution: The government of Imre Nagy recognizes newly established revolutionary workers' councils.
10-30.text: Hungarian Revolution: The government of Imre Nagy recognizes newly established revolutionary workers' councils. Army officer Béla Király leads anti-Soviet militias in an attack on the headquarters of the Hungarian Working People's Party.

10-30.title: European Union
10-30.subtitle: Sweden becomes the first European Union member state to officially recognize Palestine as an independent and sovereign state.
10-30.text: Sweden becomes the first European Union member state to officially recognize Palestine as an independent and sovereign state.

10-30.title: 2020 Aegean Sea earthquake
10-30.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
10-30.text: A magnitude 7.0 earthquake strikes the Aegean Sea between Greece and Turkey, triggering a tsunami. At least 119 people die mainly due to collapsed buildings.

10-30.title: Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450
10-30.subtitle: Forty-five people are killed when Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450 crashes into Suchdol, Prague, while on approach to Pr...
10-30.text: Forty-five people are killed when Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450 crashes into Suchdol, Prague, while on approach to Prague Ruzyně Airport (now Václav Havel Airport Prague) in Czechoslovakia (present-day Czech Republic).

10-30.title: Space Shuttle Challenger
10-30.subtitle: Space Shuttle Challenger lifts off for mission STS-61-A, its final successful mission.
10-30.text: Space Shuttle Challenger lifts off for mission STS-61-A, its final successful mission.

10-31.title: Soyuz TM-31
10-31.subtitle: Soyuz TM-31 launches, carrying the first resident crew to the International Space Station.
10-31.text: Soyuz TM-31 launches, carrying the first resident crew to the International Space Station. The ISS has been crewed continuously since then.

10-31.title: VSS Enterprise
10-31.subtitle: During a test flight, the VSS Enterprise, a Virgin Galactic experimental spaceflight test vehicle, suffers a catastrophi...
10-31.text: During a test flight, the VSS Enterprise, a Virgin Galactic experimental spaceflight test vehicle, suffers a catastrophic in-flight breakup and crashes in the Mojave Desert, California.

10-31.title: Hungarian Revolution of 1956
10-31.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution of 1956: A Revolutionary Headquarters is established in Hungary.
10-31.text: Hungarian Revolution of 1956: A Revolutionary Headquarters is established in Hungary. Following Imre Nagy's announcement of October 30, banned non-Communist political parties are reformed, and the MDP is replaced by the MSZMP.  József Mindszenty is released from prison. The Soviet Politburo makes the decision to crush the Revolution.

10-31.title: Houston
10-31.subtitle: A federal grand jury in Houston, Texas indicts former Enron chief financial officer Andrew Fastow on 78 counts of wire f...
10-31.text: A federal grand jury in Houston, Texas indicts former Enron chief financial officer Andrew Fastow on 78 counts of wire fraud, money laundering, conspiracy and obstruction of justice related to the collapse of his ex-employer.

10-31.title: Vietnam War October surprise
10-31.subtitle: Vietnam War October surprise: Citing progress with the Paris peace talks, US President Lyndon B.
10-31.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-31.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

11-05.title: Saddam Hussein
11-05.subtitle: Saddam Hussein, the former president of Iraq, and his co-defendants Barzan Ibrahim al-Tikriti and Awad Hamed al-Bandar, ...
11-05.text: Saddam Hussein, the former president of Iraq, and his co-defendants Barzan Ibrahim al-Tikriti and Awad Hamed al-Bandar, are sentenced to death in the al-Dujail trial for their roles in the 1982 massacre of 148 Shia Muslims.

11-05.title: USS Rentz
11-05.subtitle: USS Rentz, USS Reeves and USS Oldendorf visit Qingdao, China; the first US naval visit to China since 1949.
11-05.text: USS Rentz, USS Reeves and USS Oldendorf visit Qingdao, China; the first US naval visit to China since 1949.

11-05.title: Mars Orbiter Mission
11-05.subtitle: India launches the Mars Orbiter Mission, its first interplanetary probe.
11-05.text: India launches the Mars Orbiter Mission, its first interplanetary probe.

11-05.title: Donald Trump
11-05.subtitle: Donald Trump becomes the first president of the United States to be elected to a non-consecutive second term in 132 year...
11-05.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
11-05.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

11-05.title: Chang'e 1
11-05.subtitle: China's first lunar satellite, Chang'e 1, goes into orbit around the Moon.
11-05.text: China's first lunar satellite, Chang'e 1, goes into orbit around the Moon.

11-01.title: Kerala
11-01.subtitle: The Indian states Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Mysore are formally created under the States Reorganisation Act; Kanyakuma...
11-01.text: The Indian states Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Mysore are formally created under the States Reorganisation Act; Kanyakumari district is joined to Tamil Nadu from Kerala. Delhi was established as a union territory.

11-01.title: Nuclear weapons testing
11-01.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: The United States successfully detonates Ivy Mike, the first thermonuclear device, at the Eniwetok atoll.
11-01.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
11-01.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

11-01.title: Head of the Chechen Republic
11-01.subtitle: President of the Chechen Republic Dzhokhar Dudayev declares sovereignty of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria from the Russian Federation.
11-01.text: President of the Chechen Republic Dzhokhar Dudayev declares sovereignty of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria from the Russian Federation.

11-01.title: Alberto Natusch
11-01.subtitle: In Bolivia, Colonel Alberto Natusch executes a bloody coup d'état against the constitutional government of Wálter Guevara.
11-01.text: In Bolivia, Colonel Alberto Natusch executes a bloody coup d'état against the constitutional government of Wálter Guevara.

11-01.title: Arecibo Observatory
11-01.subtitle: The Arecibo Observatory in Arecibo, Puerto Rico, with the largest radio telescope ever constructed, officially opens.
11-01.text: The Arecibo Observatory in Arecibo, Puerto Rico, with the largest radio telescope ever constructed, officially opens.

11-02.title: Ethiopia–Tigray peace agreement
11-02.subtitle: A peace agreement is signed between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front, ending the Tigray War.
11-02.text: A peace agreement is signed between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front, ending the Tigray War.

11-02.title: Expedition 1
11-02.subtitle: Expedition 1 arrived at the International Space Station for the first long-duration stay onboard.
11-02.text: Expedition 1 arrived at the International Space Station for the first long-duration stay onboard. From this day to present, a continuous human presence in space on the station remains uninterrupted.

11-02.title: British Satellite Broadcasting
11-02.subtitle: British Satellite Broadcasting and Sky Television plc merge to form BSkyB as a result of massive losses.
11-02.text: British Satellite Broadcasting and Sky Television plc merge to form BSkyB as a result of massive losses.

11-02.title: Morris worm
11-02.subtitle: The Morris worm, the first Internet-distributed computer worm to gain significant mainstream media attention, is launched from MIT.
11-02.text: The Morris worm, the first Internet-distributed computer worm to gain significant mainstream media attention, is launched from MIT.

11-02.title: M1 motorway
11-02.subtitle: The first section of the M1 motorway, the first inter-urban motorway in the United Kingdom, is opened between the presen...
11-02.text: The first section of the M1 motorway, the first inter-urban motorway in the United Kingdom, is opened between the present junctions 5 and 18, along with the M10 motorway and M45 motorway.

11-03.title: Sputnik program
11-03.subtitle: Sputnik program: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 2.
11-03.text: Sputnik program: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 2. On board is the first animal to enter orbit, a dog named Laika.

11-03.title: Mariner program
11-03.subtitle: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 10 toward Mercury.
11-03.text: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 10 toward Mercury. On March 29, 1974, it becomes the first space probe to reach that planet.

11-03.title: Space Shuttle program
11-03.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Atlantis launches on STS-66.
11-03.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

11-03.title: 2020 United States presidential election
11-03.subtitle: The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump.
11-03.text: The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump. On November 7, Biden was declared the winner.

11-03.title: Compact of Free Association
11-03.subtitle: The Compact of Free Association becomes law, granting the Federated States of Micronesia and the Marshall Islands independence from the United States.
11-03.text: The Compacts of Free Association (COFA) are international agreements establishing and governing the relationships of free association between the United States and the three Pacific Island sovereign states of the Federated States of Micronesia (FSM), the Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI), and the Republic of Palau. As a result, these countries are sometimes known as the Freely Associated States (FASs). All three agreements next expire in 2043.

11-04.title: Barack Obama
11-04.subtitle: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.
11-04.text: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.

11-04.title: Soviet Union
11-04.subtitle: Soviet troops enter Hungary to end the Hungarian revolution against the Soviet Union that started on October 23.
11-04.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

11-04.title: Arno
11-04.subtitle: The Arno River floods Florence, Italy, to a maximum depth of 6.
11-04.text: The Arno River floods Florence, Italy, to a maximum depth of 6.7 m (22 ft), leaving thousands homeless and destroying millions of masterpieces of art and rare books. Venice is also submerged on the same day at its record all-time acqua alta of 194 cm (76 in).

11-04.title: Salvador Allende
11-04.subtitle: Salvador Allende takes office as President of Chile, the first Marxist to become president of a Latin American country through open elections.
11-04.text: Salvador Guillermo Allende Gossens was a Chilean socialist politician who served as the 29th president of Chile from 1970 until his death in 1973. As a socialist committed to democracy, he has been described as the first Marxist to be elected president in a liberal democracy in Latin America.

11-04.title: Operation Fishbowl
11-04.subtitle: The United States concludes Operation Fishbowl, its final above-ground nuclear weapons testing series, in anticipation o...
11-04.text: The United States concludes Operation Fishbowl, its final above-ground nuclear weapons testing series, in anticipation of the 1963 Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty.

11-07.title: Mary Robinson
11-07.subtitle: Mary Robinson becomes the first woman to be elected President of the Republic of Ireland.
11-07.text: Mary Robinson becomes the first woman to be elected President of the Republic of Ireland.

11-07.title: Cold War
11-07.subtitle: Cold War: The command post exercise Able Archer 83 begins, eventually leading to the Soviet Union to place air units in ...
11-07.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
11-07.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

11-07.title: WXYC
11-07.subtitle: WXYC, the student radio station of the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, launches the world's first internet radio broadcast.
11-07.text: WXYC, the student radio station of the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, launches the world's first internet radio broadcast.

11-07.title: Drug Enforcement Administration
11-07.subtitle: The U.
11-07.text: The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration discovers one of the country's largest LSD labs inside a converted military missile silo in Wamego, Kansas.

11-07.title: Bangladesh
11-07.subtitle: In Bangladesh, a joint force of people and soldiers takes part in an uprising led by Colonel Abu Taher that ousts and ki...
11-07.text: Bangladesh, officially the People's Republic of Bangladesh, is a country in South Asia. It is the eighth-most populous country in the world and among the most densely populated with a population of over 174 million within an area of 148,460 square kilometres (57,320 sq mi). Bangladesh shares land borders with India to the north, west, and east, and Myanmar to the southeast.

11-06.title: Sumburgh disaster
11-06.subtitle: Sumburgh disaster: A British International Helicopters Boeing 234LR Chinook crashes 2.
11-06.text: Sumburgh disaster: A British International Helicopters Boeing 234LR Chinook crashes 2.5 miles (4.0 km) east of Sumburgh Airport killing 45 people. It is the deadliest civilian helicopter crash on record.

11-06.title: United States Atomic Energy Commission
11-06.subtitle: The United States Atomic Energy Commission tests the largest U.
11-06.text: The United States Atomic Energy Commission tests the largest U.S. underground hydrogen bomb, code-named Cannikin, on Amchitka Island in the Aleutians.

11-06.title: Meet the Press
11-06.subtitle: Meet the Press, the longest running television program in history, makes its debut on NBC Television.
11-06.text: Meet the Press, the longest running television program in history, makes its debut on NBC Television.

11-06.title: Tammy Baldwin
11-06.subtitle: Tammy Baldwin becomes the first openly gay politician to be elected to the United States Senate.
11-06.text: Tammy Baldwin becomes the first openly gay politician to be elected to the United States Senate.

11-06.title: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ
11-06.subtitle: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ is appointed to head the South Vietnamese government by General Dương Văn Minh's junta, five days after ...
11-06.text: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ is appointed to head the South Vietnamese government by General Dương Văn Minh's junta, five days after the latter deposed and assassinated President Ngô Đình Diệm.

11-10.title: Southern Airways
11-10.subtitle: Southern Airways Flight 49 from Birmingham, Alabama is hijacked and, at one point, is threatened with crashing into the ...
11-10.text: Southern Airways Flight 49 from Birmingham, Alabama is hijacked and, at one point, is threatened with crashing into the nuclear installation at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory. After two days, the plane lands in Havana, Cuba, where the hijackers are jailed by Fidel Castro.

11-10.title: Mars
11-10.subtitle: Over five months after landing on Mars, NASA declares the Phoenix mission concluded after communications with the lander were lost.
11-10.text: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun. It is also known as the "Red Planet", for its orange-red appearance. Mars is a desert-like rocky planet with a tenuous atmosphere that is primarily carbon dioxide.
